./handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_EXTRA_ROUND4_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:2839:     4	//! TRACE_MATRIX WP § 0 axiom 1: state monotonicity.
./handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_EXTRA_ROUND4_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:2877:handover/specs/CO1_7_EXTRA_HEAD_T_WIRING_v1_2026-04-29.md:109:**Atomicity** (per Codex Q-B + round-2 MF9 wording correction): under acquired `q_w` + `writer_w` write locks, after `writer_w.commit(&entry)?` returns `Ok`, the remaining operations are an `AtomicU64::store` (infallible), a plain `*q_w = q_next` move (infallible), and `advance_head_t` (infallible). For writers whose `head_commit_oid_hex` returns `Some` (Git2LedgerWriter), this is a **post-commit non-failing best-effort head binding** — `q.head_t` advances atomically with `ledger_root_t` and `next_logical_t`. For writers returning `None` (InMemoryLedgerWriter), `advance_head_t` is **explicit no-op preservation** — `q.head_t` stays at its prior value (which equals `q_next.head_t` after the `*q_w = q_next` move because CO1.7 K3 v1.2 forbids transition bodies from mutating head_t).
./handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_EXTRA_ROUND4_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:2907:PASS. ./handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_EXTRA_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:130:## § 0 Scope decision (round-1 driven)
./handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_EXTRA_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:134:Round-1 dual external audit on the prior bundled CO1.7.5 v1 spec (`334111a`) returned CHALLENGE/CHALLENGE. The conservative-merged verdict (`CO1_7_5_DUAL_AUDIT_VERDICT_R1_2026-04-29.md`) found that the v1 bundling crossed Anti-Oreo three-layer boundaries: D1 transition bodies require FC1 top-white predicate execution methods + FC2 middle-black state schemas that don't exist in shipped code (CO P2.x family substrate not yet shipped per `PROJECT_DECISION_MAP § 3.4`).
./handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_EXTRA_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:157:3. **D4-substrate-independent** — One conformance test `tests/cas_payload_round_trip` (`CasStore::put` → `get` round-trip with CID stability post-CO1.4-extra). Other 3 D4 tests (replay state-root + system-signature canonical-message + un-ignore byte-identity) move to future CO1.7.5 atom because they require D1 transition bodies to actually commit.
./handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_EXTRA_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:159:### 0.4 Process commitment (active reconciliation per round-1 Gemini MF1+MF3 + Codex Q-A; round-2 MF1 corrected)
./handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_EXTRA_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:168:**Asserted authority principle** (strengthened per round-1 Gemini MF3): a later, more specific, audited spec (CO1.7 v1.2 round-3 PASS/PASS; CO1.1.4-pre1 PASS/PASS) **legitimately supersedes** earlier general specs (STATE v1.4 round-4 PASS/PASS) within the layered boundary the later spec covers. This is consistent with the project's atom-decomposition pattern: each atom locks its own surface; downstream atoms refine via PASS/PASS audit, not by editing upstream artifacts.
./handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_EXTRA_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:213:**Atomicity** (per Codex Q-B + round-2 MF9 wording correction): under acquired `q_w` + `writer_w` write locks, after `writer_w.commit(&entry)?` returns `Ok`, the remaining operations are an `AtomicU64::store` (infallible), a plain `*q_w = q_next` move (infallible), and `advance_head_t` (infallible). For writers whose `head_commit_oid_hex` returns `Some` (Git2LedgerWriter), this is a **post-commit non-failing best-effort head binding** — `q.head_t` advances atomically with `ledger_root_t` and `next_logical_t`. For writers returning `None` (InMemoryLedgerWriter), `advance_head_t` is **explicit no-op preservation** — `q.head_t` stays at its prior value (which equals `q_next.head_t` after the `*q_w = q_next` move because CO1.7 K3 v1.2 forbids transition bodies from mutating head_t).
./handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_EXTRA_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:249:    /// (no default to inherit) so the choice is explicit, not implicit.
./handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_EXTRA_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:257:This is a **breaking change** to any third-party `LedgerWriter` impl outside the workspace (would no longer compile). Inside the workspace, only Git2LedgerWriter and InMemoryLedgerWriter implement the trait; both get explicit declarations above. Forward-compat: any future LedgerWriter impl is forced to declare its OID semantics explicitly — a desirable property for a constitutional anchor field.
./handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_EXTRA_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:265:Round-2 Codex Q-7 + Gemini Q5 converged on placing Sequencer at TuringBus directly (not nested through Kernel). Rationale per round-2 MF4:

./handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_EXTRA_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:324:CO1.7-extra now touches a single STEP_B-restricted file: `src/bus.rs`. No combined-ceremony justification needed. Per `STEP_B_PROTOCOL.md` Phase 0, the change is "minimum sufficient version" and has no less-invasive alternative (the typed-tx submission path needs SOME entry-point in the runtime layer; TuringBus is the canonical orchestrator).

./handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_EXTRA_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:670:Both `Git2LedgerWriter` and `InMemoryLedgerWriter` (and any future impl) must explicitly declare. Compiler-enforced. Both audits' safety arguments (silent stagnation prevention + post-commit no-panic) satisfied.
./handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_EXTRA_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:674:Codex Q-7: STEP_B Phase 0 asks for less-invasive alternative; TuringBus already owns runtime orchestration and avoids Kernel serde/debug/topology debt. Codex Q-3 (M3c): saying Kernel "holds the driver, not the data" papers over Sequencer's heavy ownership of CAS + keypair + registries + writer + QState. Kernel `src/kernel.rs:5-6` explicit warning against domain-specific terms.

Conservative merged CHALLENGE. v1.2 closes the 3 v1.1→v1.2 patches: (a) C3 actually wired in code (`CanonicalMessage::LedgerEntrySigning([u8;32])` + `transition_ledger_emitter::sign_ledger_entry`); (b) K3 head_t mutation explicitly deferred to CO1.7.5+ (no longer claimed in v1.x); (c) `ObjectType::Transition` replaced with shipped `ObjectType::ProposalPayload`. Plus typo fix and 1 new test. Awaiting round-3.
./handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_EXTRA_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:758:| **R2-C3** | Spec claimed "C3 CLOSED" but `system_keypair.rs` had no LedgerEntry path; skeleton itself said "deferred to CO1.7.5+" | Wave 4-B additive extension shipped: `CanonicalMessage::LedgerEntrySigning([u8;32])` (opaque digest variant; avoids transition_ledger ↔ system_keypair circular dep) + `canonical_digest` match arm + new `pub(crate) mod transition_ledger_emitter` with `sign_ledger_entry(keypair, digest)`. Skeleton test 9 (`signature_round_trip_and_transplant_defense`) now exercises the real roundtrip + K2 + D1 defenses. | Codex round-2 must-fix #1 |
./handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_EXTRA_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:759:| **R2-K3** | Spec § 3 / § 5 said "CO1.7 owns head_t = NodeId(commit_sha)" but `LedgerWriter::commit` returns `Hash` not commit SHA; v1.1 InMemoryLedgerWriter has no commit_sha to return at all → contradiction | head_t mutation explicitly **deferred to CO1.7.5+** (when Git2LedgerWriter exists and can return both Hash + commit SHA). v1.x ledger owns `ledger_root_t` only; `head_t` continues to be set elsewhere (currently QState placeholder; CO1.7.5 wiring concern). Spec § 0 / § 3 / § 5 updated. | Codex round-2 must-fix #2 |
./handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_EXTRA_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:772:| C2 | spec did not acknowledge that shipped `CasStore::open()` initializes empty in-memory index → cold-replay impossible | § 0 + § 5 explicit dependency + mitigation: CasStore index persistence is deferred to **CO1.4-extra** (separate atom); v1 documents the gap | Codex Q-H + Gemini Q2 |
./handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_EXTRA_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:773:| C3 | signing primitive integration via `CanonicalMessage` enum was unspecified; spec called nonexistent digest-form verifier | Path A: extend `CanonicalMessage::LedgerEntrySigning(LedgerEntrySigningPayload)`; sign separate signing payload (NOT raw `LedgerEntry`); new API `keypair.sign_ledger_entry(payload) → SystemSignature` | Codex Q-G + Gemini Q4 |
./handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_EXTRA_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:779:| K6 | `tx_kind as u8` cast without `#[repr(u8)]` → fragile discriminant | `#[repr(u8)]` + explicit discriminants (`Work = 0, Verify = 1, ...`) | Codex Q-H |
./handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_EXTRA_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:780:| K7 | spec promised 8 conformance tests; skeleton has 6 | Explicit list of 8 tests with skeleton-stage vs CO1.7.5-stage marker; unimplemented stubs now stage-marked | Codex Q-H |
./handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_EXTRA_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:794:- **Sequencer**: per-(runtime_repo, run_id) single-writer instance enforcing § 5.2.1 (dual-counter `submit_id`/`logical_t`, submission-order serialization, post-commit `logical_t` assignment).
./handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_EXTRA_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:798:- **CanonicalMessage extension**: extends shipped `CanonicalMessage` enum with `LedgerEntrySigning([u8; 32])` opaque-digest variant (R2 design — avoids circular module dep); new typed sign API `transition_ledger_emitter::sign_ledger_entry(keypair, signing_payload_digest)`.
./handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_EXTRA_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:811:### What this spec is NOT replacing

./handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_EXTRA_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:941:CO1.7 extends shipped `system_keypair::CanonicalMessage` with one new variant. **R2-C3 design choice**: variant carries the **opaque 32-byte canonical_digest** of `LedgerEntrySigningPayload`, NOT the full payload struct. This avoids a circular `system_keypair ↔ transition_ledger` module dependency (the payload struct needs `Cid` from CAS module + `SystemEpoch` from system_keypair; carrying the precomputed digest sidesteps the cycle entirely). The signature still binds the full payload because `canonical_digest()` is deterministic in `transition_ledger`.

./handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_EXTRA_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:1181:- `CasMissing { at, cid }` (NEW; FullTransition only — fires if CO1.4-extra not yet landed)

./handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_EXTRA_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:5702:There is no `AGENTS.md` under this repository or its parent `projects` directory, so there are no local repo-specific instructions governing this read-only audit. I’m now extracting the exact spec and source line ranges that the round-2 fixes touched.
./handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_EXTRA_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:5726:    18	## § 0 Scope decision (round-1 driven)
./handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_EXTRA_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:5730:    22	Round-1 dual external audit on the prior bundled CO1.7.5 v1 spec (`334111a`) returned CHALLENGE/CHALLENGE. The conservative-merged verdict (`CO1_7_5_DUAL_AUDIT_VERDICT_R1_2026-04-29.md`) found that the v1 bundling crossed Anti-Oreo three-layer boundaries: D1 transition bodies require FC1 top-white predicate execution methods + FC2 middle-black state schemas that don't exist in shipped code (CO P2.x family substrate not yet shipped per `PROJECT_DECISION_MAP § 3.4`).
./handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_EXTRA_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:5753:    45	3. **D4-substrate-independent** — One conformance test `tests/cas_payload_round_trip` (`CasStore::put` → `get` round-trip with CID stability post-CO1.4-extra). Other 3 D4 tests (replay state-root + system-signature canonical-message + un-ignore byte-identity) move to future CO1.7.5 atom because they require D1 transition bodies to actually commit.
./handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_EXTRA_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:5755:    47	### 0.4 Process commitment (active reconciliation per round-1 Gemini MF1+MF3 + Codex Q-A; round-2 MF1 corrected)
./handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_EXTRA_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:5764:    56	**Asserted authority principle** (strengthened per round-1 Gemini MF3): a later, more specific, audited spec (CO1.7 v1.2 round-3 PASS/PASS; CO1.1.4-pre1 PASS/PASS) **legitimately supersedes** earlier general specs (STATE v1.4 round-4 PASS/PASS) within the layered boundary the later spec covers. This is consistent with the project's atom-decomposition pattern: each atom locks its own surface; downstream atoms refine via PASS/PASS audit, not by editing upstream artifacts.
./handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_EXTRA_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:5809:   101	**Atomicity** (per Codex Q-B + round-2 MF9 wording correction): under acquired `q_w` + `writer_w` write locks, after `writer_w.commit(&entry)?` returns `Ok`, the remaining operations are an `AtomicU64::store` (infallible), a plain `*q_w = q_next` move (infallible), and `advance_head_t` (infallible). For writers whose `head_commit_oid_hex` returns `Some` (Git2LedgerWriter), this is a **post-commit non-failing best-effort head binding** — `q.head_t` advances atomically with `ledger_root_t` and `next_logical_t`. For writers returning `None` (InMemoryLedgerWriter), `advance_head_t` is **explicit no-op preservation** — `q.head_t` stays at its prior value (which equals `q_next.head_t` after the `*q_w = q_next` move because CO1.7 K3 v1.2 forbids transition bodies from mutating head_t).
./handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_EXTRA_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:5845:   137	    /// (no default to inherit) so the choice is explicit, not implicit.
./handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_EXTRA_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:5853:   145	This is a **breaking change** to any third-party `LedgerWriter` impl outside the workspace (would no longer compile). Inside the workspace, only Git2LedgerWriter and InMemoryLedgerWriter implement the trait; both get explicit declarations above. Forward-compat: any future LedgerWriter impl is forced to declare its OID semantics explicitly — a desirable property for a constitutional anchor field.

./handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_EXTRA_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:7208:A stronger issue emerged: § 1.1 makes `advance_head_t` `pub(crate)`, but § 3.3 puts the test in `tests/` and calls it through the crate public path. Rust integration tests cannot access `pub(crate)` items, so that test as specified will not compile. I’m checking whether the module is publicly exported, because if it is, the fix is likely just changing the helper visibility to `pub`.

./handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_EXTRA_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:7357:handover/specs/STATE_TRANSITION_SPEC_v1_2026-04-27.md:324:**Out of scope for v1.x spec** (deferred per Codex Q5/NEW-5 round-3 PARTIAL acknowledgment): full golden fixture corpus + differential fuzzing seed + complete runner ABI for QState/SignalBundle/TransitionError. v1.4 freezes the SERIALIZATION RULE (bincode v2 big-endian + BTreeMap lex); fixtures + ABI land in **CO1.1.4-pre1** (canonical fixture corpus) + **CO1.7** (full ABI surface). This is an **explicit deferral** — not unresolved spec ambiguity. STEP_B branch A and branch B both implement the SAME bincode rule; per-tx digest matching is mechanical from v1.4. Full corpus generation is a downstream code task, not spec scope.
./handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_EXTRA_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:7387:handover/specs/CO1_1_4_PRE1_TYPED_TX_ABI_v1_2026-04-28.md:47:**Why this atom exists**: spec § 2.5 of `STATE_TRANSITION_SPEC_v1_2026-04-27.md` explicitly deferred "full ABI surface for QState/SignalBundle/TransitionError" to CO1.7. CO1.7 spec § 0 places the per-kind tx schemas in `STATE_TRANSITION_SPEC § 1` ("frozen on paper, not yet in code"). When CO1.7-impl A1 (Git2LedgerWriter, commit `a03cc52`) shipped, downstream A2 (TypedTx + dispatch_transition) discovered ~30 supporting schema types are required but **none of them exist in code** — only `MicroCoin` is defined. This atom defines that ABI surface in isolation under its own dual-audit gate, per the project's per-atom audit principle (CLAUDE.md "Audit Standard").
./handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_EXTRA_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:7388:handover/specs/CO1_1_4_PRE1_TYPED_TX_ABI_v1_2026-04-28.md:77:- **`SignalBundle` typed shape** — STATE spec uses `SignalBundle::empty()` / `::finalize(...)` / `::task_expired(...)` / `::terminal_summary(...)` constructors. v1 of this atom emits a minimal typed `SignalBundle` (single enum-like discriminator + payload) sufficient for CO1.7-impl to compile; full event-stream design lands in CO1.9 L6 signal indices.
./handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_EXTRA_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:7450:    71	### Out of scope (explicit deferral)

./handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_EXTRA_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:7456:    77	- **`SignalBundle` typed shape** — STATE spec uses `SignalBundle::empty()` / `::finalize(...)` / `::task_expired(...)` / `::terminal_summary(...)` constructors. v1 of this atom emits a minimal typed `SignalBundle` (single enum-like discriminator + payload) sufficient for CO1.7-impl to compile; full event-stream design lands in CO1.9 L6 signal indices.
./handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_EXTRA_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:7459:    80	### What this atom is NOT replacing
./handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_EXTRA_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:7466:    87	**Constitutional concern**: CO1.7 LedgerEntry stores typed-tx payloads in L3 CAS via `tx_payload_cid: Cid`. The current shipped `CasStore::open()` initializes an empty in-memory index (CO1.4 store.rs:67); after process restart the CAS bytes are unrecoverable until the index is repopulated. This means **cold-replay of L4 cannot reconstruct typed payloads** — a direct Art. 0.2 (tape canonicality) violation if uncorrected.
./handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_EXTRA_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:7500:47:**Why this atom exists**: spec § 2.5 of `STATE_TRANSITION_SPEC_v1_2026-04-27.md` explicitly deferred "full ABI surface for QState/SignalBundle/TransitionError" to CO1.7. CO1.7 spec § 0 places the per-kind tx schemas in `STATE_TRANSITION_SPEC § 1` ("frozen on paper, not yet in code"). When CO1.7-impl A1 (Git2LedgerWriter, commit `a03cc52`) shipped, downstream A2 (TypedTx + dispatch_transition) discovered ~30 supporting schema types are required but **none of them exist in code** — only `MicroCoin` is defined. This atom defines that ABI surface in isolation under its own dual-audit gate, per the project's per-atom audit principle (CLAUDE.md "Audit Standard").
./handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_EXTRA_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:7501:77:- **`SignalBundle` typed shape** — STATE spec uses `SignalBundle::empty()` / `::finalize(...)` / `::task_expired(...)` / `::terminal_summary(...)` constructors. v1 of this atom emits a minimal typed `SignalBundle` (single enum-like discriminator + payload) sufficient for CO1.7-impl to compile; full event-stream design lands in CO1.9 L6 signal indices.
./handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_EXTRA_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:7508:   261	- **I-CANON-G** (v1.1 NEW per C-1): each agent-signed and system-emitted typed-tx has a paired `*SigningPayload` struct + `canonical_digest()` with explicit domain prefix `b"turingosv4.<actor>.<purpose>.v1"`. Domain prefix bytes are part of the SHA-256 input. 6 distinct domains (work / verify / challenge agent + finalize_reward / task_expire / terminal_summary system) yield pairwise-distinct digests.
./handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_EXTRA_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:7517:   270	| `Strings serialized as UTF-8 with explicit length prefix u32-BE` | **u64 BE** length prefix (bincode encodes `usize` as u64 under `with_fixed_int_encoding`; [src/enc/impls.rs:128](https://docs.rs/bincode/2.0.1/src/bincode/enc/impls.rs.html)). The same applies to BTreeMap / BTreeSet / Vec lengths. |
./handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_EXTRA_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:7520:   273	**v1.1 decision**: keep u32 variants + u64 lengths; do NOT introduce a custom serde adapter to force u8 discriminants (which would force re-encoding of all existing fixtures + complicate forward-compat for >256 variants). The locked golden fixtures in `EXPECTED_HEX_*` reflect the actual u32/u64 codec.
./handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_EXTRA_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:7536:   289	If a future audit finds a security defect in a domain prefix (`b"turingosv4.<actor>.<purpose>.v1"`), rotation follows this discipline:
./handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_EXTRA_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:7582:PASS. The § 0.4 table is now factually correct: head_t supersession is enacted in CO1.7-extra D2, while SignalKind migrates to future CO1.7.5 ([spec:51-54](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/handover/specs/CO1_7_EXTRA_HEAD_T_WIRING_v1_2026-04-29.md:51)). The authority principle also holds ([spec:56](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/handover/specs/CO1_7_EXTRA_HEAD_T_WIRING_v1_2026-04-29.md:56)).
./handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_EXTRA_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:7590:The helper shape is logically correct, but not testable as specified. § 1.1 declares it `pub(crate)` ([spec:78](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/handover/specs/CO1_7_EXTRA_HEAD_T_WIRING_v1_2026-04-29.md:78)), while § 3.3 is a flat integration test calling `turingosv4::state::sequencer::advance_head_t` ([spec:295](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/handover/specs/CO1_7_EXTRA_HEAD_T_WIRING_v1_2026-04-29.md:295), [spec:337](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/handover/specs/CO1_7_EXTRA_HEAD_T_WIRING_v1_2026-04-29.md:337)). Clean single semantics: substitution is permitted throughout Phase B → Phase E; calibration UPGRADES the bar IF and WHEN the § 3 conditions all complete. Re-hashed in Trust Root.
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R8.md:459:PREREG § 5.5 specifies p_0 calibration via 576 paired runs (144 adaptation × 2 seeds × {control, treatment}) with estimated cost "~8 wall-hours, ~$3-5". Empirical observation 2026-04-25 during launched batch (commit 650caf7+ era):
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R8.md:464:| Total batch wall-clock | ~8 hours | Realistic 3-7 days (576 runs × 15 min serial; treatment short-circuits halve some) | 9-21× |
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R8.md:467:**The 8-hour estimate was based on "~50s/run chat oneshot" assumption that turned out wrong for swarm n3 condition on the adaptation-144 problem mix.** A 7-day batch is not "overnight"; user (mid-session 2026-04-25) explicitly questioned 576-run necessity given multiple unresolved engineering questions (N-agents → PPUT relationship, swarm_N=1 vs oneshot calibration, ground-truth feedback pipeline, etc.).
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R8.md:475:**Wording correction (A8 round-1 audit, Codex finding 1)**: an earlier draft of this section claimed `p_0 = 0.10` was the *strictest possible substitute*. That was backwards. The Gate H test is `j-RR ≤ p_0`, so a SMALLER `p_0` is stricter. `0.10` is the **maximum tolerated value** the PREREG admits — i.e., the LEAST strict admissible ceiling, not the strictest. Substituting it is the equivalent of running Gate H at the weakest threshold the PREREG ever sanctioned. The substitution is *operationally permitted* (the PREREG explicitly allows up to 0.10), but it is NOT a tighter-than-original guarantee.
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R8.md:479:- The substitution may be LESS protective than an eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`. If/when calibration runs (per § 3 conditions, which are PRE-REQUISITES — they do not by themselves *guarantee* calibration completes before any specific phase), the empirical estimate may tighten the bar; until then Gate H operates at the ceiling.
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R8.md:494:3. **Per-agent budget normalization landed** (Phase A atom A5) — current `max_transactions=200` is fixed-tx budget; PREREG § 5.5 implicitly assumes tx-budget but doesn't specify; need explicit budget regime declaration for calibration to be reproducible.

Amendment defers the SCHEDULING, not the SCIENCE.

./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R8.md:5396:- A0a: 4 new rules (R-014/R-015/R-018/R-019) + judge.sh constitution-special-case + R-016 fc_trace_in_commit hook (commit 2e7f75a)
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R8.md:5398:- A0c: 5 new cases C-071..C-075 sediment session decisions (commit 2a65339)
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R8.md:5400:- A4 (post-A3): decomposed metrics — `hit_max_tx`, `tactic_diversity`, `verifier_wait_ms` added as non-Optional v2 fields + `compute_tactic_diversity` helper; per-row decomposition of `solve_rate` / `tokens_per_solve` / `time_per_solve` (all derivable from existing `progress` / `total_run_token_count` / `total_wall_time_ms`). FC-trace: FC2-N22 (HALT decomposition for `hit_max_tx`) + FC1-N11 (∏p decision diversity for `tactic_diversity`) + FC1-N12 (oracle scope for `verifier_wait_ms`).
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R8.md:5402:- A6 (post-A5): per-line FC tagging via structured JSON events — new `fc_trace` module (pure stdlib; zero new deps); `FcId` enum (FC1-N7 / FC1-N11 / FC1-N12 / FC1-E18 / FC2-N20 / FC2-N22 / FC3-N31); `fc_event!`-style `emit_event` API; `FC_TRACE=1` gate (cached in `OnceLock`); `FC_TRACE_FILE=<path>` redirects emit to file (default sink stderr). 9 anchor sites wired (post-A8e total — 8 in `run_swarm` + 1 in `run_oneshot`): the original A6 commit shipped 6 sites (FC2-N22 synthetic short-circuit, FC2-N20 mr tick, FC2-N22 OMEGA full-proof, FC2-N22 OMEGA per-tactic, FC2-N22 natural MaxTxExhausted with `budget_regime` payload, FC1-N12 verify bracket in oneshot). A8e fix F4 added 3 more in `run_swarm` (FC1-N12 verify_omega_detailed × 2 paths + FC1-N12 verify_partial), bringing the post-A8e total to 9 production emit sites. Counted by grepping `fc_trace::emit_event(` in `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/bin/evaluator.rs` (Gemini R6 finding: round-5 phrasing "Six anchor sites" was stale relative to its own following sentence — corrected here in A8e6 K4). End-to-end smoke test exercises FC_TRACE=1 in a child process (subprocess required because `OnceLock` caches the gate-read; resolves item 7 of TRACE_MATRIX § 5 "Per-line FC tagging via tracing crate"). FC-trace: meta-witness for the 5-step compile loop (Proposal → Lean ground truth → Logging → Capability compilation → ↑H-VPPUT). Trust Root manifest 27 → 28 (corrected A8e5 J4; matches the canonical chain in `genesis_payload.toml` header).
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R8.md:5404:- A8e8 (post-A8e7, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-7 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini CHALLENGE — both convergent on rewrite-implementation gaps): five fixes including one substantive PREREG logic correction. **M1** packet § 2 + § 4 Trust Root count bumped 34 → 35 (post-A8e7 added `A8_AUDIT_HISTORY` to manifest). **M2** this history doc's A8e7 entry stamped with actual SHA `aaedc9d` + round-7 verdicts + A8e8 fix list (round-7 sealed). **M3** packet rewritten to strip ALL historical lineage text — no "(post-A8e F4)", "(added by A8eN)", "(chain position N via A8e)", "(+ A8eN fix XN)". Packet describes WHAT IS, no HOW; lineage lives only in this history doc. **M4 (substantive Codex R7#4)** PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2(b) corrected: removed false claim that "§ 3 conditions ensure calibration runs *before* Phase E"; § 3 conditions are PRE-REQUISITES for calibration TO RUN at all, not guarantees of completion before any specific phase. § 8 reads as the operative rule: substitution permitted at any phase including Phase E; calibration UPGRADES the bar IF and WHEN § 3 conditions complete. Single semantics, internally consistent across § 2 / § 3 / § 8. Re-hashed in Trust Root. **M5** audit runner scripts now append `experiments/minif2f_v4/tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs` so Q1.c (verify wrapper fail-closed) is verifiable from the packet bundle alone. Trust Root manifest unchanged at 35 (only re-hashes; no new files).
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R8.md:5406:- A8e6 (post-A8e5, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-6 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini CHALLENGE — both narrow): one real correctness fix + 4 doc fixes. **K1** packet `<pending>` placeholder cleanup (J2 + RQ14 wording) — same precedent as A8e5 J2 but applied to round-6 self-references. **K2 (real bug, Codex R6#2)** `src/drivers/llm_proxy.py` `detect_provider`: HuggingFace-style `deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1-Distill-Qwen-7B` was misrouting to `api.deepseek.com` because `"deepseek" in m` won before the slash check; pre-K2 logic would 404 on every Distill variant since the official DeepSeek API doesn't serve them. K2 swaps the order — slash-form is now the FIRST routing heuristic (after explicit prefix), and bare-deepseek substring only fires when there's no slash. New `test_deepseek_slash_form_routes_to_siliconflow` in `scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` pins this (covers `deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1-Distill-Qwen-7B` + `deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-V2.5` AS WELL AS the bare-form sanity case `deepseek-chat` → deepseek). **K3** TRACE_MATRIX A8e3 row stale "21 positional args" → 24 (matches packet update from A8e5 J5; Codex R6#3 caught the inconsistency between TRACE_MATRIX and the packet). **K4** TRACE_MATRIX A6 row top-line "Six anchor sites" → "9 anchor sites" with A6-original-6 + F4-added-3 breakdown (Gemini R6#1 caught the contradiction between the line's first sentence and its own follow-up). **K5** packet § 3 A5/A6 fix attribution: "A8e3 fix H4 corrected" → "A8e4 fix I2 corrected" (Gemini R6#2 caught the cross-reference error — H4 corrected the genesis_payload.toml TR-header comment, I2 corrected the packet § 3 numbers). Trust Root manifest unchanged at 34 (re-hashes of `llm_proxy.py` + `scripts/test_llm_proxy.py`; no new files).
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R8.md:5409:- A8e3 (post-A8e2, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-3 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini CHALLENGE — both convergent on packet staleness; no VETO): six narrow cleanup fixes. **H1** A8 packet § 3 A1 description: "Mathematically conservative" → "least-strict admissible value" with explicit Type-I implications. **H2** packet § 3 A6 description: 6 → 9 anchor sites (added F4 swarm sites explicitly). **H3** packet § 6 Q2.a / Q4.a / Q4.d marked CLOSED with closure rationale. **H4** `genesis_payload.toml` TR header phrasing about A1: "conservative ceiling" → "max-tolerated ceiling". **H5** § 5 item 7 here in TRACE_MATRIX: "(commit pending) + 6 wired" → "CLOSED + 9 wired". **H6** G1 wrapper test fail-closed on missing `python3` (was soft skip; Codex R3 caught the silent-pass risk); explicit opt-out `SKIP_LLM_PROXY_PYTHON_CONFORMANCE=1` for deliberate downgrades (logged loudly). Trust Root manifest unchanged at 34 (only re-hashes; no new files). Non-blocking observation deferred to Phase B+: `make_pput` signature was 21 positional args at round-3; A8e fix F1 added the `run_id` parameter, bringing the post-A8e count to **24** (Gemini R3 Finding 2 + Gemini R5/Codex R6 spot-checks; A8e6 K3 corrected here) — refactor to a builder pattern is recommended but not gating.
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R8.md:5410:- A8e2 (post-A8e, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-2 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini CHALLENGE — both convergent): three dual-audit fixes. **G1** (Codex R2#1 + Gemini R2#1) `experiments/minif2f_v4/tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs` wraps `python3 scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` in a Rust integration test, making the V3L-27 round-robin conformance gate run on every `cargo test --workspace`. **G2** (Codex R2#2) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 8` audit-requirements paragraph reworded — round-1 fix corrected § 2 but left § 8 still calling the substitution a "strictest plausible bar"; now consistent across the doc. Re-hashed in Trust Root. **G3** (Codex R2#3 + Gemini R2#2) `A8_EXIT_PACKET` updated for round-2 reality: § 2 cumulative-tests table extended with A8e/A8e2 columns, § 4 manifest-clean assertion bumped 30→34, § 5 round-1 risk #5 (no FC1-N12 in swarm verify path) removed (closed by F4), § 6 Q7.a/b numbers updated, round-2 outcome + round-3 fixes section appended; TRACE_MATRIX stale `run_corr_id` symbol row replaced; "in CI" softened to "via Rust wrapper post-A8e2". FC-trace: meta governance + V3L-27 mitigation gating (FC1-N7). Trust Root manifest 33 → 34 (`tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs`).
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R8.md:5411:- A8e (post-A7, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-1 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini VETO): six dual-audit fixes. **F1** (Codex#2 + Gemini Q4) `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/run_id.rs` mints one stable run_id per run, threaded into both `emit_event` and `make_pput` (replaces `run_corr_id` vs internal `run_id` ms drift; oneshot also stops using `oneshot_{problem_file}` placeholder). **F2** (Gemini VETO Q6) `scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` adds 15-test conformance battery — round-robin distributes [2,2,2] across 6 calls, single-key pool degrades to all-zero, two-key pool alternates [2,2]; exercises the V3L-27 mitigation without a live API. (A8e2 G1 wires this into `cargo test --workspace` via a Rust shell-out wrapper — see § 2 below.) **F3** (Codex#4) routing matrix bug fixed — `Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct` was misrouting to DashScope because `m.startswith("qwen")` won after the slash check; now slash-form ⇒ siliconflow first, bare-qwen ⇒ dashscope second. **F4** (Codex#3) FC1-N12 emit added to all 3 swarm verify sites (`verify_omega_detailed` × 2 + `verify_partial`), each with verdict + elapsed_ms + path stamp; closes the swarm-side gap in the 5-step compile-loop witness. **F5** (Codex#5) `genesis_payload.toml` header + TRACE_MATRIX milestones reconciled to actual 31 entries (round-1 packet undercounted by 1 — agent_models.rs from A3 was already in TR before A1). **F6** (Codex#1) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2` wording corrected — `p_0=0.10` substitution is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling (max tolerated value for `j-RR ≤ p_0`), NOT the strictest possible substitute as round-1 mistakenly claimed; explicit statistical-implications paragraph added (no Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive; substitution may be less protective than eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`). FC-trace: FC1-N12 (oracle scope swarm coverage) + FC1-N7 (δ provider routing) + Art. V (amendment governance). Trust Root manifest 31 → 33 (run_id.rs + test_llm_proxy.py).
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R8.md:5419:A0b added the missing `tests/fc_alignment_conformance.rs` (was only in `.claude/worktrees/phase-8a-snapshot/`). 17 ✅ rows in TRACE_MATRIX now have automated witness tests. Symbol drift is now caught at `cargo test` time, not at next dual audit.
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R8.md:5450:| `judge.sh` constitution.md special case | `.claude/hooks/judge.sh:50-67` | FC3-N3 sudo-gate enforcement (closes silent-bypass via `*.md` skip-list) | ✅ |
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R8.md:5454:| `RunAggregate::{hit_max_tx, tactic_diversity, verifier_wait_ms}` + `compute_tactic_diversity` (A4) | `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/jsonl_schema.rs` | FC2-N22 HALT decomposition (hit_max_tx splits natural max-tx exhaustion from OMEGA accept and from B7-extra synthetic short-circuit); FC1-N11 ∏p decision diversity (tactic_diversity = distinct/total over append+complete+step proposals); FC1-N12 oracle scope (verifier_wait_ms = cumulative Lean wall-clock per run, ≤ total_wall_time_ms by construction) | ✅ |
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R8.md:5455:| `make_pput` A4 args + per-call-site verifier brackets + per-tool proposal hashing (A4) | `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/bin/evaluator.rs` | wires the 3 fields at every emit site (oneshot + swarm OMEGA + swarm step Complete + swarm synthetic short-circuit + swarm natural max-tx exhaustion); 5 unit/conformance tests (`test_a4_decomposed_metrics_round_trip`, `test_a4_tactic_diversity_helper`, `test_a4_verifier_wait_bounded_by_total_wall_time`, `test_a4_emit_max_tx_exhaustion_row`, `test_a4_synthetic_short_circuit_does_not_set_hit_max_tx`) | ✅ |
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R8.md:5459:| `run_id::mint_run_id` correlation key + 9 wired FC events (A6 + A8e F4) | `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/run_id.rs` + `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/bin/evaluator.rs` | A6 introduced a per-run correlation id (originally named `run_corr_id`, now unified into `run_id`) anchoring all events. A8e fix F1 (Codex#2 + Gemini Q4) split this out into a dedicated module + threaded the same id into `make_pput` so FC events and v2 jsonl rows share one identifier (no ms drift). Anchor sites: FC2-N22 synthetic short-circuit / mr tick FC2-N20 / OMEGA full-proof FC2-N22 / OMEGA per-tactic FC2-N22 / natural MaxTxExhausted FC2-N22 (with `budget_regime` payload from A5) / FC1-N12 oneshot verify (A6) / FC1-N12 swarm `verify_omega_detailed` × 2 paths (A8e F4) / FC1-N12 swarm `verify_partial` (A8e F4). End-to-end smoke `tests/fc_trace_smoke.rs` exercises FC_TRACE=1 in a child process (forced because `OnceLock` caches the gate-read). | ✅ |
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R8.md:5460:| `tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs` Rust→Python wrapper (A8e2 fix G1) | `experiments/minif2f_v4/tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs` | A8 round-2 audit (Codex R2#1 + Gemini R2#1): the round-robin conformance battery in `scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` was only manual; tests that don't run automatically are documentation, not gates. G1 wires the Python suite into `cargo test --workspace` via a Rust integration test that shells to `python3` and asserts exit 0 + the unittest "OK" trailer. The V3L-27 mitigation (case C-027) is now a recurring conformance gate exercised on every commit that touches Rust + every CI pipeline that already exercises Rust tests. | ✅ |

./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R8.md:5714:handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:11:- A6 (post-A5): per-line FC tagging via structured JSON events — new `fc_trace` module (pure stdlib; zero new deps); `FcId` enum (FC1-N7 / FC1-N11 / FC1-N12 / FC1-E18 / FC2-N20 / FC2-N22 / FC3-N31); `fc_event!`-style `emit_event` API; `FC_TRACE=1` gate (cached in `OnceLock`); `FC_TRACE_FILE=<path>` redirects emit to file (default sink stderr). 9 anchor sites wired (post-A8e total — 8 in `run_swarm` + 1 in `run_oneshot`): the original A6 commit shipped 6 sites (FC2-N22 synthetic short-circuit, FC2-N20 mr tick, FC2-N22 OMEGA full-proof, FC2-N22 OMEGA per-tactic, FC2-N22 natural MaxTxExhausted with `budget_regime` payload, FC1-N12 verify bracket in oneshot). A8e fix F4 added 3 more in `run_swarm` (FC1-N12 verify_omega_detailed × 2 paths + FC1-N12 verify_partial), bringing the post-A8e total to 9 production emit sites. Counted by grepping `fc_trace::emit_event(` in `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/bin/evaluator.rs` (Gemini R6 finding: round-5 phrasing "Six anchor sites" was stale relative to its own following sentence — corrected here in A8e6 K4). End-to-end smoke test exercises FC_TRACE=1 in a child process (subprocess required because `OnceLock` caches the gate-read; resolves item 7 of TRACE_MATRIX § 5 "Per-line FC tagging via tracing crate"). FC-trace: meta-witness for the 5-step compile loop (Proposal → Lean ground truth → Logging → Capability compilation → ↑H-VPPUT). Trust Root manifest 27 → 28 (corrected A8e5 J4; matches the canonical chain in `genesis_payload.toml` header).
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R8.md:5716:handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:13:- A8e8 (post-A8e7, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-7 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini CHALLENGE — both convergent on rewrite-implementation gaps): five fixes including one substantive PREREG logic correction. **M1** packet § 2 + § 4 Trust Root count bumped 34 → 35 (post-A8e7 added `A8_AUDIT_HISTORY` to manifest). **M2** this history doc's A8e7 entry stamped with actual SHA `aaedc9d` + round-7 verdicts + A8e8 fix list (round-7 sealed). **M3** packet rewritten to strip ALL historical lineage text — no "(post-A8e F4)", "(added by A8eN)", "(chain position N via A8e)", "(+ A8eN fix XN)". Packet describes WHAT IS, no HOW; lineage lives only in this history doc. **M4 (substantive Codex R7#4)** PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2(b) corrected: removed false claim that "§ 3 conditions ensure calibration runs *before* Phase E"; § 3 conditions are PRE-REQUISITES for calibration TO RUN at all, not guarantees of completion before any specific phase. § 8 reads as the operative rule: substitution permitted at any phase including Phase E; calibration UPGRADES the bar IF and WHEN § 3 conditions complete. Single semantics, internally consistent across § 2 / § 3 / § 8. Re-hashed in Trust Root. **M5** audit runner scripts now append `experiments/minif2f_v4/tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs` so Q1.c (verify wrapper fail-closed) is verifiable from the packet bundle alone. Trust Root manifest unchanged at 35 (only re-hashes; no new files).
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R8.md:5718:handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:15:- A8e6 (post-A8e5, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-6 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini CHALLENGE — both narrow): one real correctness fix + 4 doc fixes. **K1** packet `<pending>` placeholder cleanup (J2 + RQ14 wording) — same precedent as A8e5 J2 but applied to round-6 self-references. **K2 (real bug, Codex R6#2)** `src/drivers/llm_proxy.py` `detect_provider`: HuggingFace-style `deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1-Distill-Qwen-7B` was misrouting to `api.deepseek.com` because `"deepseek" in m` won before the slash check; pre-K2 logic would 404 on every Distill variant since the official DeepSeek API doesn't serve them. K2 swaps the order — slash-form is now the FIRST routing heuristic (after explicit prefix), and bare-deepseek substring only fires when there's no slash. New `test_deepseek_slash_form_routes_to_siliconflow` in `scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` pins this (covers `deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1-Distill-Qwen-7B` + `deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-V2.5` AS WELL AS the bare-form sanity case `deepseek-chat` → deepseek). **K3** TRACE_MATRIX A8e3 row stale "21 positional args" → 24 (matches packet update from A8e5 J5; Codex R6#3 caught the inconsistency between TRACE_MATRIX and the packet). **K4** TRACE_MATRIX A6 row top-line "Six anchor sites" → "9 anchor sites" with A6-original-6 + F4-added-3 breakdown (Gemini R6#1 caught the contradiction between the line's first sentence and its own follow-up). **K5** packet § 3 A5/A6 fix attribution: "A8e3 fix H4 corrected" → "A8e4 fix I2 corrected" (Gemini R6#2 caught the cross-reference error — H4 corrected the genesis_payload.toml TR-header comment, I2 corrected the packet § 3 numbers). Trust Root manifest unchanged at 34 (re-hashes of `llm_proxy.py` + `scripts/test_llm_proxy.py`; no new files).
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R8.md:5721:handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:18:- A8e3 (post-A8e2, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-3 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini CHALLENGE — both convergent on packet staleness; no VETO): six narrow cleanup fixes. **H1** A8 packet § 3 A1 description: "Mathematically conservative" → "least-strict admissible value" with explicit Type-I implications. **H2** packet § 3 A6 description: 6 → 9 anchor sites (added F4 swarm sites explicitly). **H3** packet § 6 Q2.a / Q4.a / Q4.d marked CLOSED with closure rationale. **H4** `genesis_payload.toml` TR header phrasing about A1: "conservative ceiling" → "max-tolerated ceiling". **H5** § 5 item 7 here in TRACE_MATRIX: "(commit pending) + 6 wired" → "CLOSED + 9 wired". **H6** G1 wrapper test fail-closed on missing `python3` (was soft skip; Codex R3 caught the silent-pass risk); explicit opt-out `SKIP_LLM_PROXY_PYTHON_CONFORMANCE=1` for deliberate downgrades (logged loudly). Trust Root manifest unchanged at 34 (only re-hashes; no new files). Non-blocking observation deferred to Phase B+: `make_pput` signature was 21 positional args at round-3; A8e fix F1 added the `run_id` parameter, bringing the post-A8e count to **24** (Gemini R3 Finding 2 + Gemini R5/Codex R6 spot-checks; A8e6 K3 corrected here) — refactor to a builder pattern is recommended but not gating.
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R8.md:5722:handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:19:- A8e2 (post-A8e, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-2 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini CHALLENGE — both convergent): three dual-audit fixes. **G1** (Codex R2#1 + Gemini R2#1) `experiments/minif2f_v4/tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs` wraps `python3 scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` in a Rust integration test, making the V3L-27 round-robin conformance gate run on every `cargo test --workspace`. **G2** (Codex R2#2) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 8` audit-requirements paragraph reworded — round-1 fix corrected § 2 but left § 8 still calling the substitution a "strictest plausible bar"; now consistent across the doc. Re-hashed in Trust Root. **G3** (Codex R2#3 + Gemini R2#2) `A8_EXIT_PACKET` updated for round-2 reality: § 2 cumulative-tests table extended with A8e/A8e2 columns, § 4 manifest-clean assertion bumped 30→34, § 5 round-1 risk #5 (no FC1-N12 in swarm verify path) removed (closed by F4), § 6 Q7.a/b numbers updated, round-2 outcome + round-3 fixes section appended; TRACE_MATRIX stale `run_corr_id` symbol row replaced; "in CI" softened to "via Rust wrapper post-A8e2". FC-trace: meta governance + V3L-27 mitigation gating (FC1-N7). Trust Root manifest 33 → 34 (`tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs`).
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R8.md:5723:handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:20:- A8e (post-A7, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-1 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini VETO): six dual-audit fixes. **F1** (Codex#2 + Gemini Q4) `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/run_id.rs` mints one stable run_id per run, threaded into both `emit_event` and `make_pput` (replaces `run_corr_id` vs internal `run_id` ms drift; oneshot also stops using `oneshot_{problem_file}` placeholder). **F2** (Gemini VETO Q6) `scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` adds 15-test conformance battery — round-robin distributes [2,2,2] across 6 calls, single-key pool degrades to all-zero, two-key pool alternates [2,2]; exercises the V3L-27 mitigation without a live API. (A8e2 G1 wires this into `cargo test --workspace` via a Rust shell-out wrapper — see § 2 below.) **F3** (Codex#4) routing matrix bug fixed — `Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct` was misrouting to DashScope because `m.startswith("qwen")` won after the slash check; now slash-form ⇒ siliconflow first, bare-qwen ⇒ dashscope second. **F4** (Codex#3) FC1-N12 emit added to all 3 swarm verify sites (`verify_omega_detailed` × 2 + `verify_partial`), each with verdict + elapsed_ms + path stamp; closes the swarm-side gap in the 5-step compile-loop witness. **F5** (Codex#5) `genesis_payload.toml` header + TRACE_MATRIX milestones reconciled to actual 31 entries (round-1 packet undercounted by 1 — agent_models.rs from A3 was already in TR before A1). **F6** (Codex#1) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2` wording corrected — `p_0=0.10` substitution is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling (max tolerated value for `j-RR ≤ p_0`), NOT the strictest possible substitute as round-1 mistakenly claimed; explicit statistical-implications paragraph added (no Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive; substitution may be less protective than eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`). FC-trace: FC1-N12 (oracle scope swarm coverage) + FC1-N7 (δ provider routing) + Art. V (amendment governance). Trust Root manifest 31 → 33 (run_id.rs + test_llm_proxy.py).

./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R8.md:5733:handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:106:7. ~~**Per-line FC tagging via tracing crate** — Plan agent's recommendation in N-experiments brainstorm. Phase A6 deferred; will land before Phase B (homogeneous experiments).~~ **CLOSED**: A6 (`89994c7`) shipped `fc_trace.rs` + 6 production emit sites (5 in `run_swarm` + 1 in `run_oneshot`); A8e fix F4 added 3 more in `run_swarm` (`verify_omega_detailed` alone + tape+payload retry, plus `verify_partial`), bringing the post-A8e total to **9 wired anchor sites**. Implementation chose pure stdlib over the `tracing` crate to avoid a new dep tree; the macro surface (`emit_event` + `FcId` enum) was kept small so Phase D+ can swap to a real `tracing-subscriber` bridge locally.
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R8.md:5740:handover/preregistration/PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md:19:**The 8-hour estimate was based on "~50s/run chat oneshot" assumption that turned out wrong for swarm n3 condition on the adaptation-144 problem mix.** A 7-day batch is not "overnight"; user (mid-session 2026-04-25) explicitly questioned 576-run necessity given multiple unresolved engineering questions (N-agents → PPUT relationship, swarm_N=1 vs oneshot calibration, ground-truth feedback pipeline, etc.).
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R8.md:5741:handover/preregistration/PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md:27:**Wording correction (A8 round-1 audit, Codex finding 1)**: an earlier draft of this section claimed `p_0 = 0.10` was the *strictest possible substitute*. That was backwards. The Gate H test is `j-RR ≤ p_0`, so a SMALLER `p_0` is stricter. `0.10` is the **maximum tolerated value** the PREREG admits — i.e., the LEAST strict admissible ceiling, not the strictest. Substituting it is the equivalent of running Gate H at the weakest threshold the PREREG ever sanctioned. The substitution is *operationally permitted* (the PREREG explicitly allows up to 0.10), but it is NOT a tighter-than-original guarantee.
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R8.md:5744:handover/preregistration/PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md:46:3. **Per-agent budget normalization landed** (Phase A atom A5) — current `max_transactions=200` is fixed-tx budget; PREREG § 5.5 implicitly assumes tx-budget but doesn't specify; need explicit budget regime declaration for calibration to be reproducible.
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R8.md:5747:handover/preregistration/PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md:104:- `handover/architect-insights/THESIS_V2_GROUND_TRUTH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md` (context: ground-truth feedback discipline)
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R8.md:5759:handover/audits/A8_AUDIT_HISTORY_2026-04-26.md:56:1. (Codex R2#1 + Gemini R2#1) F2's `test_llm_proxy.py` exists but is documented as manual-only; no CI integration. Tests-not-running-automatically = documentation, not gates. Closes Gemini's round-1 VETO at the *artifact* level but NOT at the *process* level.
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R8.md:5805:handover/audits/A8_EXIT_PACKET_2026-04-26.md:7:**Decision rule**: PASS → Phase B (kernel instrumentation + PPUT accounting) authorized; CHALLENGE → in-cycle fixes; VETO → Phase A redesign.

./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R8.md:5810:handover/audits/A8_EXIT_PACKET_2026-04-26.md:104:  - explicit `provider:...` prefix → that provider (if known)
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R8.md:5811:handover/audits/A8_EXIT_PACKET_2026-04-26.md:111:- `scripts/test_llm_proxy.py`: 16-test routing + round-robin conformance suite (no live API). Wrapped by `experiments/minif2f_v4/tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs` so the suite runs on every `cargo test --workspace`. The wrapper FAILS CLOSED if `python3` is missing; explicit opt-out `SKIP_LLM_PROXY_PYTHON_CONFORMANCE=1` (logged loudly).
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R8.md:5825:handover/audits/A8_EXIT_PACKET_2026-04-26.md:144:- (Q2.b) Re-calibration conditions in § 3 are PRE-REQUISITES for calibration to run, not guarantees that calibration completes before any specific phase. Is this framed correctly without claiming calibration MUST run before Phase E?
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R8.md:5827:handover/preregistration/PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md:27:**Wording correction (A8 round-1 audit, Codex finding 1)**: an earlier draft of this section claimed `p_0 = 0.10` was the *strictest possible substitute*. That was backwards. The Gate H test is `j-RR ≤ p_0`, so a SMALLER `p_0` is stricter. `0.10` is the **maximum tolerated value** the PREREG admits — i.e., the LEAST strict admissible ceiling, not the strictest. Substituting it is the equivalent of running Gate H at the weakest threshold the PREREG ever sanctioned. The substitution is *operationally permitted* (the PREREG explicitly allows up to 0.10), but it is NOT a tighter-than-original guarantee.
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R8.md:5829:handover/preregistration/PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md:31:- The substitution may be LESS protective than an eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`. If/when calibration runs (per § 3 conditions, which are PRE-REQUISITES — they do not by themselves *guarantee* calibration completes before any specific phase), the empirical estimate may tighten the bar; until then Gate H operates at the ceiling.
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R8.md:5836:handover/preregistration/PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md:96:- Does deferral leave any phase blocked indefinitely? (Should not — § 3 lists explicit re-calibration conditions; if those never met, Phase E proceeds with the operationally-permitted ceiling substitution per § 2 final paragraph.)
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R8.md:5844:handover/audits/A8_AUDIT_HISTORY_2026-04-26.md:39:- **F6** PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2 reworded: `p_0=0.10` → least-strict admissible ceiling; explicit Type-I implications paragraph added. Closes #5.
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R8.md:5847:handover/audits/A8_AUDIT_HISTORY_2026-04-26.md:63:- **G3** Packet § 2 cumulative table extended; § 5 Risk #5 removed; § 6 Q7.a/b numbers updated 264/34; TRACE_MATRIX `run_corr_id` row replaced with unified `run_id` row; "in CI" softened. Closes finding #3.
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R8.md:5857:handover/audits/A8_AUDIT_HISTORY_2026-04-26.md:201:- **M1** (Codex R7#1): Packet TR count bumped 34 → 35 wherever cited.
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R8.md:5858:handover/audits/A8_AUDIT_HISTORY_2026-04-26.md:204:- **M4** (Codex R7#4): PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2 + § 3 + § 8 reconciled: removed the false claim that § 3 "ensures calibration before Phase E"; § 8 now reads as the operative rule (Phase E proceeds with the substitution if § 3 conditions haven't completed). Clean single semantics: substitution is permitted throughout Phase B → Phase E; calibration UPGRADES the bar IF and WHEN the § 3 conditions all complete. Re-hashed in Trust Root.
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R8.md:5928:    27	**Wording correction (A8 round-1 audit, Codex finding 1)**: an earlier draft of this section claimed `p_0 = 0.10` was the *strictest possible substitute*. That was backwards. The Gate H test is `j-RR ≤ p_0`, so a SMALLER `p_0` is stricter. `0.10` is the **maximum tolerated value** the PREREG admits — i.e., the LEAST strict admissible ceiling, not the strictest. Substituting it is the equivalent of running Gate H at the weakest threshold the PREREG ever sanctioned. The substitution is *operationally permitted* (the PREREG explicitly allows up to 0.10), but it is NOT a tighter-than-original guarantee.
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R8.md:5932:    31	- The substitution may be LESS protective than an eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`. If/when calibration runs (per § 3 conditions, which are PRE-REQUISITES — they do not by themselves *guarantee* calibration completes before any specific phase), the empirical estimate may tighten the bar; until then Gate H operates at the ceiling.
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R8.md:5947:    46	3. **Per-agent budget normalization landed** (Phase A atom A5) — current `max_transactions=200` is fixed-tx budget; PREREG § 5.5 implicitly assumes tx-budget but doesn't specify; need explicit budget regime declaration for calibration to be reproducible.
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R8.md:5957:    56	PREREG_PPUT_CCL_2026-04-26 § 5.5 originally listed p_0 calibration as a Phase B prerequisite ("Schedule: Phase B7 mandatory; not deferrable to Phase D"). This amendment **explicitly OVERRIDES that "not deferrable" clause** for the deferral conditions in § 3 above.
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R8.md:5970:    69	- **PREREG § 5.5 protocol itself** — the calibration *protocol* (288 control + 288 treatment paired runs, max-over-seeds, etc.) remains the agreed-upon procedure for IF calibration ever runs. Amendment defers the SCHEDULING, not the SCIENCE.
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R8.md:5980:    79	This pattern is per CLAUDE.md "Common Law": amendments are recorded as separate cases / docs that supersede specific sections, leaving the original frozen for reproducibility. PREREG_PPUT_CCL_2026-04-26.md SHA-256 in Trust Root manifest UNCHANGED.

./handover/audits/GEMINI_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R12.md:14:All specific questions posed to the auditor (Q1–Q5) have been verified against the provided materials and are found to be satisfactory. The fixes for the 13 substantive findings from prior rounds, including critical issues in routing logic, statistical framing, and gate machinery, have been confirmed as correctly implemented and holding in the current state.
./handover/audits/GEMINI_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R2.md:18:I have reviewed the A8 packet, the six fixes (F1–F6), and the associated source code. The fixes substantively address the letter of the five findings from Codex and the VETO-level finding from Gemini in the round-1 audit. The unification of `run_id` (F1), the correction of the routing matrix (F3), and the improved wording of the PREREG amendment (F6) are particularly well-executed.
./handover/audits/GEMINI_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R2.md:26:However, the core principle of a conformance test is its recurring, automated execution. The audit packet asks this directly in RQ3: "does it run in any CI pipeline, or only manually?". The packet provides no answer, and there is no evidence (e.g., a change to a CI configuration file) that this test is executed automatically as part of the pre-commit, push, or merge pipeline.
./handover/audits/GEMINI_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R2.md:32:-   **Required Fix**: The `test_llm_proxy.py` script must be integrated into an automated CI pipeline that runs on relevant code changes. Evidence of this integration (e.g., a modified CI configuration file) must be included in the next audit packet.
./handover/audits/GEMINI_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R2.md:38:Risk #5 states: "No FC1-N12 emit in `run_swarm` verify path." This was a valid finding in round 1 (and was Codex's finding #3). However, fix F4 explicitly addresses this by adding `emit_event` calls for `FcId::Fc1N12` at all three verification sites in `run_swarm`: the two `verify_omega_detailed` calls and the one `verify_partial` call.
./handover/audits/run_gemini_whitepaper_v2_audit.py:31:**Status**: v2 is NOT a constitution.md edit. constitution.md remains supreme. v2 acts as the highest校准 mirror over derivative documents (Plan v3.2 / Blueprint / v1 whitepaper / Deepthink). The user explicitly chose "tactical alignment" over "full constitutional merge" to skip Phase Z′ 6-stage rerun + TRACE_MATRIX rewrite + conformance test rebuild.
./handover/audits/run_gemini_whitepaper_v2_audit.py:37:Q1. **Anti-Oreo coherence**: Does v2 preserve constitution.md's Anti-Oreo three-layer structure (⚪⚫⚪) without weakening it? Specifically does v2 § 3 give bottom-white tools equal weight with top-white predicates?
./handover/audits/run_gemini_whitepaper_v2_audit.py:39:Q2. **ChainTape-as-implementation discipline**: v2 § 公理 5 + § 13 + § 18 explicitly forbid "blockchain as the body of TuringOS." Does v2 maintain this discipline throughout? Any text that accidentally reverts to chain-as-primary?
./handover/audits/run_gemini_whitepaper_v2_audit.py:41:Q3. **Constitutional consistency**: Are there any v2 statements that contradict constitution.md (Art 0 Turing fundamentalism + Art 0.1 4-element mapping + Art 0.2 Tape Canonical + Art 0.3 区块链化保留 + Art 0.4 Q_t version-controlled + Art I-V signal management + Art IV Boot + Art V Go Meta)? List each conflict precisely.
./handover/audits/run_gemini_whitepaper_v2_audit.py:55:Q10. **Tactical-alignment status sustainability**: Can v2 reasonably function as "highest校准 mirror above derivative docs but below constitution.md"? Or does this dual authority create ambiguity that will fail under future Wave decisions?
./handover/audits/run_gemini_whitepaper_v2_audit.py:61:## Q1-Q10 verdicts (with §/file:line citations)
./handover/audits/GEMINI_CO1_7_EXTRA_ROUND4_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:15:-   **MF4 TuringBus placement (strengthened by B3)**: The round-2 decision to place the `Sequencer` in `TuringBus`—preserving the `Kernel`'s "pure topology" doctrine—is now perfectly reflected throughout the v1.2 spec. Patch B3 removes the last two stale references to the `Kernel` from the single-sentence summary and the pre-implementation gate. This corrective action eliminates textual dissonance and makes the architectural intent read coherently end-to-end. This is a strict improvement in clarity.
./handover/audits/GEMINI_CO1_7_EXTRA_ROUND4_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:17:-   **MF2 helper extraction (soundness preserved by B2)**: Widening the visibility of `advance_head_t` from `pub(crate)` to `pub` is an appropriate and pragmatic engineering trade-off that does not compromise encapsulation. The function is a well-defined, pure helper encapsulating a critical piece of constitutional logic. Making it `pub` is necessary for the flat integration test structure (per round-2 MF5) to directly verify its behavior. This is a standard Rust pattern for testability and is preferable to a more brittle, complex test that would need to invoke the entire `apply_one` machinery. The spec's explicit justification for this change is sound.
./handover/audits/GEMINI_CO1_7_EXTRA_ROUND4_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:23:In summary, the v1.2 patches successfully address the concrete blockers from the round-3 CHALLENGE without introducing any architectural regression.
./handover/audits/GEMINI_CO1_7_EXTRA_ROUND4_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:27:None. The v1.2 patches are exclusively corrective. They fix a compile error (B1), adjust visibility for testability in a standard way (B2), and resolve documentation inconsistencies (B3, B4). These changes do not introduce any new architectural surfaces or defects; they simply improve the correctness, testability, and clarity of the v1.1 architecture that was already deemed sound.
./handover/audits/GEMINI_CO1_7_EXTRA_ROUND4_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:37:High. This round-4 audit verifies a small set of mechanical patches that were direct responses to the round-3 CHALLENGE verdict. The patches are correct, well-justified, and improve the overall quality of the specification. The dual-audit process has successfully converged on a sound, coherent, and implementable design that is ready to pass its pre-implementation gate.

./handover/audits/run_codex_co1_7_impl_bundle_round1_audit.sh:24:| **A1** Git2LedgerWriter | `a03cc52` | ~525 | 5 | git2-rs commit chain on `refs/transitions/main`; tree blobs (payload_cid / signature / entry_canonical); deterministic author time (logical_t, 0); reopen_recovers_chain test for cold-restart capability |
./handover/audits/run_codex_co1_7_impl_bundle_round1_audit.sh:46:**Q-A. A1 (Git2LedgerWriter) integration discipline**: tree-blob choice (payload_cid + signature + entry_canonical) vs original commit-message-body approach (which had a binary normalization bug). Is the deterministic author time (`(logical_t as i64, 0)`) sufficient for byte-identical commit OIDs across runs? Are the 5 tests load-bearing (open_recovers_chain proves cold-restart; what else might miss)?
./handover/audits/run_codex_co1_7_impl_bundle_round1_audit.sh:50:**Q-C. A2 Sequencer ApplyError vs spec § 3 line 307 deviation**: spec writes apply_one signature as `Result<LedgerEntry, TransitionError>`; impl widens to `Result<LedgerEntry, ApplyError>` with TransitionError as a wrapped variant. **Defensible vs spec drift?** Argument for: TransitionError stays closed-taxonomy (CO1.1.4-pre1 § 7.2 additive-only commitment); infra errors (CasError / KeypairError / LedgerWriterError) shouldn't pollute it. Argument against: spec is authoritative, deviations need explicit closure.
./handover/audits/run_codex_co1_7_impl_bundle_round1_audit.sh:60:**Q-H. CO1.4-extra sidecar JSONL discipline**: append BEFORE in-memory insert (so crash mid-write keeps runtime consistent — durable+memory both present, or neither). Strict mode on corrupted JSONL line (returns IndexParse error vs skip-and-warn). Both correct?
./handover/audits/run_codex_co1_7_impl_bundle_round1_audit.sh:62:**Q-I. CO1.4-extra durability gap on idempotent put**: idempotent put short-circuits before sidecar write (correct — content is already durable from prior put). But what about a partial write that left the in-memory index updated but sidecar not flushed? Current code appends THEN inserts — addresses this. Confirm no other gap.
./handover/audits/run_codex_co1_7_impl_bundle_round1_audit.sh:64:**Q-J. Cross-atom A2↔A4 consistency**: Sequencer.apply_one stages 5-9 (sign + ledger_root fold + commit) vs replay_full_transition stages 4-9 (verify + decode + dispatch + state_root + ledger_root). Are these symmetric — does every byte the sequencer writes get verified by replay? Specifically: signing payload digest pre-image must be EXACTLY what apply_one constructed at stage 5 vs what replay reconstructs at stage 4.
./handover/audits/run_codex_co1_7_impl_bundle_round1_audit.sh:67:- Type errors that cargo check missed?
./handover/audits/run_codex_co1_7_impl_bundle_round1_audit.sh:72:**Q-L. Implementation gating**: with bundle at 237/0 PASS, is CO1.7.5 (per-kind transition bodies + STEP_B bus.rs/kernel.rs wiring) implementable end-to-end against this bundle? Specific blockers to call out.
./handover/audits/run_codex_co1_7_impl_bundle_round1_audit.sh:84:## Q-H CO1.4-extra sidecar discipline

./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:132:### A6 (FC tracing) — `89994c7` (+ A8e fix F4)
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:135:  - FC2-N22 synthetic short-circuit (swarm)
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:146:- 7 tests (6 unit + 1 end-to-end smoke `tests/fc_trace_smoke.rs` exercising `FC_TRACE=1` in a child process). Plus 3 unit tests in `run_id.rs`.
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:150:- `src/drivers/llm_proxy.py` ported from v3 with one load-bearing v4 change: per-provider multi-key round-robin. `detect_provider()` routing (post-A8e F3 + A8e6 K2): `provider:...` explicit prefix wins; else slash-form ⇒ `siliconflow` FIRST (covers `Qwen/...`, `openai/...`, `meta-llama/...`, `deepseek-ai/...` HuggingFace-style ids); else `"deepseek" in m` ⇒ `deepseek` (bare-form only); else `m.startswith("qwen")` ⇒ `dashscope`; else `dashscope` default.
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:153:- `scripts/test_llm_proxy.py`: 16-test routing + round-robin conformance suite (no live API). Wrapped by `experiments/minif2f_v4/tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs` (added by A8e2 G1) so the suite runs on every `cargo test --workspace`. The wrapper FAILS CLOSED if `python3` is missing (A8e3 H6); explicit opt-out `SKIP_LLM_PROXY_PYTHON_CONFORMANCE=1`.
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:171:2. **FC-trace coverage still partial**. 9 wired anchor sites cover HALT decomposition (FC2-N22 × 4 exit paths) + mr tick (FC2-N20) + Lean oracle scope (FC1-N12 × 4 sites: oneshot + swarm `verify_omega_detailed` × 2 + swarm `verify_partial`). Still NOT emitting: FC1-N7 prompt-build, FC1-N11 ∏p decision diversity (per-proposal), FC1-E18 preserve-Q_t (per ∏p=0), FC3-N31 WAL append. The `FcId` enum reserves these variants. Phase B+ kernel instrumentation should fill them in as the Phase B emit boundary lands.

(**Codex R6#2 — REAL BUG**) `detect_provider` misroutes HuggingFace-style `deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1-Distill-Qwen-7B` to `api.deepseek.com` because `"deepseek" in m` wins before the slash check. The official DeepSeek API only serves bare `deepseek-chat` / `deepseek-v4-flash`, not Distill variants — would 404 on first invocation.
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:436:PREREG § 5.5 specifies p_0 calibration via 576 paired runs (144 adaptation × 2 seeds × {control, treatment}) with estimated cost "~8 wall-hours, ~$3-5". Empirical observation 2026-04-25 during launched batch (commit 650caf7+ era):
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:441:| Total batch wall-clock | ~8 hours | Realistic 3-7 days (576 runs × 15 min serial; treatment short-circuits halve some) | 9-21× |
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:444:**The 8-hour estimate was based on "~50s/run chat oneshot" assumption that turned out wrong for swarm n3 condition on the adaptation-144 problem mix.** A 7-day batch is not "overnight"; user (mid-session 2026-04-25) explicitly questioned 576-run necessity given multiple unresolved engineering questions (N-agents → PPUT relationship, swarm_N=1 vs oneshot calibration, ground-truth feedback pipeline, etc.).
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:452:**Wording correction (A8 round-1 audit, Codex finding 1)**: an earlier draft of this section claimed `p_0 = 0.10` was the *strictest possible substitute*. That was backwards. The Gate H test is `j-RR ≤ p_0`, so a SMALLER `p_0` is stricter. `0.10` is the **maximum tolerated value** the PREREG admits — i.e., the LEAST strict admissible ceiling, not the strictest. Substituting it is the equivalent of running Gate H at the weakest threshold the PREREG ever sanctioned. The substitution is *operationally permitted* (the PREREG explicitly allows up to 0.10), but it is NOT a tighter-than-original guarantee.
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:471:3. **Per-agent budget normalization landed** (Phase A atom A5) — current `max_transactions=200` is fixed-tx budget; PREREG § 5.5 implicitly assumes tx-budget but doesn't specify; need explicit budget regime declaration for calibration to be reproducible.
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:481:PREREG_PPUT_CCL_2026-04-26 § 5.5 originally listed p_0 calibration as a Phase B prerequisite ("Schedule: Phase B7 mandatory; not deferrable to Phase D"). This amendment **explicitly OVERRIDES that "not deferrable" clause** for the deferral conditions in § 3 above.
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:494:- **PREREG § 5.5 protocol itself** — the calibration *protocol* (288 control + 288 treatment paired runs, max-over-seeds, etc.) remains the agreed-upon procedure for IF calibration ever runs. Amendment defers the SCHEDULING, not the SCIENCE.
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:504:This pattern is per CLAUDE.md "Common Law": amendments are recorded as separate cases / docs that supersede specific sections, leaving the original frozen for reproducibility. PREREG_PPUT_CCL_2026-04-26.md SHA-256 in Trust Root manifest UNCHANGED.
