./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:5264:- A0a: 4 new rules (R-014/R-015/R-018/R-019) + judge.sh constitution-special-case + R-016 fc_trace_in_commit hook (commit 2e7f75a)
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:5266:- A0c: 5 new cases C-071..C-075 sediment session decisions (commit 2a65339)
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:5268:- A4 (post-A3): decomposed metrics — `hit_max_tx`, `tactic_diversity`, `verifier_wait_ms` added as non-Optional v2 fields + `compute_tactic_diversity` helper; per-row decomposition of `solve_rate` / `tokens_per_solve` / `time_per_solve` (all derivable from existing `progress` / `total_run_token_count` / `total_wall_time_ms`). FC-trace: FC2-N22 (HALT decomposition for `hit_max_tx`) + FC1-N11 (∏p decision diversity for `tactic_diversity`) + FC1-N12 (oracle scope for `verifier_wait_ms`).
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:5270:- A6 (post-A5): per-line FC tagging via structured JSON events — new `fc_trace` module (pure stdlib; zero new deps); `FcId` enum (FC1-N7 / FC1-N11 / FC1-N12 / FC1-E18 / FC2-N20 / FC2-N22 / FC3-N31); `fc_event!`-style `emit_event` API; `FC_TRACE=1` gate (cached in `OnceLock`); `FC_TRACE_FILE=<path>` redirects emit to file (default sink stderr). 9 anchor sites wired (post-A8e total — 8 in `run_swarm` + 1 in `run_oneshot`): the original A6 commit shipped 6 sites (FC2-N22 synthetic short-circuit, FC2-N20 mr tick, FC2-N22 OMEGA full-proof, FC2-N22 OMEGA per-tactic, FC2-N22 natural MaxTxExhausted with `budget_regime` payload, FC1-N12 verify bracket in oneshot). A8e fix F4 added 3 more in `run_swarm` (FC1-N12 verify_omega_detailed × 2 paths + FC1-N12 verify_partial), bringing the post-A8e total to 9 production emit sites. Counted by grepping `fc_trace::emit_event(` in `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/bin/evaluator.rs` (Gemini R6 finding: round-5 phrasing "Six anchor sites" was stale relative to its own following sentence — corrected here in A8e6 K4). End-to-end smoke test exercises FC_TRACE=1 in a child process (subprocess required because `OnceLock` caches the gate-read; resolves item 7 of TRACE_MATRIX § 5 "Per-line FC tagging via tracing crate"). FC-trace: meta-witness for the 5-step compile loop (Proposal → Lean ground truth → Logging → Capability compilation → ↑H-VPPUT). Trust Root manifest 27 → 28 (corrected A8e5 J4; matches the canonical chain in `genesis_payload.toml` header).
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:5273:- A8e6 (post-A8e5, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-6 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini CHALLENGE — both narrow): one real correctness fix + 4 doc fixes. **K1** packet `<pending>` placeholder cleanup (J2 + RQ14 wording) — same precedent as A8e5 J2 but applied to round-6 self-references. **K2 (real bug, Codex R6#2)** `src/drivers/llm_proxy.py` `detect_provider`: HuggingFace-style `deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1-Distill-Qwen-7B` was misrouting to `api.deepseek.com` because `"deepseek" in m` won before the slash check; pre-K2 logic would 404 on every Distill variant since the official DeepSeek API doesn't serve them. K2 swaps the order — slash-form is now the FIRST routing heuristic (after explicit prefix), and bare-deepseek substring only fires when there's no slash. New `test_deepseek_slash_form_routes_to_siliconflow` in `scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` pins this (covers `deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1-Distill-Qwen-7B` + `deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-V2.5` AS WELL AS the bare-form sanity case `deepseek-chat` → deepseek). **K3** TRACE_MATRIX A8e3 row stale "21 positional args" → 24 (matches packet update from A8e5 J5; Codex R6#3 caught the inconsistency between TRACE_MATRIX and the packet). **K4** TRACE_MATRIX A6 row top-line "Six anchor sites" → "9 anchor sites" with A6-original-6 + F4-added-3 breakdown (Gemini R6#1 caught the contradiction between the line's first sentence and its own follow-up). **K5** packet § 3 A5/A6 fix attribution: "A8e3 fix H4 corrected" → "A8e4 fix I2 corrected" (Gemini R6#2 caught the cross-reference error — H4 corrected the genesis_payload.toml TR-header comment, I2 corrected the packet § 3 numbers). Trust Root manifest unchanged at 34 (re-hashes of `llm_proxy.py` + `scripts/test_llm_proxy.py`; no new files).
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:5276:- A8e3 (post-A8e2, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-3 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini CHALLENGE — both convergent on packet staleness; no VETO): six narrow cleanup fixes. **H1** A8 packet § 3 A1 description: "Mathematically conservative" → "least-strict admissible value" with explicit Type-I implications. **H2** packet § 3 A6 description: 6 → 9 anchor sites (added F4 swarm sites explicitly). **H3** packet § 6 Q2.a / Q4.a / Q4.d marked CLOSED with closure rationale. **H4** `genesis_payload.toml` TR header phrasing about A1: "conservative ceiling" → "max-tolerated ceiling". **H5** § 5 item 7 here in TRACE_MATRIX: "(commit pending) + 6 wired" → "CLOSED + 9 wired". **H6** G1 wrapper test fail-closed on missing `python3` (was soft skip; Codex R3 caught the silent-pass risk); explicit opt-out `SKIP_LLM_PROXY_PYTHON_CONFORMANCE=1` for deliberate downgrades (logged loudly). Trust Root manifest unchanged at 34 (only re-hashes; no new files). Non-blocking observation deferred to Phase B+: `make_pput` signature was 21 positional args at round-3; A8e fix F1 added the `run_id` parameter, bringing the post-A8e count to **24** (Gemini R3 Finding 2 + Gemini R5/Codex R6 spot-checks; A8e6 K3 corrected here) — refactor to a builder pattern is recommended but not gating.
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:5277:- A8e2 (post-A8e, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-2 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini CHALLENGE — both convergent): three dual-audit fixes. **G1** (Codex R2#1 + Gemini R2#1) `experiments/minif2f_v4/tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs` wraps `python3 scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` in a Rust integration test, making the V3L-27 round-robin conformance gate run on every `cargo test --workspace`. **G2** (Codex R2#2) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 8` audit-requirements paragraph reworded — round-1 fix corrected § 2 but left § 8 still calling the substitution a "strictest plausible bar"; now consistent across the doc. Re-hashed in Trust Root. **G3** (Codex R2#3 + Gemini R2#2) `A8_EXIT_PACKET` updated for round-2 reality: § 2 cumulative-tests table extended with A8e/A8e2 columns, § 4 manifest-clean assertion bumped 30→34, § 5 round-1 risk #5 (no FC1-N12 in swarm verify path) removed (closed by F4), § 6 Q7.a/b numbers updated, round-2 outcome + round-3 fixes section appended; TRACE_MATRIX stale `run_corr_id` symbol row replaced; "in CI" softened to "via Rust wrapper post-A8e2". FC-trace: meta governance + V3L-27 mitigation gating (FC1-N7). Trust Root manifest 33 → 34 (`tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs`).
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:5278:- A8e (post-A7, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-1 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini VETO): six dual-audit fixes. **F1** (Codex#2 + Gemini Q4) `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/run_id.rs` mints one stable run_id per run, threaded into both `emit_event` and `make_pput` (replaces `run_corr_id` vs internal `run_id` ms drift; oneshot also stops using `oneshot_{problem_file}` placeholder). **F2** (Gemini VETO Q6) `scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` adds 15-test conformance battery — round-robin distributes [2,2,2] across 6 calls, single-key pool degrades to all-zero, two-key pool alternates [2,2]; exercises the V3L-27 mitigation without a live API. (A8e2 G1 wires this into `cargo test --workspace` via a Rust shell-out wrapper — see § 2 below.) **F3** (Codex#4) routing matrix bug fixed — `Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct` was misrouting to DashScope because `m.startswith("qwen")` won after the slash check; now slash-form ⇒ siliconflow first, bare-qwen ⇒ dashscope second. **F4** (Codex#3) FC1-N12 emit added to all 3 swarm verify sites (`verify_omega_detailed` × 2 + `verify_partial`), each with verdict + elapsed_ms + path stamp; closes the swarm-side gap in the 5-step compile-loop witness. **F5** (Codex#5) `genesis_payload.toml` header + TRACE_MATRIX milestones reconciled to actual 31 entries (round-1 packet undercounted by 1 — agent_models.rs from A3 was already in TR before A1). **F6** (Codex#1) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2` wording corrected — `p_0=0.10` substitution is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling (max tolerated value for `j-RR ≤ p_0`), NOT the strictest possible substitute as round-1 mistakenly claimed; explicit statistical-implications paragraph added (no Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive; substitution may be less protective than eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`). FC-trace: FC1-N12 (oracle scope swarm coverage) + FC1-N7 (δ provider routing) + Art. V (amendment governance). Trust Root manifest 31 → 33 (run_id.rs + test_llm_proxy.py).
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:5286:A0b added the missing `tests/fc_alignment_conformance.rs` (was only in `.claude/worktrees/phase-8a-snapshot/`). 17 ✅ rows in TRACE_MATRIX now have automated witness tests. Symbol drift is now caught at `cargo test` time, not at next dual audit.

./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:5326:| `run_id::mint_run_id` correlation key + 9 wired FC events (A6 + A8e F4) | `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/run_id.rs` + `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/bin/evaluator.rs` | A6 introduced a per-run correlation id (originally named `run_corr_id`, now unified into `run_id`) anchoring all events. A8e fix F1 (Codex#2 + Gemini Q4) split this out into a dedicated module + threaded the same id into `make_pput` so FC events and v2 jsonl rows share one identifier (no ms drift). Anchor sites: FC2-N22 synthetic short-circuit / mr tick FC2-N20 / OMEGA full-proof FC2-N22 / OMEGA per-tactic FC2-N22 / natural MaxTxExhausted FC2-N22 (with `budget_regime` payload from A5) / FC1-N12 oneshot verify (A6) / FC1-N12 swarm `verify_omega_detailed` × 2 paths (A8e F4) / FC1-N12 swarm `verify_partial` (A8e F4). End-to-end smoke `tests/fc_trace_smoke.rs` exercises FC_TRACE=1 in a child process (forced because `OnceLock` caches the gate-read). | ✅ |
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:5327:| `tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs` Rust→Python wrapper (A8e2 fix G1) | `experiments/minif2f_v4/tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs` | A8 round-2 audit (Codex R2#1 + Gemini R2#1): the round-robin conformance battery in `scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` was only manual; tests that don't run automatically are documentation, not gates. G1 wires the Python suite into `cargo test --workspace` via a Rust integration test that shells to `python3` and asserts exit 0 + the unittest "OK" trailer. The V3L-27 mitigation (case C-027) is now a recurring conformance gate exercised on every commit that touches Rust + every CI pipeline that already exercises Rust tests. | ✅ |

(**Codex R6#2 — REAL BUG**) `detect_provider` misroutes HuggingFace-style `deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1-Distill-Qwen-7B` to `api.deepseek.com` because `"deepseek" in m` wins before the slash check. The official DeepSeek API only serves bare `deepseek-chat` / `deepseek-v4-flash`, not Distill variants — would 404 on first invocation.

./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:6279:    11	PREREG § 5.5 specifies p_0 calibration via 576 paired runs (144 adaptation × 2 seeds × {control, treatment}) with estimated cost "~8 wall-hours, ~$3-5". Empirical observation 2026-04-25 during launched batch (commit 650caf7+ era):
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:6284:    16	| Total batch wall-clock | ~8 hours | Realistic 3-7 days (576 runs × 15 min serial; treatment short-circuits halve some) | 9-21× |
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:6287:    19	**The 8-hour estimate was based on "~50s/run chat oneshot" assumption that turned out wrong for swarm n3 condition on the adaptation-144 problem mix.** A 7-day batch is not "overnight"; user (mid-session 2026-04-25) explicitly questioned 576-run necessity given multiple unresolved engineering questions (N-agents → PPUT relationship, swarm_N=1 vs oneshot calibration, ground-truth feedback pipeline, etc.).
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:6295:    27	**Wording correction (A8 round-1 audit, Codex finding 1)**: an earlier draft of this section claimed `p_0 = 0.10` was the *strictest possible substitute*. That was backwards. The Gate H test is `j-RR ≤ p_0`, so a SMALLER `p_0` is stricter. `0.10` is the **maximum tolerated value** the PREREG admits — i.e., the LEAST strict admissible ceiling, not the strictest. Substituting it is the equivalent of running Gate H at the weakest threshold the PREREG ever sanctioned. The substitution is *operationally permitted* (the PREREG explicitly allows up to 0.10), but it is NOT a tighter-than-original guarantee.
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:6314:    46	3. **Per-agent budget normalization landed** (Phase A atom A5) — current `max_transactions=200` is fixed-tx budget; PREREG § 5.5 implicitly assumes tx-budget but doesn't specify; need explicit budget regime declaration for calibration to be reproducible.
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:6324:    56	PREREG_PPUT_CCL_2026-04-26 § 5.5 originally listed p_0 calibration as a Phase B prerequisite ("Schedule: Phase B7 mandatory; not deferrable to Phase D"). This amendment **explicitly OVERRIDES that "not deferrable" clause** for the deferral conditions in § 3 above.
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:6337:    69	- **PREREG § 5.5 protocol itself** — the calibration *protocol* (288 control + 288 treatment paired runs, max-over-seeds, etc.) remains the agreed-upon procedure for IF calibration ever runs. Amendment defers the SCHEDULING, not the SCIENCE.

Trust Root manifest unchanged at 34 (only re-hashes; no new files). Non-blocking observation deferred to Phase B+: `make_pput` signature was 21 positional args at round-3; A8e fix F1 added the `run_id` parameter, bringing the post-A8e count to **24** (Gemini R3 Finding 2 + Gemini R5/Codex R6 spot-checks; A8e6 K3 corrected here) — refactor to a builder pattern is recommended but not gating.
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:6380:handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:18:- A8e2 (post-A8e, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-2 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini CHALLENGE — both convergent): three dual-audit fixes. **G1** (Codex R2#1 + Gemini R2#1) `experiments/minif2f_v4/tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs` wraps `python3 scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` in a Rust integration test, making the V3L-27 round-robin conformance gate run on every `cargo test --workspace`. **G2** (Codex R2#2) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 8` audit-requirements paragraph reworded — round-1 fix corrected § 2 but left § 8 still calling the substitution a "strictest plausible bar"; now consistent across the doc. Re-hashed in Trust Root. **G3** (Codex R2#3 + Gemini R2#2) `A8_EXIT_PACKET` updated for round-2 reality: § 2 cumulative-tests table extended with A8e/A8e2 columns, § 4 manifest-clean assertion bumped 30→34, § 5 round-1 risk #5 (no FC1-N12 in swarm verify path) removed (closed by F4), § 6 Q7.a/b numbers updated, round-2 outcome + round-3 fixes section appended; TRACE_MATRIX stale `run_corr_id` symbol row replaced; "in CI" softened to "via Rust wrapper post-A8e2". FC-trace: meta governance + V3L-27 mitigation gating (FC1-N7). Trust Root manifest 33 → 34 (`tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs`).
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:6381:handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:19:- A8e (post-A7, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-1 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini VETO): six dual-audit fixes. **F1** (Codex#2 + Gemini Q4) `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/run_id.rs` mints one stable run_id per run, threaded into both `emit_event` and `make_pput` (replaces `run_corr_id` vs internal `run_id` ms drift; oneshot also stops using `oneshot_{problem_file}` placeholder). **F2** (Gemini VETO Q6) `scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` adds 15-test conformance battery — round-robin distributes [2,2,2] across 6 calls, single-key pool degrades to all-zero, two-key pool alternates [2,2]; exercises the V3L-27 mitigation without a live API. (A8e2 G1 wires this into `cargo test --workspace` via a Rust shell-out wrapper — see § 2 below.) **F3** (Codex#4) routing matrix bug fixed — `Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct` was misrouting to DashScope because `m.startswith("qwen")` won after the slash check; now slash-form ⇒ siliconflow first, bare-qwen ⇒ dashscope second. **F4** (Codex#3) FC1-N12 emit added to all 3 swarm verify sites (`verify_omega_detailed` × 2 + `verify_partial`), each with verdict + elapsed_ms + path stamp; closes the swarm-side gap in the 5-step compile-loop witness. **F5** (Codex#5) `genesis_payload.toml` header + TRACE_MATRIX milestones reconciled to actual 31 entries (round-1 packet undercounted by 1 — agent_models.rs from A3 was already in TR before A1). **F6** (Codex#1) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2` wording corrected — `p_0=0.10` substitution is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling (max tolerated value for `j-RR ≤ p_0`), NOT the strictest possible substitute as round-1 mistakenly claimed; explicit statistical-implications paragraph added (no Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive; substitution may be less protective than eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`). FC-trace: FC1-N12 (oracle scope swarm coverage) + FC1-N7 (δ provider routing) + Art. V (amendment governance). Trust Root manifest 31 → 33 (run_id.rs + test_llm_proxy.py).

./handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:63:**Q-5. P5 closure (golden fixtures + new tests)**: Hex constants actually load-bearing (assertion fails on diff)? cargo test --lib confirms 17/17 PASS? TerminalSummary in round_trip_all_variants + tx_kind_projection + golden? Cross-variant non-collision pairwise (7×7) or just smoke? BTreeSet permutation test would catch a HashMap-style codec bug?
./handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:65:**Q-6. P6 closure (codec wording)**: spec § 7.1 wording now matches bincode-2.0.1 source citations? Decision to keep u32/u64 (not force u8) — is that the right call given >256 variant ceiling never likely to be hit? Any cascaded impact on shipping CO1.7 LedgerEntrySigningPayload digest stability? (CO1.7 commit a03cc52 used same canonical_encode helper.)
./handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:67:**Q-7. P8 + P9 closure (Q-derived + dual-sign + cold-replay gate)**: Spec § 4.1 commits replay (CO1.7-impl A4) to Q-derived discipline — is this binding enough? Spec § 0.1 cross-atom ordering gate phrased as "MUST NOT ship before CO1.4-extra" — strong constitutional commitment or hedged language?
./handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:73:- Type errors that cargo check missed?
./handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:78:**Q-10. Implementation gating (overall)**: with v1.1 closure, is CO1.7-impl A2 (TypedTx + Sequencer + dispatch_transition) implementable end-to-end against this ABI surface? Specific blockers to call out.
./handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:94:## Top must-fix (if CHALLENGE) — be specific
./handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:97:Be rigorous. Cite spec § + code line numbers. Per memory `feedback_dual_audit_conflict`: do NOT pass on principle; do NOT veto on principle. Real defects = CHALLENGE; foundational design flaw = VETO; clean closure = PASS.
./handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:115:| **P1** | AgentSignature reused 64-byte adapter without domain separation; comments implied "exclude signature" digest with no signing payload | NEW signing-payload structs (`WorkSigningPayload` / `VerifySigningPayload` / `ChallengeSigningPayload` / `FinalizeRewardSigningPayload` / `TaskExpireSigningPayload` / `TerminalSummarySigningPayload`) — each has explicit domain prefix (`b"turingosv4.<actor>.<purpose>.v1"`) prepended to bincode body bytes in `canonical_digest()`. Plus `to_signing_payload()` projection on each tx. | C-1 (Codex Q-E + Gemini Q7) |
./handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:117:| **P3** | `TerminalSummaryTx` was 3-field placeholder living in `system_keypair.rs` (versus STATE § 1.5 8-field schema); locking the wrong shape into ABI | Migrated to `state::typed_tx::TerminalSummaryTx` with full 8-field STATE schema (tx_id / task_id / run_id / run_outcome / total_attempts / failure_class_histogram / last_logical_t / system_signature). `system_keypair` now signs an opaque `TerminalSummarySigning([u8; 32])` digest (same opaque-digest pattern as `LedgerEntrySigning`) — no `bottom_white ↔ state` circular dep. | C-3 (Codex Q-C must-fix-now) |
./handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:118:| **P4** | `TransitionError` had only 10 variants; STATE § 3 pseudocode invokes ~22 | Expanded to 22 variants: SignatureInvalid / StakeInsufficient / TargetWorkTxNotFound / TargetWorkTxNotVerifiable / ParentNotAcceptedYet / AcceptancePredicateFailed(PredicateId) / VerificationPredicateFailed(PredicateId) / SettlementPredicateFailed(PredicateId) / ChallengeWindowClosed / CounterexampleInsufficient / ToolNotInRegistry / ToolCreatorMismatch + 10 prior. Plus `NotYetImplemented` retained as explicit stub sentinel. | CX-1 (Codex Q-G) |
./handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:120:| **P6** | STATE § 2.5 wording wrong vs actual codec — claimed `#[repr(u8)]`-controlled enum discriminants; bincode-2 actually emits u32 BE for variants and u64 BE for lengths | This v1.1 spec § 2.5-bis explicitly documents the actual codec behavior + cross-references bincode-2 source (`bincode 2.0.1 src/features/serde/ser.rs:186`, `enc/impls.rs:68 + :128`). `#[repr(u8)]` is a Rust language attribute that does NOT control serde wire format. Recommendation accepted: keep u32 variants + u64 lengths (no codec change; spec language fixed). | CX-2 (Codex Q-D) |
./handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:122:| **P8** | FinalizeRewardTx had ambiguous {task_id, solver, reward, royalty} provenance + redundant system_signature unclear | This spec § 4 explicitly states {task_id, solver, reward} are **Q-DERIVED at replay** (re-fetched from ClaimsIndex by claim_id; wire fields are ledger summary, NOT trusted from wire); `system_signature` is RETAINED with explicit dual-sign rationale (this sig binds the tx-payload bytes; the L4 `LedgerEntrySigningPayload` sig binds the sequencer-stamped envelope; both are needed). | C-3 + GM-2 |
./handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:131:**Why this atom exists**: spec § 2.5 of `STATE_TRANSITION_SPEC_v1_2026-04-27.md` explicitly deferred "full ABI surface for QState/SignalBundle/TransitionError" to CO1.7. CO1.7 spec § 0 places the per-kind tx schemas in `STATE_TRANSITION_SPEC § 1` ("frozen on paper, not yet in code"). When CO1.7-impl A1 (Git2LedgerWriter, commit `a03cc52`) shipped, downstream A2 (TypedTx + dispatch_transition) discovered ~30 supporting schema types are required but **none of them exist in code** — only `MicroCoin` is defined. This atom defines that ABI surface in isolation under its own dual-audit gate, per the project's per-atom audit principle (CLAUDE.md "Audit Standard").
./handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:149:7. **Money newtype**: `StakeMicroCoin(MicroCoin)` (non-negative invariant enforced at business layer; type-level newtype prevents accidental mix with general `MicroCoin`).
./handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:155:### Out of scope (explicit deferral)
./handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:157:- **MetaTx + ancillaries** (`PredicatePatch`, `ToolPatch`, `JudgeSignature`, `HumanSignature`, `ConstitutionCheckProof`, `ReversibilityPlan`) — STATE spec § 1.6 declares MetaTx is **v4.1 only**; v4 emits `MetaProposalDraft` to L3 CAS, not L4. ⏭ deferred.
./handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:161:- **`SignalBundle` typed shape** — STATE spec uses `SignalBundle::empty()` / `::finalize(...)` / `::task_expired(...)` / `::terminal_summary(...)` constructors. v1 of this atom emits a minimal typed `SignalBundle` (single enum-like discriminator + payload) sufficient for CO1.7-impl to compile; full event-stream design lands in CO1.9 L6 signal indices.
./handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:164:### What this atom is NOT replacing
./handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:171:**Constitutional concern**: CO1.7 LedgerEntry stores typed-tx payloads in L3 CAS via `tx_payload_cid: Cid`. The current shipped `CasStore::open()` initializes an empty in-memory index (CO1.4 store.rs:67); after process restart the CAS bytes are unrecoverable until the index is repopulated. This means **cold-replay of L4 cannot reconstruct typed payloads** — a direct Art. 0.2 (tape canonicality) violation if uncorrected.
./handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:200:**Crate boundary**: `state::typed_tx` consumes (a) `state::q_state` types (Hash, AgentId, TxId, NodeId), (b) `economy::money::MicroCoin` + `StakeMicroCoin`, (c) `bottom_white::cas::schema::Cid`, (d) `bottom_white::ledger::system_keypair::{SystemEpoch, SystemSignature}`. No new outward dependencies; no circular dep risk.
./handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:246:/// FinalizedSlash variant of TxStatus can't accidentally accept arbitrary CIDs.
./handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:256:**Spec gap**: STATE_TRANSITION_SPEC § 3.4 uses `FinalizeTx::from(claim_id, reward)` constructor pattern but provides no explicit struct. CO1.7 spec § 1 lists `TxKind::FinalizeReward = 4` but defers the struct to "frozen in STATE_TRANSITION_SPEC § 1" — which the STATE spec doesn't actually contain.
./handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:289:- L4 `LedgerEntry.system_signature` signs the **sequencer-stamped envelope** (`LedgerEntrySigningPayload.canonical_digest()` via `b"turingosv4.ledger_entry_signing.v1"` — CO1.7 spec § 1.2). Audit-relevant for: "this `(logical_t, parent_ledger_root, tx_payload_cid)` was committed by the sequencer".
./handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:331:The `TxKind` enum already exists in `transition_ledger.rs` with `#[repr(u8)]` and explicit discriminants. `TypedTx::tx_kind()` is the projection used by CO1.7 sequencer apply_one stage 5 (`tx_kind: TxKind::from_typed(&tx)` → renamed `TypedTx::tx_kind(&tx)` for ergonomics).
./handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:345:- **I-CANON-G** (v1.1 NEW per C-1): each agent-signed and system-emitted typed-tx has a paired `*SigningPayload` struct + `canonical_digest()` with explicit domain prefix `b"turingosv4.<actor>.<purpose>.v1"`. Domain prefix bytes are part of the SHA-256 input. 6 distinct domains (work / verify / challenge agent + finalize_reward / task_expire / terminal_summary system) yield pairwise-distinct digests.
./handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:354:| `Strings serialized as UTF-8 with explicit length prefix u32-BE` | **u64 BE** length prefix (bincode encodes `usize` as u64 under `with_fixed_int_encoding`; [src/enc/impls.rs:128](https://docs.rs/bincode/2.0.1/src/bincode/enc/impls.rs.html)). The same applies to BTreeMap / BTreeSet / Vec lengths. |
./handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:357:**v1.1 decision**: keep u32 variants + u64 lengths; do NOT introduce a custom serde adapter to force u8 discriminants (which would force re-encoding of all existing fixtures + complicate forward-compat for >256 variants). The locked golden fixtures in `EXPECTED_HEX_*` reflect the actual u32/u64 codec.
./handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:386:| **D-2** | § 3.4 `FinalizeTx::from(claim_id, reward)` opaque constructor | **explicit `FinalizeRewardTx` struct** with Q-derived field discipline (§ 4.1) + dual-sign rationale (§ 4.2) | spec gap; derived schema. |
./handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:408:- **Spec rounds**: 1-2 expected. The bulk is mechanical transcription; § 4 (FinalizeRewardTx derivation) + § 5 D-1 (TxStatus elision) are the only design decisions auditors are likely to test.
./handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:415:## § 12 What this spec does NOT specify

./handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:2782:| **C-1** | **Agent-signature domain separation** | Q-E: `AgentSignature` has no signed-payload struct + no domain prefix; comments imply an "exclude signature" digest without a signing payload — impossible | Q7: type-level distinction insufficient; agent-signed payloads MUST add unique domain separators (`v4.agent_sig.work` vs `v4.agent_sig.verify` vs `v4.agent_sig.challenge`) to prevent type confusion attacks |
./handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:2783:| **C-2** | **Lock golden fixture hex + expand test coverage** | Q-J: golden tests only assert length=64 + self-stability; don't lock hex; `TypedTx::TerminalSummary` excluded from round-trip / kind / golden tests | Q9: "phase 1 record-only" insufficient for an ABI-defining atom; must hardcode SHA-256 hex; add cross-variant non-collision + BTreeMap permutation independence + zero-value default tests |
./handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:2784:| **C-3** | **Schema parity (TerminalSummary 8-field + claim-id newtype + signing-payload structs)** | Q-C must-fix-now: STATE § 1.5 has 8 fields; shipped 3-field placeholder is exactly the ABI being frozen; future additions are decode-breaking. Move tx schema OUT of `system_keypair.rs` into `state::typed_tx`; system_keypair signs opaque digest. Q-B: `claim_id: TxId` should be `claim_id: ClaimId` newtype (STATE speaks in ClaimId; finalization order is by claim_id). | Q6: FinalizeRewardTx system_signature might be REDUNDANT given LedgerEntrySigningPayload signs envelope; spec must explicitly distinguish "agent-to-runtime sign" from "runtime-to-ledger sign" or DROP the field; claim_id should be typed ClaimId not reused TxId |
./handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:2790:| ID | Item | Codex citation |
./handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:2792:| **CX-1** | **TransitionError taxonomy incomplete** | Q-G: missing `SignatureInvalid`, `StakeInsufficient`, predicate failures, target-not-found/not-verifiable, challenge-window-closed, counterexample-insufficient, tool errors, parent-not-accepted etc. STATE § 3 pseudocode raises these; code v1 lists only 10 stub variants. `NotYetImplemented` is acceptable as transition-stage sentinel but not as production error. |
./handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:2798:| ID | Item | Gemini citation |
./handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:2800:| **GM-1** | **Art 0.2 cold-replay constitutional commitment** | Q4: payload data lives in CAS (referenced via `tx_payload_cid: Cid`); CO1.7 spec § 0 already deferred CAS index persistence to **CO1.4-extra**. Until that ships, cold-restart loses payload data → tape is non-canonical → Art 0.2 violation. v1.1 of CO1.1.4-pre1 must explicitly gate PASS on a concrete commitment to ship CO1.4-extra alongside or before CO1.7-impl A4 (replay_full_transition). Strategic risk: shipping a constitutionally-non-compliant tape layer, even temporarily, is too high. |
./handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:2807:- **Codex Q-H (HasSubmitter correctness)**: PASS. Work/Verify/Challenge delegate to actual submitter; Reuse returns None correctly (creator is royalty recipient, not submitter). Outer delegation correct.
./handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:2809:- **Gemini Q1-Q3, Q5, Q8**: implicit PASS (not in must-fix list). Constitutional alignment, Inv 3 interaction, v4/v4.1 boundary preservation, reconstructibility-via-Q-derivation, forward sustainability all OK.
./handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:2820:| **P2**: add `ClaimId(pub TxId)` newtype (spec § 4 + typed_tx.rs); update FinalizeRewardTx.claim_id; specify in spec § 4 that {task_id, solver, reward, royalty edges} are Q-derived at replay (NOT trusted from wire) | C-3 partial (claim-id) | typed_tx.rs + spec § 4 |
./handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:2822:| **P4**: complete `TransitionError` taxonomy with all variants invoked in STATE § 3 pseudocode (~12-15 additional variants); keep `NotYetImplemented` as explicit stub sentinel | CX-1 | typed_tx.rs |
./handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:2824:| **P6**: spec patch — STATE § 2.5 wording drift (variant index = u32 BE, lengths = u64 BE; `#[repr(u8)]` does NOT control wire format) — either fix the spec OR add a custom serde adapter forcing u8 discriminants. **Decision required**: cheap (spec patch) vs expensive (codec change forces re-encode of all existing fixtures). Given v1 is brand-new, recommend spec patch + accept u32/u64 sizing. | CX-2 | spec § 2.5 OR new serde adapter |
./handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:2826:| **P8**: spec § 5 add explicit Q-derived note for FinalizeRewardTx fields {task_id, solver, reward, royalty}; spec § 4 commit to making LedgerEntrySigningPayload the SOLE signing point for FinalizeRewardTx (drop FinalizeRewardTx.system_signature OR clarify dual-sign rationale) | C-3 + GM-2 | spec § 4 + § 5 |
./handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:2827:| **P9**: spec patch — § 0 add explicit "Art 0.2 cold-replay gate": v1.1 PASS contingent on CO1.4-extra commitment (explicit cross-atom dependency; CO1.7-impl A4 must NOT ship before CO1.4-extra) | GM-1 | spec § 0 |
./handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:2864:4. **Domain separation is non-negotiable when a 64-byte signature is reused for distinct semantic roles** (agent vs system; work vs verify vs challenge). Type-level distinction at the API surface is necessary but not sufficient; the canonical_digest pre-image must encode the role. Sedimented: when introducing a new signature primitive, the canonical_digest spec must include a stable role-prefix byte string (`b"v4.<actor>.<purpose>.v1"`).
./handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:2866:5. **Cold-replay → Art 0.2 commitment is a real cross-atom gate**: declaring "CO1.4-extra is a separate atom" is not enough; the deferred dependency creates a window where the tape is non-canonical. Sedimented: v1.1 must explicitly state the cross-atom ordering constraint (CO1.4-extra MUST ship before CO1.7-impl A4 replay_full_transition) and PASS is contingent on that ordering being honored.
./handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:2875:# State Transition Specification v1.4

./handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:3585:**v1.3 fix**: split runtime side effects (signing, logical time assignment) from pure transition. Runtime constructs `TaskExpireTx` BEFORE entering pure transition; pure `task_expire_transition` takes already-signed tx as argument. This restores § 2 + § 3 pure-boundary discipline (Codex Q1.3 + new-issue #1 fix).
./handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:3661:### 3.6.5 Agent Implicit Init (v1.3 fixed: trait-based submitter resolution per Codex re-audit Q1.4)
./handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:3665:**v1.3 fix**: WorkTx has `agent_id`; VerifyTx has `verifier_agent`; ChallengeTx has `challenger_agent`; ReuseTx has no submitting-agent field (it's a fact-tx). Introduce a `Tx::submitter_id() -> Option<AgentId>` trait method that each tx implements explicitly:
./handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:3678:fn implicit_init_agent_if_new(q_next: &mut QState, tx: &impl HasSubmitter) {
./handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:3691:**Rule**: each transition function MUST call `implicit_init_agent_if_new(&mut q_next, tx)` as the FIRST statement of stage 4 (after stage 3 predicate gate, before any user-state mutation). For `ReuseTx`, `submitter_id()` returns None; no init happens; that's intentional (ReuseTx has no submitting agent to init).
./handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:3693:**Why implicit (not explicit `register_agent_transition`)**:
./handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:3694:- Satoshi parallel: Bitcoin addresses are implicitly created at first use; no separate register step
./handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:3697:- v4.1+: if needed, can add explicit `agent_register_tx` later WITHOUT breaking implicit-init (new tx is purely additive)
./handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:3746:| I-STAKE | tx.stake ≤ q.balances_t[tx.agent_id]; debit atomic | stage 3, 6 | `tests/stake_atomicity.rs` |

./handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:3801:If CO P1 audit demands stronger guarantees, the TLA+ model is upgraded to a full PlusCal program with TLC model checking. For v4 scope, the type-level + conformance-test combination is deemed sufficient by Codex.
./handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:3811:| 11.1 | False-challenge reputation penalty undefined | **v1.2 (Codex Q10 fix)**: false_challenge_reputation_penalty is **fixed to 0 in v4** (NOT configurable). Pseudocode resolves contradiction: challenge_transition stage 3 returns `Err(CounterexampleInsufficient)` BEFORE any state mutation; no executable path for nonzero penalty → "configurable" prose retired. v4.1+ MAY add explicit `failed_challenge_penalty_transition` if needed. | NO (v4) |
./handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:3822:**Why**: spec § 3 pseudocode is single-threaded; CO P1 may parallelize Phase C 5 modes × N seeds. Without explicit serialization rule, two work_tx can race on same parent_state_root, both pass `I-PARENT`, but produce different (logical_t, tx_id) sequences across STEP_B branches → state_root divergence.

Plus `to_signing_payload()` projection on each tx. | C-1 (Codex Q-E + Gemini Q7) |
./handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:5072:handover/specs/CO1_1_4_PRE1_TYPED_TX_ABI_v1_2026-04-28.md:14:| **P3** | `TerminalSummaryTx` was 3-field placeholder living in `system_keypair.rs` (versus STATE § 1.5 8-field schema); locking the wrong shape into ABI | Migrated to `state::typed_tx::TerminalSummaryTx` with full 8-field STATE schema (tx_id / task_id / run_id / run_outcome / total_attempts / failure_class_histogram / last_logical_t / system_signature). `system_keypair` now signs an opaque `TerminalSummarySigning([u8; 32])` digest (same opaque-digest pattern as `LedgerEntrySigning`) — no `bottom_white ↔ state` circular dep. | C-3 (Codex Q-C must-fix-now) |
./handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:5073:handover/specs/CO1_1_4_PRE1_TYPED_TX_ABI_v1_2026-04-28.md:15:| **P4** | `TransitionError` had only 10 variants; STATE § 3 pseudocode invokes ~22 | Expanded to 22 variants: SignatureInvalid / StakeInsufficient / TargetWorkTxNotFound / TargetWorkTxNotVerifiable / ParentNotAcceptedYet / AcceptancePredicateFailed(PredicateId) / VerificationPredicateFailed(PredicateId) / SettlementPredicateFailed(PredicateId) / ChallengeWindowClosed / CounterexampleInsufficient / ToolNotInRegistry / ToolCreatorMismatch + 10 prior. Plus `NotYetImplemented` retained as explicit stub sentinel. | CX-1 (Codex Q-G) |
./handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:5076:handover/specs/CO1_1_4_PRE1_TYPED_TX_ABI_v1_2026-04-28.md:28:**Why this atom exists**: spec § 2.5 of `STATE_TRANSITION_SPEC_v1_2026-04-27.md` explicitly deferred "full ABI surface for QState/SignalBundle/TransitionError" to CO1.7. CO1.7 spec § 0 places the per-kind tx schemas in `STATE_TRANSITION_SPEC § 1` ("frozen on paper, not yet in code"). When CO1.7-impl A1 (Git2LedgerWriter, commit `a03cc52`) shipped, downstream A2 (TypedTx + dispatch_transition) discovered ~30 supporting schema types are required but **none of them exist in code** — only `MicroCoin` is defined. This atom defines that ABI surface in isolation under its own dual-audit gate, per the project's per-atom audit principle (CLAUDE.md "Audit Standard").
./handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:5086:handover/specs/CO1_1_4_PRE1_TYPED_TX_ABI_v1_2026-04-28.md:283:| **D-2** | § 3.4 `FinalizeTx::from(claim_id, reward)` opaque constructor | **explicit `FinalizeRewardTx` struct** with Q-derived field discipline (§ 4.1) + dual-sign rationale (§ 4.2) | spec gap; derived schema. |
./handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:5095:handover/specs/STATE_TRANSITION_SPEC_v1_2026-04-27.md:270:| `self.graveyard: HashMap<String, Vec<String>>` | `bus.rs:48` | **ILLEGAL sidecar** (Art. 0.2 explicitly anti-patterned) | retire; replace with `RejectedAttemptSummary` stamped on next accepted tx + `TerminalSummaryTx` |
./handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:5097:handover/specs/STATE_TRANSITION_SPEC_v1_2026-04-27.md:324:**Out of scope for v1.x spec** (deferred per Codex Q5/NEW-5 round-3 PARTIAL acknowledgment): full golden fixture corpus + differential fuzzing seed + complete runner ABI for QState/SignalBundle/TransitionError. v1.4 freezes the SERIALIZATION RULE (bincode v2 big-endian + BTreeMap lex); fixtures + ABI land in **CO1.1.4-pre1** (canonical fixture corpus) + **CO1.7** (full ABI surface). This is an **explicit deferral** — not unresolved spec ambiguity. STEP_B branch A and branch B both implement the SAME bincode rule; per-tx digest matching is mechanical from v1.4. Full corpus generation is a downstream code task, not spec scope.

Plus `to_signing_payload()` projection on each tx. | C-1 (Codex Q-E + Gemini Q7) |
./handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:7474:    14	| **P3** | `TerminalSummaryTx` was 3-field placeholder living in `system_keypair.rs` (versus STATE § 1.5 8-field schema); locking the wrong shape into ABI | Migrated to `state::typed_tx::TerminalSummaryTx` with full 8-field STATE schema (tx_id / task_id / run_id / run_outcome / total_attempts / failure_class_histogram / last_logical_t / system_signature). `system_keypair` now signs an opaque `TerminalSummarySigning([u8; 32])` digest (same opaque-digest pattern as `LedgerEntrySigning`) — no `bottom_white ↔ state` circular dep. | C-3 (Codex Q-C must-fix-now) |
./handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:7475:    15	| **P4** | `TransitionError` had only 10 variants; STATE § 3 pseudocode invokes ~22 | Expanded to 22 variants: SignatureInvalid / StakeInsufficient / TargetWorkTxNotFound / TargetWorkTxNotVerifiable / ParentNotAcceptedYet / AcceptancePredicateFailed(PredicateId) / VerificationPredicateFailed(PredicateId) / SettlementPredicateFailed(PredicateId) / ChallengeWindowClosed / CounterexampleInsufficient / ToolNotInRegistry / ToolCreatorMismatch + 10 prior. Plus `NotYetImplemented` retained as explicit stub sentinel. | CX-1 (Codex Q-G) |
./handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:7477:    17	| **P6** | STATE § 2.5 wording wrong vs actual codec — claimed `#[repr(u8)]`-controlled enum discriminants; bincode-2 actually emits u32 BE for variants and u64 BE for lengths | This v1.1 spec § 2.5-bis explicitly documents the actual codec behavior + cross-references bincode-2 source (`bincode 2.0.1 src/features/serde/ser.rs:186`, `enc/impls.rs:68 + :128`). `#[repr(u8)]` is a Rust language attribute that does NOT control serde wire format. Recommendation accepted: keep u32 variants + u64 lengths (no codec change; spec language fixed). | CX-2 (Codex Q-D) |
./handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:7479:    19	| **P8** | FinalizeRewardTx had ambiguous {task_id, solver, reward, royalty} provenance + redundant system_signature unclear | This spec § 4 explicitly states {task_id, solver, reward} are **Q-DERIVED at replay** (re-fetched from ClaimsIndex by claim_id; wire fields are ledger summary, NOT trusted from wire); `system_signature` is RETAINED with explicit dual-sign rationale (this sig binds the tx-payload bytes; the L4 `LedgerEntrySigningPayload` sig binds the sequencer-stamped envelope; both are needed). | C-3 + GM-2 |
./handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:7488:    28	**Why this atom exists**: spec § 2.5 of `STATE_TRANSITION_SPEC_v1_2026-04-27.md` explicitly deferred "full ABI surface for QState/SignalBundle/TransitionError" to CO1.7. CO1.7 spec § 0 places the per-kind tx schemas in `STATE_TRANSITION_SPEC § 1` ("frozen on paper, not yet in code"). When CO1.7-impl A1 (Git2LedgerWriter, commit `a03cc52`) shipped, downstream A2 (TypedTx + dispatch_transition) discovered ~30 supporting schema types are required but **none of them exist in code** — only `MicroCoin` is defined. This atom defines that ABI surface in isolation under its own dual-audit gate, per the project's per-atom audit principle (CLAUDE.md "Audit Standard").
./handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:7506:    46	7. **Money newtype**: `StakeMicroCoin(MicroCoin)` (non-negative invariant enforced at business layer; type-level newtype prevents accidental mix with general `MicroCoin`).
./handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:7512:    52	### Out of scope (explicit deferral)
./handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:7514:    54	- **MetaTx + ancillaries** (`PredicatePatch`, `ToolPatch`, `JudgeSignature`, `HumanSignature`, `ConstitutionCheckProof`, `ReversibilityPlan`) — STATE spec § 1.6 declares MetaTx is **v4.1 only**; v4 emits `MetaProposalDraft` to L3 CAS, not L4. ⏭ deferred.
./handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:7518:    58	- **`SignalBundle` typed shape** — STATE spec uses `SignalBundle::empty()` / `::finalize(...)` / `::task_expired(...)` / `::terminal_summary(...)` constructors. v1 of this atom emits a minimal typed `SignalBundle` (single enum-like discriminator + payload) sufficient for CO1.7-impl to compile; full event-stream design lands in CO1.9 L6 signal indices.
./handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:7521:    61	### What this atom is NOT replacing
./handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:7528:    68	**Constitutional concern**: CO1.7 LedgerEntry stores typed-tx payloads in L3 CAS via `tx_payload_cid: Cid`. The current shipped `CasStore::open()` initializes an empty in-memory index (CO1.4 store.rs:67); after process restart the CAS bytes are unrecoverable until the index is repopulated. This means **cold-replay of L4 cannot reconstruct typed payloads** — a direct Art. 0.2 (tape canonicality) violation if uncorrected.
./handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:7557:    97	**Crate boundary**: `state::typed_tx` consumes (a) `state::q_state` types (Hash, AgentId, TxId, NodeId), (b) `economy::money::MicroCoin` + `StakeMicroCoin`, (c) `bottom_white::cas::schema::Cid`, (d) `bottom_white::ledger::system_keypair::{SystemEpoch, SystemSignature}`. No new outward dependencies; no circular dep risk.
./handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:7612:   186	- L4 `LedgerEntry.system_signature` signs the **sequencer-stamped envelope** (`LedgerEntrySigningPayload.canonical_digest()` via `b"turingosv4.ledger_entry_signing.v1"` — CO1.7 spec § 1.2). Audit-relevant for: "this `(logical_t, parent_ledger_root, tx_payload_cid)` was committed by the sequencer".
./handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:7637:   242	- **I-CANON-G** (v1.1 NEW per C-1): each agent-signed and system-emitted typed-tx has a paired `*SigningPayload` struct + `canonical_digest()` with explicit domain prefix `b"turingosv4.<actor>.<purpose>.v1"`. Domain prefix bytes are part of the SHA-256 input. 6 distinct domains (work / verify / challenge agent + finalize_reward / task_expire / terminal_summary system) yield pairwise-distinct digests.
./handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:7646:   251	| `Strings serialized as UTF-8 with explicit length prefix u32-BE` | **u64 BE** length prefix (bincode encodes `usize` as u64 under `with_fixed_int_encoding`; [src/enc/impls.rs:128](https://docs.rs/bincode/2.0.1/src/bincode/enc/impls.rs.html)). The same applies to BTreeMap / BTreeSet / Vec lengths. |
./handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:7649:   254	**v1.1 decision**: keep u32 variants + u64 lengths; do NOT introduce a custom serde adapter to force u8 discriminants (which would force re-encoding of all existing fixtures + complicate forward-compat for >256 variants). The locked golden fixtures in `EXPECTED_HEX_*` reflect the actual u32/u64 codec.
./handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:7678:   283	| **D-2** | § 3.4 `FinalizeTx::from(claim_id, reward)` opaque constructor | **explicit `FinalizeRewardTx` struct** with Q-derived field discipline (§ 4.1) + dual-sign rationale (§ 4.2) | spec gap; derived schema. |
./handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:7722:   324	**Out of scope for v1.x spec** (deferred per Codex Q5/NEW-5 round-3 PARTIAL acknowledgment): full golden fixture corpus + differential fuzzing seed + complete runner ABI for QState/SignalBundle/TransitionError. v1.4 freezes the SERIALIZATION RULE (bincode v2 big-endian + BTreeMap lex); fixtures + ABI land in **CO1.1.4-pre1** (canonical fixture corpus) + **CO1.7** (full ABI surface). This is an **explicit deferral** — not unresolved spec ambiguity. STEP_B branch A and branch B both implement the SAME bincode rule; per-tx digest matching is mechanical from v1.4. Full corpus generation is a downstream code task, not spec scope.

./handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:7905:handover/specs/CO1_1_4_PRE1_TYPED_TX_ABI_v1_2026-04-28.md:19:| **P8** | FinalizeRewardTx had ambiguous {task_id, solver, reward, royalty} provenance + redundant system_signature unclear | This spec § 4 explicitly states {task_id, solver, reward} are **Q-DERIVED at replay** (re-fetched from ClaimsIndex by claim_id; wire fields are ledger summary, NOT trusted from wire); `system_signature` is RETAINED with explicit dual-sign rationale (this sig binds the tx-payload bytes; the L4 `LedgerEntrySigningPayload` sig binds the sequencer-stamped envelope; both are needed). | C-3 + GM-2 |

./handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:9173:Payloads are acceptable for an ABI skeleton if richer rejection context is logged elsewhere, but one rule should be made explicit later: when a predicate bundle has multiple failures, transition code must choose/report predicate IDs deterministically. ./handover/audits/GEMINI_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R11.md:14:My audit confirms that the core code artifacts for Phase A are sound and the specific questions posed to the auditor (Q1-Q5) are answered satisfactorily by the current state. The fixes for all prior-round findings, including the substantive bugs from rounds R1, R3, R6, R7, R8, and R10, appear correct and are pinned by conformance tests where appropriate.
./handover/audits/GEMINI_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R11.md:16:However, the audit also reveals that the process discipline established by the A8e7 split has not been applied consistently across all governance artifacts, and a minor code-quality issue persists. These findings, while not blocking the technical work of Phase B, represent a failure to fully adhere to the project's own hard-won governance precedents.
./handover/audits/GEMINI_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R11.md:20:1.  **[CHALLENGE]** The header of `TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md` contains extensive historical lineage detailing the changes made in each audit-fix commit (e.g., "A8e11 (post-A8e10, FIX-THEN-PROCEED...): two substantive gate-machinery hardenings..."). This violates the central principle of the A8e7 structural split, which mandates that "current state" documents (like the TRACE_MATRIX) describe WHAT IS, while the `A8_AUDIT_HISTORY` document describes HOW it got there. This recurring pattern of documentary drift was the primary driver of CHALLENGE verdicts in rounds 2-6 and its persistence in a core governance artifact is a significant process failure.
./handover/audits/GEMINI_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R11.md:28:3.  **[CHALLENGE]** The signature of the `make_pput` function in the evaluator uses `Option` for parameters (`total_run_token_count`, `failed_branch_count`, `total_wall_time_ms`) that are non-optional in the `PputResult` struct it constructs. All call sites within `evaluator.rs` provide `Some(...)` values, and the function body immediately unwraps them with a default. This indicates an incomplete refactoring. While not a runtime bug, it creates an unnecessary divergence between the function's contract and its actual usage, reducing code clarity and maintainability. The signature should be changed to require the non-optional types directly.
./handover/audits/GEMINI_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R13.md:12:This audit has reviewed the Phase A → B Exit Audit Packet (A8) and its companion history document. The review was conducted from the mandated perspective of a skeptical, independent, and adversarial auditor. The following sections detail the verification of the specific questions posed in the packet, followed by a final verdict.
./handover/audits/GEMINI_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R13.md:18:-   **(Q1.c) `llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs` fail-closed**: **PASS**. The source code for `experiments/minif2f_v4/tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs` (appended) contains an explicit `assert!` (line 60) that checks for the presence of `python3` on the system PATH before attempting to run the conformance suite. The panic message is descriptive and directs the user to either install the dependency or use the explicit `SKIP_LLM_PROXY_PYTHON_CONFORMANCE=1` opt-out. This correctly implements the fail-closed behavior mandated by the A8e3 fix H6.
./handover/audits/GEMINI_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R13.md:23:-   **(Q2.b) Re-calibration conditions**: **PASS**. The framing in `PREREG_AMENDMENT...md` § 3 correctly presents the five conditions as pre-requisites for calibration to run, not as guarantees of completion before any specific phase. This is consistent with the operative rule in § 8, which allows Phase E to proceed with the ceiling substitution if the conditions are not met. The language avoids making a false promise, a flaw corrected by the A8e8 fix M4.
./handover/audits/GEMINI_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R13.md:26:### Q3 — Atomicity, FC-trace discipline, governance
./handover/audits/GEMINI_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R13.md:28:-   **(Q3.a) Commits**: **PASS**. The packet (§ 1) asserts that each atom A1–A7 was a single atomic commit with an `FC-trace` tag and that none touched `constitution.md`. While the git log cannot be verified from the packet alone, the existence of the `judge.sh` pre-commit hook (R-016), which is itself in the Trust Root, provides strong process evidence that this discipline is enforced.
./handover/audits/GEMINI_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R13.md:30:-   **(Q3.c) Trust Root manifest**: **PASS**. The manifest has grown to 38 entries, and the audit history shows a methodical and justified expansion. The A8e13 fix Q1, which enforces recursive verification of child manifests (e.g., `cases/MANIFEST.sha256`), directly addresses the concern of whether all entries are load-bearing. Tampering with a child file (like a specific case YAML) without regenerating the parent manifest now correctly triggers a boot failure. The Trust Root gate is demonstrably strong.
./handover/audits/GEMINI_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R13.md:32:### Q4 — Phase A → B exit decision
./handover/audits/GEMINI_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R13.md:34:-   **(Q4.a-c) Spot-checks**: **PASS**. The packet reports PASS for all three automated checks (`cargo test --workspace`, `python3 scripts/test_llm_proxy.py`, `bash scripts/smoke_siliconflow.sh`). The reported metrics (267 PASS / 29 ignored / 0 failed; 16/16 PASS; 3/3 keys PASS) are accepted as correct.
./handover/audits/GEMINI_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R13.md:41:-   **(Q5.b) History append-only**: **PASS**. The structure and explicit mandate of the history document confirm its append-only nature.
./handover/audits/GEMINI_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R13.md:48:All specific questions posed to this auditor have been answered in the affirmative. The code, documentation, and governance artifacts are internally consistent and aligned with the pre-registration contracts. The groundwork for Phase B is complete and robust. There are no open findings.
./handover/audits/EXT_GEMINI_PHASE_8_R1R8_2026-04-22.md:22:**PASS** — `prompt.rs::build_agent_prompt` now includes the `own_reputation` parameter and renders it as "Reputation: N citations"; `evaluator.rs` correctly passes the value.
./handover/audits/EXT_GEMINI_PHASE_8_R1R8_2026-04-22.md:25:**PASS** — The doc comment on `halt_with_reason` now explicitly and clearly explains the intentional semantic split between the live `q_state` (latest reason) and the durable ledger event (first reason).
./handover/audits/EXT_GEMINI_PHASE_8_R1R8_2026-04-22.md:28:**PASS** — `src/sdk/predicate.rs` now has `#[allow(dead_code)]` on the `Predicate` trait, along with a comment justifying it as M-1 preservation, silencing the warning.
./handover/audits/GEMINI_PAPER1_V2_AUDIT_2026-04-24.md:8:Here is a structured critique of the Paper 1 v2 draft, adhering to the specified role and format.
./handover/audits/GEMINI_PAPER1_V2_AUDIT_2026-04-24.md:14:This review assesses the v2 draft's resilience to peer-review scrutiny. While the revisions from v1 represent a significant methodological improvement—particularly the pre-registration, N=4 ablation, and honest disclosure of the proxy-saturation incident—several critical issues remain that would likely prevent acceptance at a top-tier systems/reliability venue.
./handover/audits/GEMINI_PAPER1_V2_AUDIT_2026-04-24.md:18:The statistical discipline is much improved, but a key inconsistency in the multiplicity correction remains.
./handover/audits/GEMINI_PAPER1_V2_AUDIT_2026-04-24.md:20:*   **Inconsistent Family-of-Tests Definition**: The pre-registration document (`PREREG_E1V2...`) defines the family for Bonferroni correction as size=4, listing: (1) hard-set A vs B, (2) ablation vs B, (3) easy-set Δ, and (4) per-seed containment. However, the paper's Results (§ 4) reports p-values for three distinct McNemar tests on the hard-set data: (1) B vs A, (2) Ablation vs A, and (3) B vs Ablation. The "Ablation vs A" test, which yields the borderline p=0.0156 result (§ 4.2.1), is not explicitly enumerated in the pre-registered family. A reviewer would flag this as post-hoc hypothesis testing. The author must either (a) state that the pre-registered family was size=3 (A-B, Abl-A, B-Abl) and re-calculate α=0.05/3=0.0167, which would change the interpretation of the Abl-A result, or (b) justify why the Abl-A test was performed but not included in the pre-registered multiplicity plan.
./handover/audits/GEMINI_PAPER1_V2_AUDIT_2026-04-24.md:21:*   **Low Power for Secondary Claims**: The paper correctly concludes that the Meta-Planner's contribution is not statistically distinguishable (p=0.34). However, with only 6 discordant pairs (4 vs 2), the experiment had very low power to detect anything but a large effect. The minimum number of discordant pairs needed to clear α=0.0125 in one direction (e.g., 7 vs 0) was not achieved. This is a limitation of N=40, and while acknowledged, it means the claim that the Meta-Planner is "unresolved" is weak; the experiment was not designed with sufficient power to resolve it in the first place.
./handover/audits/GEMINI_PAPER1_V2_AUDIT_2026-04-24.md:34:*   **Meta-Cognitive Confound**: The paper commendably flags the "prompt leakage caveat" (§ 6.3) where the Meta-Planner prompt is meta-cognitive, unlike the other three tactic-list prompts. This is more than a "leakage" issue; it's a fundamental confound. The experiment cannot distinguish between "gains from heterogeneity of 4 peer skills" and "gains from adding one meta-cognitive prompt to a pool of 3 object-level skills." The conclusion that the effect is from "generic prompt heterogeneity" (§ Abstract) is therefore imprecise. A more accurate, though weaker, conclusion would be "gains from a portfolio of prompts including one with meta-cognitive instructions." The current design cannot justify the stronger "generic" claim.
./handover/audits/GEMINI_PAPER1_V2_AUDIT_2026-04-24.md:35:*   **Mechanism of Heterogeneity**: The paper correctly refutes the Meta-Planner as *the* mechanism but offers little insight into the alternative. The claim that heterogeneity "collectively cover[s] more of the tactic space" (§ 1.2) is asserted but not strongly evidenced. The tactic-composition analysis (§ 5.2) is descriptive but shallow. A stronger analysis would show which specific tactics from which specific skill prompts were essential for the B-unique solves.
./handover/audits/GEMINI_PAPER1_V2_AUDIT_2026-04-24.md:44:### 5. Reproducibility challenges (REPRO)
./handover/audits/GEMINI_PAPER1_V2_AUDIT_2026-04-24.md:48:*   **Honest but Unplanned Deviation**: The proxy-saturation incident (§ 3.5) was handled with impressive transparency. However, it remains a deviation from the (unspecified) execution plan in the pre-registration. The pre-reg did not specify a concurrency policy, which was an omission. While the serial re-run was the correct scientific choice, it highlights that the pre-registration was incomplete.
./handover/audits/GEMINI_PAPER1_V2_AUDIT_2026-04-24.md:49:*   **Deferred Analysis**: Key analyses are "deferred to Appendix C" which is incomplete (§ 4.5, § 4.6). Specifically, the node-count distribution and winning-agent distribution are critical for interpreting the "multi-agent" aspect of the work. A complete paper submitted for review must include this data, as it directly impacts the interpretation of whether solves are single-agent discoveries or multi-agent collaborations.
./handover/audits/GEMINI_PAPER1_V2_AUDIT_2026-04-24.md:56:*   **"Portfolio Effect" Overreach**: While a good framing, calling it a "portfolio effect" (§ Abstract, § 1.2) implies a known mechanism from finance (diversification reducing risk/increasing returns). The paper demonstrates a performance gain from diversity but doesn't provide enough evidence to confirm the mechanism is analogous to a financial portfolio (e.g., by showing that different skills excel on different problem types, reducing the risk of failure on any single type). The term is suggestive but not fully justified by the analysis provided.
./handover/audits/GEMINI_PAPER1_V2_AUDIT_2026-04-24.md:64:The paper is a model of methodological improvement and transparency, but the discovery of the `mathd_algebra_246` anomaly fundamentally compromises the "hard-set" evaluation and inflates the headline result. This, combined with the unresolved multiplicity definition and the meta-cognitive confound, means the work is not yet ready for publication.
./handover/audits/GEMINI_PAPER1_V2_AUDIT_2026-04-24.md:72:| **P1** | CAUSE | Re-scope the primary claim from "generic heterogeneity" to "heterogeneity including a meta-cognitive prompt". | The current design cannot disentangle the effect of diversity from the effect of adding a higher-level instruction, so the claim must be more precise. |
./handover/audits/GEMINI_PAPER1_V2_AUDIT_2026-04-24.md:80:2.  **Reconcile Pre-Registration with Analysis**: The author must provide a single, consistent definition for the family of hypotheses under multiplicity correction and justify it based on the pre-registration document. The p-value for the "Ablation vs A" test must be interpreted under this corrected framework.
./handover/audits/GEMINI_PAPER1_V2_AUDIT_2026-04-24.md:83:### Specific Claim to Cut
./handover/audits/EXT_GEMINI_PHASE_8_BATCH_2026-04-22.md:48:- **`test_decide_tactic_permitted` 替换**: **PASS**.
./handover/audits/EXT_GEMINI_PHASE_8_BATCH_2026-04-22.md:50:  - **分析**: 这是“正确更新语义”。旧测试断言 `decide` 被允许，这与 C-011 的完整精神相悖。新测试 `test_bare_decide_forbidden` 和 `test_bare_omega_forbidden` 精确地测试了 C-050 判例所规定的新规则：禁止裸调用，但允许合法的、带命名空间限定的 Mathlib 用法。这是在修复一个测试本身的 bug，而不是隐藏一个产品 bug。
./handover/audits/EXT_GEMINI_PHASE_8_BATCH_2026-04-22.md:63:    1.  **字符串误杀**: **是**。如果一个 Lean 证明包含一个字符串字面量，如 `have h_str : String := "decide the fate"`, `has_bare_tactic_invocation` 会错误地将其中的 `"decide"` 识别为一个裸策略调用并拒绝。因为 `before` 字符是 `"`，它不是字母数字或 `_`，也不是 `.` 或 `:`。这是一个需要修复的边界情况。
./handover/audits/EXT_GEMINI_PHASE_8_BATCH_2026-04-22.md:64:    2.  **Unicode identifier**: 实现使用了 `.is_ascii_alphanumeric()`，这对于允许 UTF-8 标识符的 Lean4 来说是不够鲁棒的。例如，一个合法的限定调用 `Mathlib.αβγ.decide`，如果 `γ` 是一个多字节字符，`text.as_bytes()[i - 1]` 可能会索引到字符的中间，导致不可预测的行为。
./handover/audits/EXT_GEMINI_PHASE_8_BATCH_2026-04-22.md:70:- **8.F `reputation_by_author` 对 self-citation 计数**: **PASS**.
./handover/audits/EXT_GEMINI_PHASE_8_BATCH_2026-04-22.md:71:  - **证据**: `tests/reputation.rs::reputation_self_citation_counts`
./handover/audits/EXT_GEMINI_PHASE_8_BATCH_2026-04-22.md:133:2.  **Test Sufficiency (WAL Replay)**: 在 `TuringBus::from_wal` 或其调用路径中，增加逻辑：在重放事件后，检查最后一个事件是否为 `Halt`，并相应地设置 `bus.q_state`。同时，在 `tests/wal_resume.rs` 中增加一个测试用例来验证这一点。

./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R13.md:570:4. (**Gemini R12 — substantive run_swarm bug**) When an agent's `search_count >= search_cap` (default 20), the search tool is correctly stripped from `tools_desc` (line 870), but `search_cache[agent_id]` is not cleared. The cached results from the agent's last successful search keep being injected into every subsequent prompt's `hits_ref` (line 869) for the rest of the run. The agent reasons from stale data forever; uncontrolled experimental variable that would confound any swarm experiment that exercises the cap.
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R13.md:596:Cumulative cost so far ~$70 (12 rounds × ~$5–7 — note A8e12 was a no-audit harness amplifier between R10 and R11); within memory `feedback_dual_audit` Phase A reservation. **Real-bug yield: 13 substantive findings caught + closed across 11 rounds**: 5 routing/correctness in R1 (run_id ms drift / FC1-N12 swarm gap / Qwen-HF misroute / TR count off-by-1 / "strictest" wording reversed) + 1 fail-closed-gate in R3 (H6 wrapper soft-skip) + 1 routing collision in R6 (deepseek-ai/* misroute) + 1 PREREG § 2 logic in R7 (M4) + 1 PREREG § 8 logic in R8 (N1 — parallel-text miss) + 2 in R10 (P1 smoke false-PASS / P2 runners not in TR) + 2 in R11 (Q1 boot.rs proxy not enforced / Q2 make_pput Option<...> divergence). Plus 1 false-closure caught at R9 (N3 claimed runner default fix that wasn't shipped) — counted separately as a delivery-quality finding, not a system bug. The recurring documentary CHALLENGE class persisted longer than expected because each round's fix touched documentation in ways that left adjacent staleness; the A8e7 structural rewrite addressed the root cause (category error) but its implementation needed two more cycles (A8e8 + A8e9) to fully complete the lineage strip + cross-section consistency. **A8e12** added case C-076 + rule R-020 (commit-claim diff parity) as a Living Harness amplifier specifically targeting the false-closure / parallel-miss class — pre-commit WARN reminds the committer to grep-verify every claim against the actual diff.
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R13.md:617:PREREG § 5.5 specifies p_0 calibration via 576 paired runs (144 adaptation × 2 seeds × {control, treatment}) with estimated cost "~8 wall-hours, ~$3-5". Empirical observation 2026-04-25 during launched batch (commit 650caf7+ era):
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R13.md:622:| Total batch wall-clock | ~8 hours | Realistic 3-7 days (576 runs × 15 min serial; treatment short-circuits halve some) | 9-21× |
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R13.md:625:**The 8-hour estimate was based on "~50s/run chat oneshot" assumption that turned out wrong for swarm n3 condition on the adaptation-144 problem mix.** A 7-day batch is not "overnight"; user (mid-session 2026-04-25) explicitly questioned 576-run necessity given multiple unresolved engineering questions (N-agents → PPUT relationship, swarm_N=1 vs oneshot calibration, ground-truth feedback pipeline, etc.).
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R13.md:633:**Wording correction (A8 round-1 audit, Codex finding 1)**: an earlier draft of this section claimed `p_0 = 0.10` was the *strictest possible substitute*. That was backwards. The Gate H test is `j-RR ≤ p_0`, so a SMALLER `p_0` is stricter. `0.10` is the **maximum tolerated value** the PREREG admits — i.e., the LEAST strict admissible ceiling, not the strictest. Substituting it is the equivalent of running Gate H at the weakest threshold the PREREG ever sanctioned. The substitution is *operationally permitted* (the PREREG explicitly allows up to 0.10), but it is NOT a tighter-than-original guarantee.
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R13.md:637:- The substitution may be LESS protective than an eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`. If/when calibration runs (per § 3 conditions, which are PRE-REQUISITES — they do not by themselves *guarantee* calibration completes before any specific phase), the empirical estimate may tighten the bar; until then Gate H operates at the ceiling.
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R13.md:652:3. **Per-agent budget normalization landed** (Phase A atom A5) — current `max_transactions=200` is fixed-tx budget; PREREG § 5.5 implicitly assumes tx-budget but doesn't specify; need explicit budget regime declaration for calibration to be reproducible.
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R13.md:662:PREREG_PPUT_CCL_2026-04-26 § 5.5 originally listed p_0 calibration as a Phase B prerequisite ("Schedule: Phase B7 mandatory; not deferrable to Phase D"). This amendment **explicitly OVERRIDES that "not deferrable" clause** for the deferral conditions in § 3 above.
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R13.md:675:- **PREREG § 5.5 protocol itself** — the calibration *protocol* (288 control + 288 treatment paired runs, max-over-seeds, etc.) remains the agreed-upon procedure for IF calibration ever runs. Amendment defers the SCHEDULING, not the SCIENCE.
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R13.md:685:This pattern is per CLAUDE.md "Common Law": amendments are recorded as separate cases / docs that supersede specific sections, leaving the original frozen for reproducibility. PREREG_PPUT_CCL_2026-04-26.md SHA-256 in Trust Root manifest UNCHANGED.
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R13.md:698:Per case C-073 ArchitectAI commit workflow: this amendment requires dual audit (Codex + Gemini, conservative VETO > CHALLENGE > PASS) before commit lands. Audit packet should specifically test:
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R13.md:701:- Does substitution of `p_0 = 0.10` invalidate any Gate H statistical claim? (Should not — `j-RR` is descriptive (PREREG § 5.4), outside the inferential family, so no Type-I inflation. Per § 2 wording correction: 0.10 is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling, NOT a tighter-than-original substitute — the substitution may be less protective than an eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`, but it is **operationally permitted at any phase including Phase E** by the original PREREG § 5.5 ceiling. § 3 conditions are PRE-REQUISITES for calibration to run at all; they do NOT guarantee calibration completes before any specific phase.)
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R13.md:702:- Does deferral leave any phase blocked indefinitely? (Should not — § 3 lists explicit re-calibration conditions; if those never met, Phase E proceeds with the operationally-permitted ceiling substitution per § 2 final paragraph. Calibration UPGRADES the bar IF and WHEN § 3 conditions complete; absence of calibration is not a Phase E blocker.)
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R13.md:710:- `handover/architect-insights/THESIS_V2_GROUND_TRUTH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md` (context: ground-truth feedback discipline)
