./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R13.md:5603:- A0a: 4 new rules (R-014/R-015/R-018/R-019) + judge.sh constitution-special-case + R-016 fc_trace_in_commit hook (commit 2e7f75a)
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R13.md:5605:- A0c: 5 new cases C-071..C-075 sediment session decisions (commit 2a65339)
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R13.md:5607:- A4 (post-A3): decomposed metrics — `hit_max_tx`, `tactic_diversity`, `verifier_wait_ms` added as non-Optional v2 fields + `compute_tactic_diversity` helper; per-row decomposition of `solve_rate` / `tokens_per_solve` / `time_per_solve` (all derivable from existing `progress` / `total_run_token_count` / `total_wall_time_ms`). FC-trace: FC2-N22 (HALT decomposition for `hit_max_tx`) + FC1-N11 (∏p decision diversity for `tactic_diversity`) + FC1-N12 (oracle scope for `verifier_wait_ms`).
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R13.md:5609:- A6 (post-A5): per-line FC tagging via structured JSON events — new `fc_trace` module (pure stdlib; zero new deps); `FcId` enum (FC1-N7 / FC1-N11 / FC1-N12 / FC1-E18 / FC2-N20 / FC2-N22 / FC3-N31); `fc_event!`-style `emit_event` API; `FC_TRACE=1` gate (cached in `OnceLock`); `FC_TRACE_FILE=<path>` redirects emit to file (default sink stderr). 9 anchor sites wired (post-A8e total — 8 in `run_swarm` + 1 in `run_oneshot`): the original A6 commit shipped 6 sites (FC2-N22 synthetic short-circuit, FC2-N20 mr tick, FC2-N22 OMEGA full-proof, FC2-N22 OMEGA per-tactic, FC2-N22 natural MaxTxExhausted with `budget_regime` payload, FC1-N12 verify bracket in oneshot). A8e fix F4 added 3 more in `run_swarm` (FC1-N12 verify_omega_detailed × 2 paths + FC1-N12 verify_partial), bringing the post-A8e total to 9 production emit sites. Counted by grepping `fc_trace::emit_event(` in `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/bin/evaluator.rs` (Gemini R6 finding: round-5 phrasing "Six anchor sites" was stale relative to its own following sentence — corrected here in A8e6 K4). End-to-end smoke test exercises FC_TRACE=1 in a child process (subprocess required because `OnceLock` caches the gate-read; resolves item 7 of TRACE_MATRIX § 5 "Per-line FC tagging via tracing crate"). FC-trace: meta-witness for the 5-step compile loop (Proposal → Lean ground truth → Logging → Capability compilation → ↑H-VPPUT). Trust Root manifest 27 → 28 (corrected A8e5 J4; matches the canonical chain in `genesis_payload.toml` header).
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R13.md:5612:- A8e13b (post-A8e13, Karpathy elegance pass per user directive "make sure the code is simple, elegant like any elegant rust kernel"): two compressions — (1) `verify_child_manifest` 50 → 33 LOC (signature 3-arg → 2-arg, match-Some-None → ok_or_else, verbose docstring → 4-line, inlined `Sha256::digest` into compare expr); (2) `make_pput` body Q2 cleanup (dropped `let failed_count` rename vestige; replaced 6-line A8e13-rationale comment with 1-line PREREG cite; struct-shorthand `failed_branch_count`). C_i / T_i renames KEPT — they make the math read like PREREG § 5. Net diff +39 / −72 LOC. No behavior change.
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R13.md:5613:- A8e13 (post-A8e12, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-11 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini CHALLENGE — 2 substantive + 4 doc): **Q1 (Codex R11#1, arch)** `src/boot.rs::verify_child_manifest` recursive verification of MANIFEST.sha256 children — pre-Q1 the proxy was convention only (boot hashed parent manifest but not children; tampering with cases/C-001.yaml without regenerating cases/MANIFEST = silent gate bypass). Manifests regenerated with repo-relative paths. Meta-finding caught during fix: `src/boot.rs` itself was not in Trust Root; tampering with verify_trust_root would silently bypass entire gate. Q1 adds boot.rs to TR. 2 new unit tests. **Q2 (Gemini R11#3, refactor)** `make_pput` `Option<u64>` → plain `u64` for 3 params (every caller passed `Some(...)`; mid-term P0-B fix had collapsed the struct fields but missed the function signature). 9 production + 4 test fixtures updated. **Q3-Q6** documentary cleanup (history sealing, TRACE_MATRIX § 5/§ 6 stale R-020 + case count, 9-vs-11 enumeration reconcile). Trust Root 37 → 38.
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R13.md:5614:- A8e12 (post-A8e11, harness amplifier per user directive 2026-04-26 中文 "C，更新你的harness，加入判例"): three threads — (1) **case C-076** sediments the false-closure lesson from A8e9 N3 + the multi-section parity miss from A8e8 M4 → A8e9 N1 into a binding precedent (every commit asserting ≥2 distinct fix items requires per-claim `Verified:` proof line); (2) **rule R-020** (`rules/active/R-020_commit_claim_diff_parity.yaml` + inline `.claude/hooks/judge.sh` implementation under R-016 sibling block) WARNs at pre-commit on multi-fix-bundle messages without `Verified:` lines; (3) **self-audit pass** before round-11 to catch documentary drift my own commits keep introducing — fixed `35-entry` → `37-entry` in both audit runner header printfs (post-A8e11 the manifest is 37 but the runners still printed the pre-A8e11 value), `proxy for 14 rules` → `proxy for 15 rules` in genesis_payload.toml comment, and regenerated `cases/MANIFEST.sha256` (50 → 51 entries with C-076) + `rules/MANIFEST.sha256` (14 → 15 entries with R-020) + re-hashed `judge.sh` (R-020 inline addition) + re-hashed both audit runners (35→37 edit). Trust Root manifest unchanged at 37 (no new TR entries; only re-hashes of existing entries — case yaml is covered by cases/MANIFEST.sha256 proxy, rule yaml by rules/MANIFEST.sha256 proxy, judge.sh inline change re-hashes the existing entry). FC-trace: FC3 (read-only / log subgraph — governance instrumentation amplifier; case + rule are Living Harness updates per memory `lesson_to_rule` skill).
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R13.md:5615:- A8e11 (post-A8e10, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-10 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini PASS): two substantive gate-machinery hardenings. **P1 (Codex R10#1)**: `scripts/_smoke_siliconflow.py` now FAILS CLOSED when any of the 3 SiliconFlow keys is missing or unresponsive — pre-P1 the smoke would PASS on a single-key configuration, exactly replicating the V3L-27 single-key collapse pattern the 3-key pool was meant to mitigate (silent gate degradation). Explicit opt-out `SILICONFLOW_SMOKE_ALLOW_PARTIAL=1` returns exit 3 (partial-PASS) for credential rotation. Verified across full-keys / primary-only / primary-only-with-opt-out paths. Re-hashed in Trust Root. **P2 (Codex R10#2)**: `handover/audits/run_codex_phase_a8_exit_audit.sh` + `handover/audits/run_gemini_phase_a8_exit_audit.py` added to Trust Root manifest. Per case C-075 these are gate machinery (R8 N3 false-closure + R9 default-R2 defects demonstrated they are load-bearing); silent tamper would silently bypass the dual-audit gate. `trust_root_immutability` required-paths list extended. Manifest 35 → 37 (3 entries: smoke re-hash + 2 runners added). **Real-bug yield through 10 rounds: 11 substantive findings** (5 R1 + 1 R3 + 1 R6 + 1 R7 + 1 R8 + 2 R10 = 11; R2/4/5/8/9 had no substantive findings, only documentary).
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R13.md:5618:- A8e8 (post-A8e7, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-7 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini CHALLENGE — both convergent on rewrite-implementation gaps): five fixes including one substantive PREREG logic correction. **M1** packet § 2 + § 4 Trust Root count bumped 34 → 35 (post-A8e7 added `A8_AUDIT_HISTORY` to manifest). **M2** this history doc's A8e7 entry stamped with actual SHA `aaedc9d` + round-7 verdicts + A8e8 fix list (round-7 sealed). **M3** packet rewritten to strip ALL historical lineage text — no "(post-A8e F4)", "(added by A8eN)", "(chain position N via A8e)", "(+ A8eN fix XN)". Packet describes WHAT IS, no HOW; lineage lives only in this history doc. **M4 (substantive Codex R7#4)** PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2(b) corrected: removed false claim that "§ 3 conditions ensure calibration runs *before* Phase E"; § 3 conditions are PRE-REQUISITES for calibration TO RUN at all, not guarantees of completion before any specific phase. § 8 reads as the operative rule: substitution permitted at any phase including Phase E; calibration UPGRADES the bar IF and WHEN § 3 conditions complete. Single semantics, internally consistent across § 2 / § 3 / § 8. Re-hashed in Trust Root. **M5** audit runner scripts now append `experiments/minif2f_v4/tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs` so Q1.c (verify wrapper fail-closed) is verifiable from the packet bundle alone. Trust Root manifest unchanged at 35 (only re-hashes; no new files).
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R13.md:5620:- A8e6 (post-A8e5, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-6 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini CHALLENGE — both narrow): one real correctness fix + 4 doc fixes. **K1** packet `<pending>` placeholder cleanup (J2 + RQ14 wording) — same precedent as A8e5 J2 but applied to round-6 self-references. **K2 (real bug, Codex R6#2)** `src/drivers/llm_proxy.py` `detect_provider`: HuggingFace-style `deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1-Distill-Qwen-7B` was misrouting to `api.deepseek.com` because `"deepseek" in m` won before the slash check; pre-K2 logic would 404 on every Distill variant since the official DeepSeek API doesn't serve them. K2 swaps the order — slash-form is now the FIRST routing heuristic (after explicit prefix), and bare-deepseek substring only fires when there's no slash. New `test_deepseek_slash_form_routes_to_siliconflow` in `scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` pins this (covers `deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1-Distill-Qwen-7B` + `deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-V2.5` AS WELL AS the bare-form sanity case `deepseek-chat` → deepseek). **K3** TRACE_MATRIX A8e3 row stale "21 positional args" → 24 (matches packet update from A8e5 J5; Codex R6#3 caught the inconsistency between TRACE_MATRIX and the packet). **K4** TRACE_MATRIX A6 row top-line "Six anchor sites" → "9 anchor sites" with A6-original-6 + F4-added-3 breakdown (Gemini R6#1 caught the contradiction between the line's first sentence and its own follow-up). **K5** packet § 3 A5/A6 fix attribution: "A8e3 fix H4 corrected" → "A8e4 fix I2 corrected" (Gemini R6#2 caught the cross-reference error — H4 corrected the genesis_payload.toml TR-header comment, I2 corrected the packet § 3 numbers). Trust Root manifest unchanged at 34 (re-hashes of `llm_proxy.py` + `scripts/test_llm_proxy.py`; no new files).
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R13.md:5623:- A8e3 (post-A8e2, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-3 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini CHALLENGE — both convergent on packet staleness; no VETO): six narrow cleanup fixes. **H1** A8 packet § 3 A1 description: "Mathematically conservative" → "least-strict admissible value" with explicit Type-I implications. **H2** packet § 3 A6 description: 6 → 9 anchor sites (added F4 swarm sites explicitly). **H3** packet § 6 Q2.a / Q4.a / Q4.d marked CLOSED with closure rationale. **H4** `genesis_payload.toml` TR header phrasing about A1: "conservative ceiling" → "max-tolerated ceiling". **H5** § 5 item 7 here in TRACE_MATRIX: "(commit pending) + 6 wired" → "CLOSED + 9 wired". **H6** G1 wrapper test fail-closed on missing `python3` (was soft skip; Codex R3 caught the silent-pass risk); explicit opt-out `SKIP_LLM_PROXY_PYTHON_CONFORMANCE=1` for deliberate downgrades (logged loudly). Trust Root manifest unchanged at 34 (only re-hashes; no new files). Non-blocking observation deferred to Phase B+: `make_pput` signature was 21 positional args at round-3; A8e fix F1 added the `run_id` parameter, bringing the post-A8e count to **24** (Gemini R3 Finding 2 + Gemini R5/Codex R6 spot-checks; A8e6 K3 corrected here) — refactor to a builder pattern is recommended but not gating.
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R13.md:5624:- A8e2 (post-A8e, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-2 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini CHALLENGE — both convergent): three dual-audit fixes. **G1** (Codex R2#1 + Gemini R2#1) `experiments/minif2f_v4/tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs` wraps `python3 scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` in a Rust integration test, making the V3L-27 round-robin conformance gate run on every `cargo test --workspace`. **G2** (Codex R2#2) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 8` audit-requirements paragraph reworded — round-1 fix corrected § 2 but left § 8 still calling the substitution a "strictest plausible bar"; now consistent across the doc. Re-hashed in Trust Root. **G3** (Codex R2#3 + Gemini R2#2) `A8_EXIT_PACKET` updated for round-2 reality: § 2 cumulative-tests table extended with A8e/A8e2 columns, § 4 manifest-clean assertion bumped 30→34, § 5 round-1 risk #5 (no FC1-N12 in swarm verify path) removed (closed by F4), § 6 Q7.a/b numbers updated, round-2 outcome + round-3 fixes section appended; TRACE_MATRIX stale `run_corr_id` symbol row replaced; "in CI" softened to "via Rust wrapper post-A8e2". FC-trace: meta governance + V3L-27 mitigation gating (FC1-N7). Trust Root manifest 33 → 34 (`tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs`).
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R13.md:5625:- A8e (post-A7, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-1 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini VETO): six dual-audit fixes. **F1** (Codex#2 + Gemini Q4) `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/run_id.rs` mints one stable run_id per run, threaded into both `emit_event` and `make_pput` (replaces `run_corr_id` vs internal `run_id` ms drift; oneshot also stops using `oneshot_{problem_file}` placeholder). **F2** (Gemini VETO Q6) `scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` adds 15-test conformance battery — round-robin distributes [2,2,2] across 6 calls, single-key pool degrades to all-zero, two-key pool alternates [2,2]; exercises the V3L-27 mitigation without a live API. (A8e2 G1 wires this into `cargo test --workspace` via a Rust shell-out wrapper — see § 2 below.) **F3** (Codex#4) routing matrix bug fixed — `Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct` was misrouting to DashScope because `m.startswith("qwen")` won after the slash check; now slash-form ⇒ siliconflow first, bare-qwen ⇒ dashscope second. **F4** (Codex#3) FC1-N12 emit added to all 3 swarm verify sites (`verify_omega_detailed` × 2 + `verify_partial`), each with verdict + elapsed_ms + path stamp; closes the swarm-side gap in the 5-step compile-loop witness. **F5** (Codex#5) `genesis_payload.toml` header + TRACE_MATRIX milestones reconciled to actual 31 entries (round-1 packet undercounted by 1 — agent_models.rs from A3 was already in TR before A1). **F6** (Codex#1) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2` wording corrected — `p_0=0.10` substitution is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling (max tolerated value for `j-RR ≤ p_0`), NOT the strictest possible substitute as round-1 mistakenly claimed; explicit statistical-implications paragraph added (no Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive; substitution may be less protective than eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`). FC-trace: FC1-N12 (oracle scope swarm coverage) + FC1-N7 (δ provider routing) + Art. V (amendment governance). Trust Root manifest 31 → 33 (run_id.rs + test_llm_proxy.py).

Empirical observation 2026-04-25 during launched batch (commit 650caf7+ era):
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R13.md:6527:    16	| Total batch wall-clock | ~8 hours | Realistic 3-7 days (576 runs × 15 min serial; treatment short-circuits halve some) | 9-21× |
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R13.md:6530:    19	**The 8-hour estimate was based on "~50s/run chat oneshot" assumption that turned out wrong for swarm n3 condition on the adaptation-144 problem mix.** A 7-day batch is not "overnight"; user (mid-session 2026-04-25) explicitly questioned 576-run necessity given multiple unresolved engineering questions (N-agents → PPUT relationship, swarm_N=1 vs oneshot calibration, ground-truth feedback pipeline, etc.).
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R13.md:6538:    27	**Wording correction (A8 round-1 audit, Codex finding 1)**: an earlier draft of this section claimed `p_0 = 0.10` was the *strictest possible substitute*. That was backwards. The Gate H test is `j-RR ≤ p_0`, so a SMALLER `p_0` is stricter. `0.10` is the **maximum tolerated value** the PREREG admits — i.e., the LEAST strict admissible ceiling, not the strictest. Substituting it is the equivalent of running Gate H at the weakest threshold the PREREG ever sanctioned. The substitution is *operationally permitted* (the PREREG explicitly allows up to 0.10), but it is NOT a tighter-than-original guarantee.
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R13.md:6542:    31	- The substitution may be LESS protective than an eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`. If/when calibration runs (per § 3 conditions, which are PRE-REQUISITES — they do not by themselves *guarantee* calibration completes before any specific phase), the empirical estimate may tighten the bar; until then Gate H operates at the ceiling.
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R13.md:6557:    46	3. **Per-agent budget normalization landed** (Phase A atom A5) — current `max_transactions=200` is fixed-tx budget; PREREG § 5.5 implicitly assumes tx-budget but doesn't specify; need explicit budget regime declaration for calibration to be reproducible.
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R13.md:6567:    56	PREREG_PPUT_CCL_2026-04-26 § 5.5 originally listed p_0 calibration as a Phase B prerequisite ("Schedule: Phase B7 mandatory; not deferrable to Phase D"). This amendment **explicitly OVERRIDES that "not deferrable" clause** for the deferral conditions in § 3 above.
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R13.md:6580:    69	- **PREREG § 5.5 protocol itself** — the calibration *protocol* (288 control + 288 treatment paired runs, max-over-seeds, etc.) remains the agreed-upon procedure for IF calibration ever runs. Amendment defers the SCHEDULING, not the SCIENCE.
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R13.md:6590:    79	This pattern is per CLAUDE.md "Common Law": amendments are recorded as separate cases / docs that supersede specific sections, leaving the original frozen for reproducibility. PREREG_PPUT_CCL_2026-04-26.md SHA-256 in Trust Root manifest UNCHANGED.
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R13.md:6603:    92	Per case C-073 ArchitectAI commit workflow: this amendment requires dual audit (Codex + Gemini, conservative VETO > CHALLENGE > PASS) before commit lands. Audit packet should specifically test:
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R13.md:6606:    95	- Does substitution of `p_0 = 0.10` invalidate any Gate H statistical claim? (Should not — `j-RR` is descriptive (PREREG § 5.4), outside the inferential family, so no Type-I inflation. Per § 2 wording correction: 0.10 is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling, NOT a tighter-than-original substitute — the substitution may be less protective than an eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`, but it is **operationally permitted at any phase including Phase E** by the original PREREG § 5.5 ceiling. § 3 conditions are PRE-REQUISITES for calibration to run at all; they do NOT guarantee calibration completes before any specific phase.)
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R13.md:6607:    96	- Does deferral leave any phase blocked indefinitely? (Should not — § 3 lists explicit re-calibration conditions; if those never met, Phase E proceeds with the operationally-permitted ceiling substitution per § 2 final paragraph. Calibration UPGRADES the bar IF and WHEN § 3 conditions complete; absence of calibration is not a Phase E blocker.)

(**Gemini R12 — substantive run_swarm bug**) When an agent's `search_count >= search_cap` (default 20), the search tool is correctly stripped from `tools_desc` (line 870), but `search_cache[agent_id]` is not cleared. The cached results from the agent's last successful search keep being injected into every subsequent prompt's `hits_ref` (line 869) for the rest of the run. The agent reasons from stale data forever; uncontrolled experimental variable that would confound any swarm experiment that exercises the cap.

./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R13.md:6784:handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:11:- A6 (post-A5): per-line FC tagging via structured JSON events — new `fc_trace` module (pure stdlib; zero new deps); `FcId` enum (FC1-N7 / FC1-N11 / FC1-N12 / FC1-E18 / FC2-N20 / FC2-N22 / FC3-N31); `fc_event!`-style `emit_event` API; `FC_TRACE=1` gate (cached in `OnceLock`); `FC_TRACE_FILE=<path>` redirects emit to file (default sink stderr). 9 anchor sites wired (post-A8e total — 8 in `run_swarm` + 1 in `run_oneshot`): the original A6 commit shipped 6 sites (FC2-N22 synthetic short-circuit, FC2-N20 mr tick, FC2-N22 OMEGA full-proof, FC2-N22 OMEGA per-tactic, FC2-N22 natural MaxTxExhausted with `budget_regime` payload, FC1-N12 verify bracket in oneshot). A8e fix F4 added 3 more in `run_swarm` (FC1-N12 verify_omega_detailed × 2 paths + FC1-N12 verify_partial), bringing the post-A8e total to 9 production emit sites. Counted by grepping `fc_trace::emit_event(` in `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/bin/evaluator.rs` (Gemini R6 finding: round-5 phrasing "Six anchor sites" was stale relative to its own following sentence — corrected here in A8e6 K4). End-to-end smoke test exercises FC_TRACE=1 in a child process (subprocess required because `OnceLock` caches the gate-read; resolves item 7 of TRACE_MATRIX § 5 "Per-line FC tagging via tracing crate"). FC-trace: meta-witness for the 5-step compile loop (Proposal → Lean ground truth → Logging → Capability compilation → ↑H-VPPUT). Trust Root manifest 27 → 28 (corrected A8e5 J4; matches the canonical chain in `genesis_payload.toml` header).
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R13.md:6787:handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:15:- A8e13 (post-A8e12, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-11 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini CHALLENGE — 2 substantive + 4 doc): **Q1 (Codex R11#1, arch)** `src/boot.rs::verify_child_manifest` recursive verification of MANIFEST.sha256 children — pre-Q1 the proxy was convention only (boot hashed parent manifest but not children; tampering with cases/C-001.yaml without regenerating cases/MANIFEST = silent gate bypass). Manifests regenerated with repo-relative paths. Meta-finding caught during fix: `src/boot.rs` itself was not in Trust Root; tampering with verify_trust_root would silently bypass entire gate. Q1 adds boot.rs to TR. 2 new unit tests. **Q2 (Gemini R11#3, refactor)** `make_pput` `Option<u64>` → plain `u64` for 3 params (every caller passed `Some(...)`; mid-term P0-B fix had collapsed the struct fields but missed the function signature). 9 production + 4 test fixtures updated. **Q3-Q6** documentary cleanup (history sealing, TRACE_MATRIX § 5/§ 6 stale R-020 + case count, 9-vs-11 enumeration reconcile). Trust Root 37 → 38.
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R13.md:6788:handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:16:- A8e12 (post-A8e11, harness amplifier per user directive 2026-04-26 中文 "C，更新你的harness，加入判例"): three threads — (1) **case C-076** sediments the false-closure lesson from A8e9 N3 + the multi-section parity miss from A8e8 M4 → A8e9 N1 into a binding precedent (every commit asserting ≥2 distinct fix items requires per-claim `Verified:` proof line); (2) **rule R-020** (`rules/active/R-020_commit_claim_diff_parity.yaml` + inline `.claude/hooks/judge.sh` implementation under R-016 sibling block) WARNs at pre-commit on multi-fix-bundle messages without `Verified:` lines; (3) **self-audit pass** before round-11 to catch documentary drift my own commits keep introducing — fixed `35-entry` → `37-entry` in both audit runner header printfs (post-A8e11 the manifest is 37 but the runners still printed the pre-A8e11 value), `proxy for 14 rules` → `proxy for 15 rules` in genesis_payload.toml comment, and regenerated `cases/MANIFEST.sha256` (50 → 51 entries with C-076) + `rules/MANIFEST.sha256` (14 → 15 entries with R-020) + re-hashed `judge.sh` (R-020 inline addition) + re-hashed both audit runners (35→37 edit). Trust Root manifest unchanged at 37 (no new TR entries; only re-hashes of existing entries — case yaml is covered by cases/MANIFEST.sha256 proxy, rule yaml by rules/MANIFEST.sha256 proxy, judge.sh inline change re-hashes the existing entry). FC-trace: FC3 (read-only / log subgraph — governance instrumentation amplifier; case + rule are Living Harness updates per memory `lesson_to_rule` skill).
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R13.md:6789:handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:17:- A8e11 (post-A8e10, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-10 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini PASS): two substantive gate-machinery hardenings. **P1 (Codex R10#1)**: `scripts/_smoke_siliconflow.py` now FAILS CLOSED when any of the 3 SiliconFlow keys is missing or unresponsive — pre-P1 the smoke would PASS on a single-key configuration, exactly replicating the V3L-27 single-key collapse pattern the 3-key pool was meant to mitigate (silent gate degradation). Explicit opt-out `SILICONFLOW_SMOKE_ALLOW_PARTIAL=1` returns exit 3 (partial-PASS) for credential rotation. Verified across full-keys / primary-only / primary-only-with-opt-out paths. Re-hashed in Trust Root. **P2 (Codex R10#2)**: `handover/audits/run_codex_phase_a8_exit_audit.sh` + `handover/audits/run_gemini_phase_a8_exit_audit.py` added to Trust Root manifest. Per case C-075 these are gate machinery (R8 N3 false-closure + R9 default-R2 defects demonstrated they are load-bearing); silent tamper would silently bypass the dual-audit gate. `trust_root_immutability` required-paths list extended. Manifest 35 → 37 (3 entries: smoke re-hash + 2 runners added). **Real-bug yield through 10 rounds: 11 substantive findings** (5 R1 + 1 R3 + 1 R6 + 1 R7 + 1 R8 + 2 R10 = 11; R2/4/5/8/9 had no substantive findings, only documentary).
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R13.md:6791:handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:20:- A8e8 (post-A8e7, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-7 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini CHALLENGE — both convergent on rewrite-implementation gaps): five fixes including one substantive PREREG logic correction. **M1** packet § 2 + § 4 Trust Root count bumped 34 → 35 (post-A8e7 added `A8_AUDIT_HISTORY` to manifest). **M2** this history doc's A8e7 entry stamped with actual SHA `aaedc9d` + round-7 verdicts + A8e8 fix list (round-7 sealed). **M3** packet rewritten to strip ALL historical lineage text — no "(post-A8e F4)", "(added by A8eN)", "(chain position N via A8e)", "(+ A8eN fix XN)". Packet describes WHAT IS, no HOW; lineage lives only in this history doc. **M4 (substantive Codex R7#4)** PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2(b) corrected: removed false claim that "§ 3 conditions ensure calibration runs *before* Phase E"; § 3 conditions are PRE-REQUISITES for calibration TO RUN at all, not guarantees of completion before any specific phase. § 8 reads as the operative rule: substitution permitted at any phase including Phase E; calibration UPGRADES the bar IF and WHEN § 3 conditions complete. Single semantics, internally consistent across § 2 / § 3 / § 8. Re-hashed in Trust Root. **M5** audit runner scripts now append `experiments/minif2f_v4/tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs` so Q1.c (verify wrapper fail-closed) is verifiable from the packet bundle alone. Trust Root manifest unchanged at 35 (only re-hashes; no new files).
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R13.md:6792:handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:22:- A8e6 (post-A8e5, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-6 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini CHALLENGE — both narrow): one real correctness fix + 4 doc fixes. **K1** packet `<pending>` placeholder cleanup (J2 + RQ14 wording) — same precedent as A8e5 J2 but applied to round-6 self-references. **K2 (real bug, Codex R6#2)** `src/drivers/llm_proxy.py` `detect_provider`: HuggingFace-style `deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1-Distill-Qwen-7B` was misrouting to `api.deepseek.com` because `"deepseek" in m` won before the slash check; pre-K2 logic would 404 on every Distill variant since the official DeepSeek API doesn't serve them. K2 swaps the order — slash-form is now the FIRST routing heuristic (after explicit prefix), and bare-deepseek substring only fires when there's no slash. New `test_deepseek_slash_form_routes_to_siliconflow` in `scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` pins this (covers `deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1-Distill-Qwen-7B` + `deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-V2.5` AS WELL AS the bare-form sanity case `deepseek-chat` → deepseek). **K3** TRACE_MATRIX A8e3 row stale "21 positional args" → 24 (matches packet update from A8e5 J5; Codex R6#3 caught the inconsistency between TRACE_MATRIX and the packet). **K4** TRACE_MATRIX A6 row top-line "Six anchor sites" → "9 anchor sites" with A6-original-6 + F4-added-3 breakdown (Gemini R6#1 caught the contradiction between the line's first sentence and its own follow-up). **K5** packet § 3 A5/A6 fix attribution: "A8e3 fix H4 corrected" → "A8e4 fix I2 corrected" (Gemini R6#2 caught the cross-reference error — H4 corrected the genesis_payload.toml TR-header comment, I2 corrected the packet § 3 numbers). Trust Root manifest unchanged at 34 (re-hashes of `llm_proxy.py` + `scripts/test_llm_proxy.py`; no new files).
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R13.md:6794:handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:25:- A8e3 (post-A8e2, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-3 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini CHALLENGE — both convergent on packet staleness; no VETO): six narrow cleanup fixes. **H1** A8 packet § 3 A1 description: "Mathematically conservative" → "least-strict admissible value" with explicit Type-I implications. **H2** packet § 3 A6 description: 6 → 9 anchor sites (added F4 swarm sites explicitly). **H3** packet § 6 Q2.a / Q4.a / Q4.d marked CLOSED with closure rationale. **H4** `genesis_payload.toml` TR header phrasing about A1: "conservative ceiling" → "max-tolerated ceiling". **H5** § 5 item 7 here in TRACE_MATRIX: "(commit pending) + 6 wired" → "CLOSED + 9 wired". **H6** G1 wrapper test fail-closed on missing `python3` (was soft skip; Codex R3 caught the silent-pass risk); explicit opt-out `SKIP_LLM_PROXY_PYTHON_CONFORMANCE=1` for deliberate downgrades (logged loudly). Trust Root manifest unchanged at 34 (only re-hashes; no new files). Non-blocking observation deferred to Phase B+: `make_pput` signature was 21 positional args at round-3; A8e fix F1 added the `run_id` parameter, bringing the post-A8e count to **24** (Gemini R3 Finding 2 + Gemini R5/Codex R6 spot-checks; A8e6 K3 corrected here) — refactor to a builder pattern is recommended but not gating.
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R13.md:6795:handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:27:- A8e (post-A7, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-1 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini VETO): six dual-audit fixes. **F1** (Codex#2 + Gemini Q4) `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/run_id.rs` mints one stable run_id per run, threaded into both `emit_event` and `make_pput` (replaces `run_corr_id` vs internal `run_id` ms drift; oneshot also stops using `oneshot_{problem_file}` placeholder). **F2** (Gemini VETO Q6) `scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` adds 15-test conformance battery — round-robin distributes [2,2,2] across 6 calls, single-key pool degrades to all-zero, two-key pool alternates [2,2]; exercises the V3L-27 mitigation without a live API. (A8e2 G1 wires this into `cargo test --workspace` via a Rust shell-out wrapper — see § 2 below.) **F3** (Codex#4) routing matrix bug fixed — `Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct` was misrouting to DashScope because `m.startswith("qwen")` won after the slash check; now slash-form ⇒ siliconflow first, bare-qwen ⇒ dashscope second. **F4** (Codex#3) FC1-N12 emit added to all 3 swarm verify sites (`verify_omega_detailed` × 2 + `verify_partial`), each with verdict + elapsed_ms + path stamp; closes the swarm-side gap in the 5-step compile-loop witness. **F5** (Codex#5) `genesis_payload.toml` header + TRACE_MATRIX milestones reconciled to actual 31 entries (round-1 packet undercounted by 1 — agent_models.rs from A3 was already in TR before A1). **F6** (Codex#1) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2` wording corrected — `p_0=0.10` substitution is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling (max tolerated value for `j-RR ≤ p_0`), NOT the strictest possible substitute as round-1 mistakenly claimed; explicit statistical-implications paragraph added (no Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive; substitution may be less protective than eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`). FC-trace: FC1-N12 (oracle scope swarm coverage) + FC1-N7 (δ provider routing) + Art. V (amendment governance). Trust Root manifest 31 → 33 (run_id.rs + test_llm_proxy.py).
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R13.md:6829:handover/audits/A8_AUDIT_HISTORY_2026-04-26.md:287:2. (**Codex R10#2 — substantive**) Audit runner scripts (`run_codex_phase_a8_exit_audit.sh` + `run_gemini_phase_a8_exit_audit.py`) are load-bearing gate tools (demonstrated by R8/R9's runner-default + false-closure defects) but were NOT in Trust Root. Per case C-075 (DO-178C tool qualification): tools producing data used by future dual audit must enter Trust Root.
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R13.md:6840:handover/audits/A8_AUDIT_HISTORY_2026-04-26.md:318:- **Q2** (Gemini R11#3 — substantive refactor): `make_pput` signature `Option<u64>` → plain `u64` for the 3 affected parameters. All 9 production call sites + 4 test fixtures updated. `wc.elapsed_ms()` returns `Option<u64>` upstream; call sites add `.unwrap_or(0)` at the boundary. `cargo test --workspace`: 267 PASS (was 265 + 2 new boot tests).
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R13.md:6841:handover/audits/A8_AUDIT_HISTORY_2026-04-26.md:322:- **Q6** (Codex R11#4): cumulative metrics paragraph reworded — "11 substantive findings" enumeration extended to 13 (was missing R10's P1+P2 = 2 findings; now lists all explicitly).
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R13.md:6846:handover/audits/A8_AUDIT_HISTORY_2026-04-26.md:353:4. (**Gemini R12 — substantive run_swarm bug**) When an agent's `search_count >= search_cap` (default 20), the search tool is correctly stripped from `tools_desc` (line 870), but `search_cache[agent_id]` is not cleared. The cached results from the agent's last successful search keep being injected into every subsequent prompt's `hits_ref` (line 869) for the rest of the run. The agent reasons from stale data forever; uncontrolled experimental variable that would confound any swarm experiment that exercises the cap.
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R13.md:6851:handover/audits/A8_AUDIT_HISTORY_2026-04-26.md:379:Cumulative cost so far ~$70 (12 rounds × ~$5–7 — note A8e12 was a no-audit harness amplifier between R10 and R11); within memory `feedback_dual_audit` Phase A reservation. **Real-bug yield: 13 substantive findings caught + closed across 11 rounds**: 5 routing/correctness in R1 (run_id ms drift / FC1-N12 swarm gap / Qwen-HF misroute / TR count off-by-1 / "strictest" wording reversed) + 1 fail-closed-gate in R3 (H6 wrapper soft-skip) + 1 routing collision in R6 (deepseek-ai/* misroute) + 1 PREREG § 2 logic in R7 (M4) + 1 PREREG § 8 logic in R8 (N1 — parallel-text miss) + 2 in R10 (P1 smoke false-PASS / P2 runners not in TR) + 2 in R11 (Q1 boot.rs proxy not enforced / Q2 make_pput Option<...> divergence). Plus 1 false-closure caught at R9 (N3 claimed runner default fix that wasn't shipped) — counted separately as a delivery-quality finding, not a system bug. The recurring documentary CHALLENGE class persisted longer than expected because each round's fix touched documentation in ways that left adjacent staleness; the A8e7 structural rewrite addressed the root cause (category error) but its implementation needed two more cycles (A8e8 + A8e9) to fully complete the lineage strip + cross-section consistency. **A8e12** added case C-076 + rule R-020 (commit-claim diff parity) as a Living Harness amplifier specifically targeting the false-closure / parallel-miss class — pre-commit WARN reminds the committer to grep-verify every claim against the actual diff.

./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R13.md:9542:   149	- (Q3.b) 5 cases C-071..C-075 sediment 2026-04-25 session decisions as constitutional precedent. Are the rulings standalone-readable + correctly cross-referenced?
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R13.md:9545:   152	### Q4 — Phase A → B exit decision
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R13.md:9546:   153	- (Q4.a) Spot-check: re-run `cargo test --workspace`; expect 267 PASS / 29 ignored / 0 failed.
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R13.md:9716:handover/audits/GEMINI_B7_EXTRA_REAUDIT_2026-04-25.md:61:    -   Yes. `compute_p0.py` exits with 1 on data integrity errors (e.g., missing pairs, seed mismatch) via `sys.exit("ERROR...")`. It exits with 2 specifically for a ceiling violation.
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R13.md:9719:handover/audits/GEMINI_B7_EXTRA_REAUDIT_2026-04-25.md:71:    -   Since `compute_p0.py` is now in the Trust Root manifest, its hash is fixed. Any execution of the calibration batch is guaranteed to run this specific version of the script, where the PREREG constants are effectively hard-coded into the `main` entry point. This prevents bypass.
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R13.md:9729:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26.md:174:- (Q2.a) Substituting `p_0 = 0.10` at the Gate H consumer (the PREREG § 5.5 ceiling) is described as conservative. Verify: does this introduce any Type-I inflation, multiplicity hazard, or family-construction inconsistency vs the original 576-run calibration protocol?
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R13.md:9732:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26.md:237:PREREG § 5.5 specifies p_0 calibration via 576 paired runs (144 adaptation × 2 seeds × {control, treatment}) with estimated cost "~8 wall-hours, ~$3-5". Empirical observation 2026-04-25 during launched batch (commit 650caf7+ era):
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R13.md:9738:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26.md:275:PREREG_PPUT_CCL_2026-04-26 § 5.5 originally listed p_0 calibration as a Phase B prerequisite ("Schedule: Phase B7 mandatory; not deferrable to Phase D"). This amendment **explicitly OVERRIDES that "not deferrable" clause** for the deferral conditions in § 3 above.

./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R13.md:9841:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26.md:9636:handover/preregistration/PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md:11:PREREG § 5.5 specifies p_0 calibration via 576 paired runs (144 adaptation × 2 seeds × {control, treatment}) with estimated cost "~8 wall-hours, ~$3-5". Empirical observation 2026-04-25 during launched batch (commit 650caf7+ era):
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R13.md:9848:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26.md:9643:handover/preregistration/PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md:49:PREREG_PPUT_CCL_2026-04-26 § 5.5 originally listed p_0 calibration as a Phase B prerequisite ("Schedule: Phase B7 mandatory; not deferrable to Phase D"). This amendment **explicitly OVERRIDES that "not deferrable" clause** for the deferral conditions in § 3 above.
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R13.md:9865:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26.md:9664:handover/preregistration/PREREG_PPUT_CCL_2026-04-26.md:435:**Why descriptive is statistically correct here**: j-RR is a constraint, not a discovery hypothesis. We do not need to argue "RR_j is significantly less than p_0"; we only need RR_j ≤ p_0. A point check is precisely the right tool. No multiplicity correction applies because this is not a test.
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R13.md:9879:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26.md:9681:handover/preregistration/PREREG_PPUT_CCL_2026-04-26.md:567:| H | **Heldout WBCG_PPUT > 0 with ArtifactState = Certified gate** (round 4 — j-RR moved out of inferential family per Codex round-3): at least one ArchitectAI-generated user-space artifact Δ has `ArtifactState(Δ) = Certified` per § 1.7 — meaning (i) was Accepted (passed § 3 + § 3.5 AuditorAI battery), (ii) was Quarantined (user-approved + ΔPPUT_meta_val > 0 + N_use ≥ 3 on meta_val), (iii) at heldout sealed eval **three** primary inferential tests reject null at family-corrected α (per § 5.3 + § 9): **j-PPUT** (sign test on n=54), **j-FAR** (non-inferiority sign test), **j-CPR** (non-inferiority sign test), AND (iv) **j-RR ≤ p_0 point check guardrail** (per § 5.4 — descriptive, NOT inferential, NO α correction), AND (v) is rollbackable (artifact directory deletion at the protocol level restores prior heldout PPUT — verified by an explicit rollback sub-eval, see § 6 E2). Quarantined-only artifacts (failed any of the five conditions) do NOT count toward Gate H. |
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R13.md:9887:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26.md:9689:handover/preregistration/PREREG_PPUT_CCL_2026-04-26.md:777:| 2026-04-26 (round 3) | **R3-2**: § 5.2 p_0 calibration protocol fully specified — toggle (`--simulate-rollback-at-tx-50`), sample (full adaptation-144 × 2 seeds = 288 paired runs), estimator (per-problem worst-case across seeds), schedule (Phase B7 mandatory before Phase D), freeze (genesis_payload.toml + Trust Root SHA-256 of calibration jsonl), ceiling (p_0 > 0.10 = ABORT), audit (dual-audit at Phase B → C transition). Closes round-2 tuning-surface concern. | Codex round-2 audit P0-gate-h (partial) |
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R13.md:9932:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26.md:9781:   435	**Why descriptive is statistically correct here**: j-RR is a constraint, not a discovery hypothesis. ./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R13.md:9965:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26.md:10007:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A0_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:5157:./handover/preregistration/scripts/run_p0_calibration.sh:407:    head -1 "${OUT_PREFIX}_treatment.jsonl" 2>/dev/null | python3 -c "import sys,json; r=json.loads(sys.stdin.read()); print(' treatment:', {k: r.get(k) for k in ('tx_count','solved','progress','synthetic_short_circuit','synthetic_timeout_or_crash')})" || true
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R13.md:9978:handover/audits/run_gemini_b7_extra_prebatch_audit.py:42:- (B) cost asymmetry — short-circuit at tx 50 understates C_i vs true 150-tx vetoed loop; mitigated by synthetic_short_circuit field doc + compute_p0.py only joins on SOLVED/UNSOLVED
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R13.md:9980:handover/audits/run_gemini_b7_extra_prebatch_audit.py:74:PREREG § 5.5 specifies `p_0 = sum_p max_seed(regression_p) / 144` where `regression_p = 1 iff control SOLVED && treatment UNSOLVED`. Treatment short-circuits at tx 50.
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R13.md:9981:handover/audits/run_gemini_b7_extra_prebatch_audit.py:77:- (Q3.a) The synthetic short-circuit at tx 50 measures "how often does control take ≥ 50 tx to solve". Is that a FAIR proxy for "how often does a Phase E artifact corrupt mid-run state"? Does this matter for j-RR ≤ p_0 guardrail?
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R13.md:9984:handover/audits/run_gemini_b7_extra_prebatch_audit.py:81:- (Q3.e) Boltzmann seeds [31415, 2718] frozen in PREREG; `run_p0_calibration.sh` hardcodes them. If a future drift forces re-calibration, are the seeds in Trust Root manifest sufficient to detect tampering, or could a runner script tamper go undetected?
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R13.md:9989:handover/audits/run_gemini_b7_extra_prebatch_audit.py:117:- (Q7.b) If any individual run hits the 40-min timeout, runner skips with MEASUREMENT_ERROR. Does this artificially raise p_0?
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R13.md:9997:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:62:- **F6** PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2 wording corrected: `p_0=0.10` is the **least-strict admissible ceiling** (max tolerated value), NOT the "strictest possible substitute". No Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive; explicit statistical-implications paragraph added. Closes Codex#1.
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R13.md:10003:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:184:- Substitutes `p_0 = 0.10` (PREREG § 5.5 ceiling) for the calibration-derived value at every Gate H consumer. The substitution is operationally permitted (the PREREG explicitly admits up to 0.10 as the ceiling) but is the **least-strict admissible value** since `j-RR ≤ p_0` makes a SMALLER `p_0` stricter — see PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2 wording correction (round-1 audit Codex#1, A8e fix F6) for full statistical implications. No Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive (PREREG § 5.4), outside the inferential family. May be less protective than an eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`; acceptable because Gate H is Phase E and § 3 conditions ensure calibration runs first. Re-calibration conditions in § 3 list 5 items (N-experiments arc complete / swarm_N=1 mode landed / per-agent budget normalization landed / hetero-LLM exp complete / Phase D ArchitectAI runtime exists).
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R13.md:10009:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:318:PREREG § 5.5 specifies p_0 calibration via 576 paired runs (144 adaptation × 2 seeds × {control, treatment}) with estimated cost "~8 wall-hours, ~$3-5". Empirical observation 2026-04-25 during launched batch (commit 650caf7+ era):
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R13.md:10012:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:334:**Wording correction (A8 round-1 audit, Codex finding 1)**: an earlier draft of this section claimed `p_0 = 0.10` was the *strictest possible substitute*. That was backwards. The Gate H test is `j-RR ≤ p_0`, so a SMALLER `p_0` is stricter. `0.10` is the **maximum tolerated value** the PREREG admits — i.e., the LEAST strict admissible ceiling, not the strictest. Substituting it is the equivalent of running Gate H at the weakest threshold the PREREG ever sanctioned. The substitution is *operationally permitted* (the PREREG explicitly allows up to 0.10), but it is NOT a tighter-than-original guarantee.
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R13.md:10019:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:363:PREREG_PPUT_CCL_2026-04-26 § 5.5 originally listed p_0 calibration as a Phase B prerequisite ("Schedule: Phase B7 mandatory; not deferrable to Phase D"). This amendment **explicitly OVERRIDES that "not deferrable" clause** for the deferral conditions in § 3 above.
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R13.md:10039:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:5110:- A8e (post-A7, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-1 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini VETO): six dual-audit fixes. **F1** (Codex#2 + Gemini Q4) `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/run_id.rs` mints one stable run_id per run, threaded into both `emit_event` and `make_pput` (replaces `run_corr_id` vs internal `run_id` ms drift; oneshot also stops using `oneshot_{problem_file}` placeholder). **F2** (Gemini VETO Q6) `scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` adds 15-test conformance battery — round-robin distributes [2,2,2] across 6 calls, single-key pool degrades to all-zero, two-key pool alternates [2,2]; exercises the V3L-27 mitigation without a live API. (A8e2 G1 wires this into `cargo test --workspace` via a Rust shell-out wrapper — see § 2 below.) **F3** (Codex#4) routing matrix bug fixed — `Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct` was misrouting to DashScope because `m.startswith("qwen")` won after the slash check; now slash-form ⇒ siliconflow first, bare-qwen ⇒ dashscope second. **F4** (Codex#3) FC1-N12 emit added to all 3 swarm verify sites (`verify_omega_detailed` × 2 + `verify_partial`), each with verdict + elapsed_ms + path stamp; closes the swarm-side gap in the 5-step compile-loop witness. **F5** (Codex#5) `genesis_payload.toml` header + TRACE_MATRIX milestones reconciled to actual 31 entries (round-1 packet undercounted by 1 — agent_models.rs from A3 was already in TR before A1). **F6** (Codex#1) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2` wording corrected — `p_0=0.10` substitution is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling (max tolerated value for `j-RR ≤ p_0`), NOT the strictest possible substitute as round-1 mistakenly claimed; explicit statistical-implications paragraph added (no Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive; substitution may be less protective than eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`). FC-trace: FC1-N12 (oracle scope swarm coverage) + FC1-N7 (δ provider routing) + Art. V (amendment governance). Trust Root manifest 31 → 33 (run_id.rs + test_llm_proxy.py).
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R13.md:10053:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:5482:    22	- **F6** PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2 wording corrected: `p_0=0.10` is the **least-strict admissible ceiling** (max tolerated value), NOT the "strictest possible substitute". ./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R13.md:10133:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:8142:handover/preregistration/PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md:27:**Wording correction (A8 round-1 audit, Codex finding 1)**: an earlier draft of this section claimed `p_0 = 0.10` was the *strictest possible substitute*. That was backwards. The Gate H test is `j-RR ≤ p_0`, so a SMALLER `p_0` is stricter. `0.10` is the **maximum tolerated value** the PREREG admits — i.e., the LEAST strict admissible ceiling, not the strictest. Substituting it is the equivalent of running Gate H at the weakest threshold the PREREG ever sanctioned. The substitution is *operationally permitted* (the PREREG explicitly allows up to 0.10), but it is NOT a tighter-than-original guarantee.
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R13.md:10134:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:8146:handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:16:- A8e (post-A7, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-1 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini VETO): six dual-audit fixes. **F1** (Codex#2 + Gemini Q4) `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/run_id.rs` mints one stable run_id per run, threaded into both `emit_event` and `make_pput` (replaces `run_corr_id` vs internal `run_id` ms drift; oneshot also stops using `oneshot_{problem_file}` placeholder). **F2** (Gemini VETO Q6) `scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` adds 15-test conformance battery — round-robin distributes [2,2,2] across 6 calls, single-key pool degrades to all-zero, two-key pool alternates [2,2]; exercises the V3L-27 mitigation without a live API. (A8e2 G1 wires this into `cargo test --workspace` via a Rust shell-out wrapper — see § 2 below.) **F3** (Codex#4) routing matrix bug fixed — `Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct` was misrouting to DashScope because `m.startswith("qwen")` won after the slash check; now slash-form ⇒ siliconflow first, bare-qwen ⇒ dashscope second. **F4** (Codex#3) FC1-N12 emit added to all 3 swarm verify sites (`verify_omega_detailed` × 2 + `verify_partial`), each with verdict + elapsed_ms + path stamp; closes the swarm-side gap in the 5-step compile-loop witness. **F5** (Codex#5) `genesis_payload.toml` header + TRACE_MATRIX milestones reconciled to actual 31 entries (round-1 packet undercounted by 1 — agent_models.rs from A3 was already in TR before A1). **F6** (Codex#1) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2` wording corrected — `p_0=0.10` substitution is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling (max tolerated value for `j-RR ≤ p_0`), NOT the strictest possible substitute as round-1 mistakenly claimed; explicit statistical-implications paragraph added (no Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive; substitution may be less protective than eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`). FC-trace: FC1-N12 (oracle scope swarm coverage) + FC1-N7 (δ provider routing) + Art. V (amendment governance). Trust Root manifest 31 → 33 (run_id.rs + test_llm_proxy.py).
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R13.md:10135:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:8149:handover/audits/A8_EXIT_PACKET_2026-04-26.md:22:- **F6** PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2 wording corrected: `p_0=0.10` is the **least-strict admissible ceiling** (max tolerated value), NOT the "strictest possible substitute". ./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R13.md:10143:handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:101:- (RQ2.b) Strict-completeness failure modes call `sys.exit("ERROR: ...")`. ./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R13.md:10197:handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:1482:2. ~~**TRACE_MATRIX of B7-extra (p_0 calibration toggle)**~~ — landed. Final implementation differs slightly from the original sketch in this section: the constitutional `bus.register_predicate(...)` API does not currently exist on `main` (it lives on the unmerged `phase-z-wtool-tools` branch — TRACE_MATRIX_v0 row FC1-N11 references it aspirationally). Rather than scope-creep B7-extra into reviving Phase Z, the synthetic predicate is implemented at the evaluator layer in `rollback_sim.rs` with an explicit short-circuit at the threshold tx. The constitutional anchor (FC1-E18 ∏p=0 → Q_t repeated, then FC2-N22 HALT via existing `MaxTxExhausted`) is unchanged; only the abstraction depth differs. Listed under § 2 above as ✅ entries.

./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R13.md:10342:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R8.md:256:- **F6** PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2 reworded: `p_0=0.10` → least-strict admissible ceiling; explicit Type-I implications paragraph added. Closes #5.
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R13.md:10345:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R8.md:459:PREREG § 5.5 specifies p_0 calibration via 576 paired runs (144 adaptation × 2 seeds × {control, treatment}) with estimated cost "~8 wall-hours, ~$3-5". Empirical observation 2026-04-25 during launched batch (commit 650caf7+ era):
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R13.md:10348:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R8.md:475:**Wording correction (A8 round-1 audit, Codex finding 1)**: an earlier draft of this section claimed `p_0 = 0.10` was the *strictest possible substitute*. That was backwards. The Gate H test is `j-RR ≤ p_0`, so a SMALLER `p_0` is stricter. `0.10` is the **maximum tolerated value** the PREREG admits — i.e., the LEAST strict admissible ceiling, not the strictest. Substituting it is the equivalent of running Gate H at the weakest threshold the PREREG ever sanctioned. The substitution is *operationally permitted* (the PREREG explicitly allows up to 0.10), but it is NOT a tighter-than-original guarantee.
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R13.md:10350:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R8.md:479:- The substitution may be LESS protective than an eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`. If/when calibration runs (per § 3 conditions, which are PRE-REQUISITES — they do not by themselves *guarantee* calibration completes before any specific phase), the empirical estimate may tighten the bar; until then Gate H operates at the ceiling.
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R13.md:10355:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R8.md:504:PREREG_PPUT_CCL_2026-04-26 § 5.5 originally listed p_0 calibration as a Phase B prerequisite ("Schedule: Phase B7 mandatory; not deferrable to Phase D"). This amendment **explicitly OVERRIDES that "not deferrable" clause** for the deferral conditions in § 3 above.

(A8e2 G1 wires this into `cargo test --workspace` via a Rust shell-out wrapper — see § 2 below.) **F3** (Codex#4) routing matrix bug fixed — `Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct` was misrouting to DashScope because `m.startswith("qwen")` won after the slash check; now slash-form ⇒ siliconflow first, bare-qwen ⇒ dashscope second. **F4** (Codex#3) FC1-N12 emit added to all 3 swarm verify sites (`verify_omega_detailed` × 2 + `verify_partial`), each with verdict + elapsed_ms + path stamp; closes the swarm-side gap in the 5-step compile-loop witness. **F5** (Codexe for `j-RR ≤ p_0`), NOT the strictest possible substitute as round-1 mistakenly claimed; explicit statistical-implications paragraph added (no Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive; substitution may be less protective than eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`). FC-trace: FC1-N12 (oracle scope swarm coverage) + FC1-N7 (δ provider routing) + Art. V (amendment governance). Trust Root manifest 31 → 33 (run_id.rs + test_llm_proxy.py).
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:10007:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:10317:handover/preregistration/PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md:27:**Wording correction (A8 round-1 audit, Codex finding 1)**: an earlier draft of this section claimed `p_0 = 0.10` was the *strictest possible substitute*. That was backwards. The Gate H test is `j-RR ≤ p_0`, so a SMALLER `p_0` is stricter. `0.10` is the **maximum tolerated value** the PREREG admits — i.e., the LEAST strict admissible ceiling, not the strictest. Substituting it is the equivalent of running Gate H at the weakest threshold the PREREG ever sanctioned. The substitution is *operationally permitted* (the PREREG explicitly allows up to 0.10), but it is NOT a tighter-than-original guarantee.
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:10008:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:10322:handover/audits/A8_EXIT_PACKET_2026-04-26.md:42:- Codex R2: **CHALLENGE / high** — 3 findings (F2 not recurring; PREREG_AMENDMENT § 8 still says "strictest plausible bar" contradicting § 2; A8 packet + TRACE_MATRIX stale on counts + risk #5 + run_corr_id symbol row + "in CI" claim).
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:10013:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:10332:handover/audits/A8_EXIT_PACKET_2026-04-26.md:179:- Substitutes `p_0 = 0.10` (PREREG § 5.5 ceiling) for the calibration-derived value at every Gate H consumer. The substitution is operationally permitted (the PREREG explicitly admits up to 0.10 as the ceiling) but is the **least-strict admissible value** since `j-RR ≤ p_0` makes a SMALLER `p_0` stricter — see PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2 wording correction (round-1 audit Codex#1, A8e fix F6) for full statistical implications. No Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive (PREREG § 5.4), outside the inferential family. May be less protective than an eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`; acceptable because Gate H is Phase E and § 3 conditions ensure calibration runs first. Re-calibration conditions in § 3 list 5 items (N-experiments arc complete / swarm_N=1 mode landed / per-agent budget normalization landed / hetero-LLM exp complete / Phase D ArchitectAI runtime exists).

Re-hashed in Trust Root. **G3** (Codex R2#3 + Gemini R2#2) `A8_EXIT_PACKET` updated for round-2 reality: § 2 cumulative-tests table extended with A8e/A8e2 columns, § 4 manifest-clean assertion bumped 30→34, § 5 round-1 risk #5 (no FC1-N12 in swarm verify path) removed (closed by F4), § 6 Q7.a/b numbers updated, round-2 outcome + round-3 fixes section appended; TRACE_MATRIX stale `run_corr_id` symbol row replaced; "in CI" softened to "via Rust wrapper post-A8e2". FC-trace: meta governance + V3L-27 mitigation gating (FC1-N7). Trust Root manifest 33 → 34 (`tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs`).

./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:10021:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:10452:    27	**Wording correction (A8 round-1 audit, Codex finding 1)**: an earlier draft of this section claimed `p_0 = 0.10` was the *strictest possible substitute*. That was backwards. The Gate H test is `j-RR ≤ p_0`, so a SMALLER `p_0` is stricter. `0.10` is the **maximum tolerated value** the PREREG admits — i.e., the LEAST strict admissible ceiling, not the strictest. Substituting it is the equivalent of running Gate H at the weakest threshold the PREREG ever sanctioned. The substitution is *operationally permitted* (the PREREG explicitly allows up to 0.10), but it is NOT a tighter-than-original guarantee.

Six anchor sites wired in `run_swarm`: FC2-N22 synthetic short-circuit, FC2-N20 mr tick, FC2-N22 OMEGA full-proof, FC2-N22 OMEGA per-tactic, FC2-N22 natural MaxTxExhausted (with `budget_regime` payload), FC1-N12 verify bracket (oneshot). End-to-end smoke test exercises FC_TRACE=1 in a child process (subprocess required because `OnceLock` caches the gate-read; resolves item 7 of TRACE_MATRIX § 5 "Per-line FC tagging via tracing crate"). FC-trace: meta-witness for the 5-step compile loop (Proposal → Lean ground truth → Logging → Capability compilation → ↑H-VPPUT). Trust Root manifest 26 → 27.

./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:10326:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26.md:9865:handover/audits/GEMINI_PHASE_A0_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:52:    **CHALLENGE**. The battery is inconsistent. While some tests like `fc1_n4_tape_constructible_with_time_arrow` are strong witnesses that call methods, others like `fc1_n1_q_state_carrier_present` use `std::any::type_name::<T>()`. This only proves the type *exists*, not that its constitutionally relevant API is intact. This is a weak form of witnessing that could allow for silent drift if, for example, a key method on `TuringBus` were removed but the type itself remained. The weaker witnesses should be strengthened to call the specific functions or methods cited as evidence in `TRACE_MATRIX_v2`.
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:10334:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26.md:10201:   135	axiom: "Every pub fn / struct / enum / trait / const in src/ must carry /// TRACE_MATRIX FCx-Nx: <role> doc-comment OR be filed as orphan with explicit cases/Cxxx or PREREG-§n.m justification in TRACE_MATRIX_v?.md"
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:10335:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26.md:10208:   142	message: "REMINDER (R-015 / Alignment Standard): edit touches a pub symbol in a Rust file. New OR modified pub items MUST carry `/// TRACE_MATRIX FC?-N?: <role>` doc-comment AND have an entry in handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v?.md (current: v1_2026-04-25). If this is genuinely orphan, file under TRACE_MATRIX § 3 with explicit Constitutional Justification (cases/Cxxx or PREREG-§n.m). Untraced pub symbols cause silent constitutional drift."

./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:10634:handover/audits/CODEX_PPUT_CCL_AUDIT_ROUND4_2026-04-26.md:4830:- **§ 9.1 (Family Timing):** The introduction of a pre-committed `k_max = 10` to define a frozen `N_max = 44` for all multiplicity corrections is the standard, correct way to handle a data-dependent family size in a pre-registration. ./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:10774:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:501:**Wording correction (A8 round-1 audit, Codex finding 1)**: an earlier draft of this section claimed `p_0 = 0.10` was the *strictest possible substitute*. That was backwards. The Gate H test is `j-RR ≤ p_0`, so a SMALLER `p_0` is stricter. `0.10` is the **maximum tolerated value** the PREREG admits — i.e., the LEAST strict admissible ceiling, not the strictest. Substituting it is the equivalent of running Gate H at the weakest threshold the PREREG ever sanctioned. The substitution is *operationally permitted* (the PREREG explicitly allows up to 0.10), but it is NOT a tighter-than-original guarantee.
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:10775:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:505:- The substitution may be LESS protective than an eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`. If/when calibration runs (per § 3 conditions, which are PRE-REQUISITES — they do not by themselves *guarantee* calibration completes before any specific phase), the empirical estimate may tighten the bar; until then Gate H operates at the ceiling.
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:10778:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:569:- Does substitution of `p_0 = 0.10` invalidate any Gate H statistical claim? (Should not — `j-RR` is descriptive (PREREG § 5.4), outside the inferential family, so no Type-I inflation. Per § 2 wording correction: 0.10 is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling, NOT a tighter-than-original substitute — the substitution may be less protective than an eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`, but it is **operationally permitted at any phase including Phase E** by the original PREREG § 5.5 ceiling. § 3 conditions are PRE-REQUISITES for calibration to run at all; they do NOT guarantee calibration completes before any specific phase.)

./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:10927:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:5431:- A8e8 (post-A8e7, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-7 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini CHALLENGE — both convergent on rewrite-implementation gaps): five fixes including one substantive PREREG logic correction. **M1** packet § 2 + § 4 Trust Root count bumped 34 → 35 (post-A8e7 added `A8_AUDIT_HISTORY` to manifest). **M2** this history doc's A8e7 entry stamped with actual SHA `aaedc9d` + round-7 verdicts + A8e8 fix list (round-7 sealed). **M3** packet rewritten to strip ALL historical lineage text — no "(post-A8e F4)", "(added by A8eN)", "(chain position N via A8e)", "(+ A8eN fix XN)". Packet describes WHAT IS, no HOW; lineage lives only in this history doc. **M4 (substantive Codex R7#4)** PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2(b) corrected: removed false claim that "§ 3 conditions ensure calibration runs *before* Phase E"; § 3 conditions are PRE-REQUISITES for calibration TO RUN at all, not guarantees of completion before any specific phase. § 8 reads as the operative rule: substitution permitted at any phase including Phase E; calibration UPGRADES the bar IF and WHEN § 3 conditions complete. Single semantics, internally consistent across § 2 / § 3 / § 8. Re-hashed in Trust Root. **M5** audit runner scripts now append `experiments/minif2f_v4/tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs` so Q1.c (verify wrapper fail-closed) is verifiable from the packet bundle alone. Trust Root manifest unchanged at 35 (only re-hashes; no new files).
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:10928:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:5433:- A8e6 (post-A8e5, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-6 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini CHALLENGE — both narrow): one real correctness fix + 4 doc fixes. **K1** packet `<pending>` placeholder cleanup (J2 + RQ14 wording) — same precedent as A8e5 J2 but applied to round-6 self-references. **K2 (real bug, Codex R6#2)** `src/drivers/llm_proxy.py` `detect_provider`: HuggingFace-style `deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1-Distill-Qwen-7B` was misrouting to `api.deepseek.com` because `"deepseek" in m` won before the slash check; pre-K2 logic would 404 on every Distill variant since the official DeepSeek API doesn't serve them. K2 swaps the order — slash-form is now the FIRST routing heuristic (after explicit prefix), and bare-deepseek substring only fires when there's no slash. New `test_deepseek_slash_form_routes_to_siliconflow` in `scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` pins this (covers `deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1-Distill-Qwen-7B` + `deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-V2.5` AS WELL AS the bare-form sanity case `deepseek-chat` → deepseek). **K3** TRACE_MATRIX A8e3 row stale "21 positional args" → 24 (matches packet update from A8e5 J5; Codex R6#3 caught the inconsistency between TRACE_MATRIX and the packet). **K4** TRACE_MATRIX A6 row top-line "Six anchor sites" → "9 anchor sites" with A6-original-6 + F4-added-3 breakdown (Gemini R6#1 caught the contradiction between the line's first sentence and its own follow-up). **K5** packet § 3 A5/A6 fix attribution: "A8e3 fix H4 corrected" → "A8e4 fix I2 corrected" (Gemini R6#2 caught the cross-reference error — H4 corrected the genesis_payload.toml TR-header comment, I2 corrected the packet § 3 numbers). Trust Root manifest unchanged at 34 (re-hashes of `llm_proxy.py` + `scripts/test_llm_proxy.py`; no new files).
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:10931:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:5436:- A8e3 (post-A8e2, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-3 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini CHALLENGE — both convergent on packet staleness; no VETO): six narrow cleanup fixes. **H1** A8 packet § 3 A1 description: "Mathematically conservative" → "least-strict admissible value" with explicit Type-I implications. **H2** packet § 3 A6 description: 6 → 9 anchor sites (added F4 swarm sites explicitly). **H3** packet § 6 Q2.a / Q4.a / Q4.d marked CLOSED with closure rationale. **H4** `genesis_payload.toml` TR header phrasing about A1: "conservative ceiling" → "max-tolerated ceiling". **H5** § 5 item 7 here in TRACE_MATRIX: "(commit pending) + 6 wired" → "CLOSED + 9 wired". **H6** G1 wrapper test fail-closed on missing `python3` (was soft skip; Codex R3 caught the silent-pass risk); explicit opt-out `SKIP_LLM_PROXY_PYTHON_CONFORMANCE=1` for deliberate downgrades (logged loudly). Trust Root manifest unchanged at 34 (only re-hashes; no new files). Non-blocking observation deferred to Phase B+: `make_pput` signature was 21 positional args at round-3; A8e fix F1 added the `run_id` parameter, bringing the post-A8e count to **24** (Gemini R3 Finding 2 + Gemini R5/Codex R6 spot-checks; A8e6 K3 corrected here) — refactor to a builder pattern is recommended but not gating.
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:10932:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:5437:- A8e2 (post-A8e, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-2 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini CHALLENGE — both convergent): three dual-audit fixes. **G1** (Codex R2#1 + Gemini R2#1) `experiments/minif2f_v4/tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs` wraps `python3 scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` in a Rust integration test, making the V3L-27 round-robin conformance gate run on every `cargo test --workspace`. **G2** (Codex R2#2) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 8` audit-requirements paragraph reworded — round-1 fix corrected § 2 but left § 8 still calling the substitution a "strictest plausible bar"; now consistent across the doc. Re-hashed in Trust Root. **G3** (Codex R2#3 + Gemini R2#2) `A8_EXIT_PACKET` updated for round-2 reality: § 2 cumulative-tests table extended with A8e/A8e2 columns, § 4 manifest-clean assertion bumped 30→34, § 5 round-1 risk #5 (no FC1-N12 in swarm verify path) removed (closed by F4), § 6 Q7.a/b numbers updated, round-2 outcome + round-3 fixes section appended; TRACE_MATRIX stale `run_corr_id` symbol row replaced; "in CI" softened to "via Rust wrapper post-A8e2". FC-trace: meta governance + V3L-27 mitigation gating (FC1-N7). Trust Root manifest 33 → 34 (`tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs`).
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:10933:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:5438:- A8e (post-A7, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-1 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini VETO): six dual-audit fixes. **F1** (Codex#2 + Gemini Q4) `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/run_id.rs` mints one stable run_id per run, threaded into both `emit_event` and `make_pput` (replaces `run_corr_id` vs internal `run_id` ms drift; oneshot also stops using `oneshot_{problem_file}` placeholder). **F2** (Gemini VETO Q6) `scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` adds 15-test conformance battery — round-robin distributes [2,2,2] across 6 calls, single-key pool degrades to all-zero, two-key pool alternates [2,2]; exercises the V3L-27 mitigation without a live API. (A8e2 G1 wires this into `cargo test --workspace` via a Rust shell-out wrapper — see § 2 below.) **F3** (Codex#4) routing matrix bug fixed — `Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct` was misrouting to DashScope because `m.startswith("qwen")` won after the slash check; now slash-form ⇒ siliconflow first, bare-qwen ⇒ dashscope second. **F4** (Codex#3) FC1-N12 emit added to all 3 swarm verify sites (`verify_omega_detailed` × 2 + `verify_partial`), each with verdict + elapsed_ms + path stamp; closes the swarm-side gap in the 5-step compile-loop witness. **F5** (Codex#5) `genesis_payload.toml` header + TRACE_MATRIX milestones reconciled to actual 31 entries (round-1 packet undercounted by 1 — agent_models.rs from A3 was already in TR before A1). **F6** (Codex#1) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2` wording corrected — `p_0=0.10` substitution is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling (max tolerated value for `j-RR ≤ p_0`), NOT the strictest possible substitute as round-1 mistakenly claimed; explicit statistical-implications paragraph added (no Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive; substitution may be less protective than eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`). FC-trace: FC1-N12 (oracle scope swarm coverage) + FC1-N7 (δ provider routing) + Art. V (amendment governance). Trust Root manifest 31 → 33 (run_id.rs + test_llm_proxy.py).
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:10935:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:5487:| `tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs` Rust→Python wrapper (A8e2 fix G1) | `experiments/minif2f_v4/tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs` | A8 round-2 audit (Codex R2#1 + Gemini R2#1): the round-robin conformance battery in `scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` was only manual; tests that don't run automatically are documentation, not gates. G1 wires the Python suite into `cargo test --workspace` via a Rust integration test that shells to `python3` and asserts exit 0 + the unittest "OK" trailer. The V3L-27 mitigation (case C-027) is now a recurring conformance gate exercised on every commit that touches Rust + every CI pipeline that already exercises Rust tests. | ✅ |
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:10949:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:5922:   153	- (Q4.a) Spot-check: re-run `cargo test --workspace`; expect 265 PASS / 29 ignored / 0 failed.

(Codex R2#1 + Gemini R2#1) F2's `test_llm_proxy.py` exists but is documented as manual-only; no CI integration. Tests-not-running-automatically = documentation, not gates. Closes Gemini's round-1 VETO at the *artifact* level but NOT at the *process* level.
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:10958:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:6002:    58	3. (Codex R2#3 + Gemini R2#2) Packet § 5 Risk #5 ("No FC1-N12 emit in run_swarm verify path") is stale (closed by F4); packet § 6 Q7.a/b numbers stale (261/30 vs actual 264/33); TRACE_MATRIX has stale `run_corr_id` symbol row + "in CI" claim.
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:10983:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:6222:    27	**Wording correction (A8 round-1 audit, Codex finding 1)**: an earlier draft of this section claimed `p_0 = 0.10` was the *strictest possible substitute*. That was backwards. The Gate H test is `j-RR ≤ p_0`, so a SMALLER `p_0` is stricter. `0.10` is the **maximum tolerated value** the PREREG admits — i.e., the LEAST strict admissible ceiling, not the strictest. Substituting it is the equivalent of running Gate H at the weakest threshold the PREREG ever sanctioned. The substitution is *operationally permitted* (the PREREG explicitly allows up to 0.10), but it is NOT a tighter-than-original guarantee.
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:10984:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:6226:    31	- The substitution may be LESS protective than an eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`. If/when calibration runs (per § 3 conditions, which are PRE-REQUISITES — they do not by themselves *guarantee* calibration completes before any specific phase), the empirical estimate may tighten the bar; until then Gate H operates at the ceiling.
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:10987:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:6290:    95	- Does substitution of `p_0 = 0.10` invalidate any Gate H statistical claim? (Should not — `j-RR` is descriptive (PREREG § 5.4), outside the inferential family, so no Type-I inflation. Per § 2 wording correction: 0.10 is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling, NOT a tighter-than-original substitute — the substitution may be less protective than an eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`, but it is **operationally permitted at any phase including Phase E** by the original PREREG § 5.5 ceiling. § 3 conditions are PRE-REQUISITES for calibration to run at all; they do NOT guarantee calibration completes before any specific phase.)
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:11026:handover/audits/GEMINI_PHASE_A0_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:52:    **CHALLENGE**. The battery is inconsistent. While some tests like `fc1_n4_tape_constructible_with_time_arrow` are strong witnesses that call methods, others like `fc1_n1_q_state_carrier_present` use `std::any::type_name::<T>()`. This only proves the type *exists*, not that its constitutionally relevant API is intact. This is a weak form of witnessing that could allow for silent drift if, for example, a key method on `TuringBus` were removed but the type itself remained. The weaker witnesses should be strengthened to call the specific functions or methods cited as evidence in `TRACE_MATRIX_v2`.
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:11028:handover/audits/run_gemini_pput_ccl_audit.py:93:    "\n\n---\n\nNow give your INDEPENDENT round-1 audit of the PREREG. Be specific: cite paragraph/§ for every CHALLENGE finding. Do not speculate beyond the document."

./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:11045:handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:1283:PREREG § 5.5 conceives of `--simulate-rollback-at-tx-50` as "synthetic mid-run rollback mimicking worst-case stochastic edge case Phase E artifacts could trigger". A worst-case Phase E artifact would corrupt state but the loop would continue spending budget. We short-circuit instead — saving wall-clock but understating cost.

./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:11095:handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:3703:./experiments/minif2f_v4/src/bin/evaluator.rs:155:    synthetic_short_circuit: Option<bool>,
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:11097:handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:3781:./handover/audits/GEMINI_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:23:-   **(Q1.c) Serialization of `synthetic_short_circuit`**: **PASS**. The implementation is correct. The field `synthetic_short_circuit` in `PputResult` (`evaluator.rs:142`) is an `Option<bool>` with `#[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]`. The default construction path in `make_pput` (`evaluator.rs:1252`) sets it to `None`, ensuring it is omitted for control runs. The treatment short-circuit path (`evaluator.rs:517`) explicitly sets it to `Some(true)`. This matches the smoke test verification.
